AI SOCIETY – Bennett University’s hub for research, collaboration, and innovation in Artificial Intelligence.

Training Minds, One Epoch at a Time.

Technical Departments  
- Natural Language Processing  
- Reinforcement Learning  
- Computer Vision  
- Generative AI  
- Research  

Non-Technical Departments  
- Management Team  
- Outreach Team  
- Creative Team  
- Multimedia Team  

Social Media & Mentors  
Mentors: 3rd / 4th-year students plus faculty members.

What is AI Society?  
The AI Specialization Club (AIS) at Bennett University cultivates passion for Artificial Intelligence through end-to-end project development, hackathons, workshops, and upskilling guided by senior mentors and experienced faculty. We actively contribute to open-source development.

Mission  
To empower students with practical AI knowledge, collaborative opportunities, and innovative thinking to shape impactful solutions for the future.

Highlights  
0+ active researchers and developers  
Research Excellence – cutting-edge AI research with published papers and innovative solutions  
Innovation Hub – fostering creativity and breakthrough ideas  
Skill Development – comprehensive training programs and mentorship  
Industry Impact – real-world applications and collaborations  

Meet Our Dream Team – passionate individuals driving innovation and excellence.

Objective:
To promote the art of programming among the student community.
To conduct programming competitions of good standards and motivate passion for programming.
To encourage and guide students to participate in national and international programming competitions and win prizes.

Introduction to Competitive Coding // Code Wars Orientation Report

Introduction to Competitive Coding – Code Wars Orientation Report  
NLH 101, 6:30 PM – 9:00 PM  
27 Aug 2019  

Core team and mentors present.  

Orientation (6:30 PM – 7:00 PM)  
- What is Competitive Coding (C.C.)  
- What is CodeChef and HackerEarth  
- How C.C. benefits first-year students  
- Common practices, tips & tricks  
- One introductory easy-medium question  
- General advice on attempting contest questions  
Participants: 50 first-year students  

Code Wars Lite – August Edition (7:00 PM – 9:00 PM)  
- 5 programming questions + 3 MCQs  
- Participants: 44  
Core team answered queries; many scored 50–70/100.  
Selection process for junior chapter membership explained.  
Two Google Forms collected feedback for Orientation and Code Wars Lite.  
Next Code Wars announced for early September.

CODE WARS September Event Report

ALH 301  6:30 PM – 9:00 PM  03 SEP ’19  
All Members of Core Present

The Code Wars (September Edition) was successfully conducted in ALH-301.  
Stats:  
- Participants: 25  
- Questions given: 5 (Programming)  

Core team members addressed participant queries throughout the event.  
Most attendees scored between 40 and 80 out of 100.  

One Google Form was submitted regarding CodeWars.  
Contest link (ENDED): http://hck.re/b196  
Form link: https://tinyurl.com/bucodewarfb

Global Program

Bachelor of Design (B. Des) Global - Fashion Design (2+2)  

Doctoral Program
Ph.D.  

The Photography Club is a platform built for photography Beginners, Amateurs, Enthusiasts and Hobbyists, on an idea to connect every photography enthusiast by a common thread. We are growing on a single set of mind where everyone is welcome to join us, and stimulate the inception of new ideas in this visual art. We are here in order to inculcate and motivate the art of photography in our students.

We are a place where people are not judged by their skills, rather they have to come up with enthusiasm, and rest we help them in providing the right expertise. We are there to give the support to one other and learn from the unlearned skills from our team as well as build on each other's artistry. We seek to display our love of photography through the constructive criticism of our peers, leisure activities, and the promotion of photography throughout the campus and carve a niche for ourselves as a club on different platforms.

We are an inclusive club, where we also promote creative videography. We welcome all sorts of individuals to come and join us over our monthly sessions, discuss their aspirations and creativity, find the like-minded people and transform them through various club activities.

List of Office Bearers of ISAAC Club

SPARK is a student club that works closely with the Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (CIE) and the Bennett Hatchory to nurture creativity and an entrepreneurial mindset. Its objectives are to encourage creative thinking, support future startup founders, and facilitate interaction with entrepreneurs, industry experts, and venture capitalists.

VERVE
“Verve” refers to a lively, energetic, and spirited quality or style of movement. It’s important to note that verve is not limited to a specific dance style but can be applied to various forms of dance, from ballet to hip-hop to contemporary. It’s about the dancer’s ability to inject their performance with vitality and exuberance, creating a memorable and impactful experience for both themselves and the audience.

Purpose

Purpose  
The club embraces a variety of dance forms rooted in Western culture, creating a lively and inclusive community. To enhance skills, it occasionally brings in experienced instructors or organizes workshops.

Major Activities  
Celebrating Western dance traditions, the club honours and embraces the various dance styles deeply rooted in Western culture. Beyond the dance floor, the club serves as a stress-relief haven. The sheer joy of dancing and the supportive atmosphere provide a welcome escape from the demands of daily life.

Activities planned for the Tenure  
To give an overview of the activities that Verve has planned for the tenure:

- Collaborations with different clubs within the university such as Verve×Panache, Verve×Ansh, etc.  
- Fresher’s Interaction  
- Convergence, a 3-day flagship event of the club  
- Flashmobs  
- Guest workshops  
- Inter-college fests/competitions like Mood Indigo IIT Bombay, Oasis BITS Pilani, to name a few

Integrated M. Tech + Ph.D.  
PGD TV & Digital Journalism  
PGD Computer Science Engineering  
PGD Computer Applications

  Global Program

ANSH – the dramatics club of Bennett University – brings stories to life through theatrical performances. Made up of passionate drama-lovers, it is the university’s hub of artistic expression: creativity flourishes, friendships form, and the magic of the stage leaves a lasting impression on members and audiences alike.

Purpose  
Ansh is Bennett University’s dramatics club—an inclusive, creative platform for acting and filmmaking enthusiasts. It nurtures creativity and cultural vibrancy by helping members master acting, directing, public speaking, improvisation, script-writing, costume design, set construction and technical production while boosting confidence and spreading an artsy vibe.

Major Activities (current tenure)  
- Stage plays and full-scale theatrical productions  
- Nukkad Natak (street plays)  
- National-level showcase goal

Planned Activities  
- Bollywood-style spectacle ‘Jashn-e Cinema’  
- Short-film projects and filmmaking emphasis  
- Acting workshops with Barry John Acting Studio  
- Study tour to the National School of Drama plus other theatre excursions  
- Off-campus outreach to raise Ansh’s profile beyond Bennett University

Club Canvass  
Ansh = theatre, nukkad natak, skits, short films.  
Aim: deliver the finest traditional theatre experience, inspired by the Natya Shastra.

Main aim is to provide the best kind of traditional theatre experience taking inspiration from our own Natya Shastra.

The club not only prepares our paltan for acting but also to act and direct plays based on the scripts written by the aspiring writers of Ansh.

Instagram Handle: @ansh.bu

Events celebrated by Ansh club in 2022

SL NO | Name of the event | Event rendition  
1 | Zangoora – The Gypsy Prince (Musical Play) | Conceived by Ansh in collaboration with Rivaaz, Verve and Advaita.  
2 | Visage (English Department workshop) | Acting-style & scene-breakdown workshop by the English department of Ansh; American Psycho was the subject.  
3 | Izhaar-e-Tamasha (Hindi Department workshop) | Acting-style & scene-breakdown workshop by the Hindi department of Ansh; Tamasha was the subject.  
4 | Akrosh (Nukkad Natak competition) | Inter-college street-play competition conducted by Ansh during Yuphoria X Zenevia.  
5 | Abhinaya (Mono-acting competition) | Inter-college solo-acting competition conducted by Ansh during Yuphoria X Zenevia.  
6 | Horror short films & monologues on Halloween | Core team of Ansh created a horror short film and performed monologues.  
7 | Nukkad Natak on Women’s Health | Performed in collaboration with Girls Health Club during Sustainability Week.

RIVAAZ

Nritya or Dance is where one surrenders to music and grace to express themselves. Rivaaz, the traditional dance club of Bennett University, is dedicated to just that. Rivaaz is the place for dance enthusiasts who are looking to explore various dance forms right from Garba to Bhangra, from Kathak to Bharatnatyam. At Rivaaz they cover all classical and folk-dance forms.

Purpose  
Rivaaz is dedicated to preserving and promoting Indian dance forms. The club provides a platform for diverse cultural activities and highlights dance and creative talent while spreading awareness and appreciation of Indian culture.

Major Activities  
- Hosts performances and events throughout the year  
- Integrates Indian values and culture into campus life  
- Events include: Garba Night, Lohri, Bollywood Night, Inter-college Competition at Symbiosis International, Noida  
- Reached the final round of Nach Baliye, a duet dance competition at Symbiosis International, Noida  

Activities Planned for the Tenure  
- Garba Night  
- Halloween  
- Lohri  
- Club Canvass  

Collaborative Event  
A joint event with Ansh, Panache, and Verve clubs scheduled for February.  

Bollywood Night  
Will promote inter-club collaboration and showcase unity among Bennett clubs.  

Past Success  
Garba Night 3.0 was successfully held and well-received by the Bennett fraternity.  

Instagram Handle  
@rivaaz_bu  

Events Celebrated by Rivaaz in 2022  
1. Garba Night 3.0 – Navratri celebration  
2. Chandrakala – solo dance competition during Uphoria x Zenevia  
3. Nrityakala – group dance competition during Uphoria x Zenevia  
4. Zangoora – dance training for the musical team  
5. BLUEMOON 7.0 – Bhangra performance at freshers  

Club Heads  
1. Vidhi Goswami – Secretary  
2. Rohitashya Chaudhuri – Joint Secretary

SL NO Name Position  
1 Vidhi Goswami Secretary  
2 Rohitashya Chaudhuri Joint Secretary  

Plot Nos 8-11, TechZone II, Greater Noida 201310, UP. India  
admissions@bennett.edu.in | info@bennett.edu.in

BU GAMers
Bennett University campus platform where all gamers unite. Join for esports, gaming events, and a thriving gaming community. Level up your college experience with us!

BU Gamers at Bennett University campus is a platform where all gamers unite! Join us for esports, gaming events, and a thriving gaming community. Level up your college experience with us!

Purpose  
BU Gamers serves as a platform for students to come together, fostering a sense of community and belonging among gamers. It encourages diversity and inclusivity, offering opportunities for members to explore new games, engage in friendly competition and develop valuable friendships while pursuing their passion for gaming within an educational setting.

Major Activities  
BU Gamers Gaming Club offers an inclusive community for diverse gaming enthusiasts. We host regular gaming events, skill-building workshops, and social gatherings. We dive into the exhilarating world of e-sports, community outreach, and networking opportunities. BU Gamers promotes responsible gaming and aims to foster personal growth and connections within the gaming culture.

Activities planned for the Tenure  
Brace yourself for some exciting upcoming gaming tournaments comprising of an all-girls Inter-College Valorant event, Online Gaming Competitions, a Couples Tournament for Valentine’s Day, and Christmas Special Tournaments in December. Stay connected with the club for details on games, rules, and prizes, and join us in celebrating the gaming community’s diversity and camaraderie.

Club Canvass  
The club is by gamers, for gamers. Come interact with the gaming community at Bennett and show off your crazy skills at the club tournaments, join in the live streams or get your amazing clips shared with the community. There’s always something great going on and you don’t want to miss it! Whether you are a professional gamer or someone getting into gaming, BU Gamers welcomes all players and all platforms, be it console, PC, or cellular.

Instagram Handle: @bu.gamers

Instagram Handle: @bu.gamers

Events celebrated by BU GAMERS in 2022

SL NO | Name of the event | Event rendition
1 | FIFA | A FIFA 1v1 held within the college.

Silhouette, the art club at Bennett University, stands as a radiant beacon of creativity on campus. Silhouette invites students to embark on a journey of self-discovery by exploring diverse art forms, fostering an environment where brushes, colours, and imagination unite.

Purpose  
Silhouette’s main aim is to nurture creativity and promote art therapy, anticipating the modern generation’s need for mental well-being. Silhouette will be a haven where students explore art’s therapeutic potential, fostering holistic growth in the community.

Major Activities  
Silhouette, the burgeoning art club at Bennett University, is rising to become a vibrant hub of creative expression and personal growth. Members are encouraged to explore various art forms—from traditional painting and sculpture to modern digital art and photography—fostering an environment of artistic exploration. The club’s leaders plan to enrich this journey by organizing workshops covering a wide spectrum of art forms at various events.

Activities Planned for the Tenure  
The agenda includes diverse workshops that ignite creativity and broaden artistic horizons:  
- Functional art: painting tote bags and transforming everyday items into personalized works of art  
- Digital art, photography, and art-therapy sessions for a well-rounded artistic experience  

Club Canvass  
Silhouette is Bennett University’s art club. It promotes creativity and imagination, providing activities for students of all interest and skill levels. The club offers opportunities and encouragement to express oneself freely, leads beautiful art projects on campus, and showcases BU’s brilliant artists. From mandala, sketching, and rangoli to quilling, digital art, and doodling, Silhouette supports every artistic pursuit.

Events celebrated by Silhouette Club in 2022

Silhouette Club supports every artistic pursuit—from mandala, sketching and rangoli to quilling, digital art and doodling.

Events celebrated by Silhouette Club in 2022

1. Garba Night – Handled all decorations.  
5. Guru Nanak Jayanti – Decorated the venue and assisted other clubs with their preparations.  
6. 2 Bluemoon 7.0 – Managed decorations.  
3. Mini Mela – During the Uphoria Zenevia fest, organised hand-drawn Polaroid painting, spiral-dip tie-dye, doodle wall, photobooth and face-painting.  
4. Halloween – Set up decorations and ran face-painting & photobooth (in collaboration with ISAC).  
5. Blood Donation Drive – Designed banners/posters and handled decoration.

Club Head

1. Vaibhavi Sharma – Secretary  
2. Kavya Rai – Joint Secretary

ASTRONOMY

The Astronomy Club at Bennett University comprises a flourishing community of astronomy and science enthusiasts who collectively explore and embrace the celestial culture in and around the National Capital Region. The club organizes workshops, quizzes, movie nights, field trips and other activities to engage students and help them broaden their knowledge about the wonders of space.

Purpose

Purpose  
The Astronomy Club supports both academic growth and social interaction. Members learn through fun, interactive events and expand their networks via campus discussions about the latest astronomical discoveries, advances, and news.

Major Activities (2022-23)  
- Live screening of Chandrayaan-3 soft landing (200+ students attended)  
- ‘Among Us’ Tournament  
- Space Moves dance competition  
- Cosmos and Chaos 4-phased astronomy-themed treasure hunt  
- Astrophotography workshop  
- Planetarium trips  

Planned Activities for the Current Tenure  
- Observatory trip  
- Short film / Play  
- Movie nights  
- Cosmos and Chaos  
- Astrophotography 2.0  
- Nakshatra talks  
- Star gazing party  
- Workshop trip  
- Quiz & Writing Competition  
- StarLab Workshop  
- Live screening of astro events  
- Club Canvass  

About the Club  
The Astronomy Club of Bennett University explores the universe, hosts expert talks, collaborative events, and competitions with cash prizes, making astronomy engaging and accessible.

Instagram Handle  
@astronomy_bu

2022 Events Celebrated  
1. Cosmos and Chaos – 4-phased astronomy-themed treasure hunt

Global Program

Pulse is the event-management club of Bennett University. It assists the student council in running events and aims to give students memorable experiences. The club organizes game nights and other interactive activities that make campus life more eventful.

Purpose  
College life is supposed to be marvellously memorable, and special occasions go a long way in making it so. That’s what the club does—plan rigorously and execute vivaciously—so events hold the potential to be etched in students’ hearts.

Major Activities  
The club’s scope is vast and impactful: empowering individuals with the knowledge and connections necessary for effective leadership and successful management roles in the event industry. Through opportunities to engage in real-world projects, they foster experiential learning, ensuring members are well-prepared for the challenges of the professional world. Beyond skill development, they aim to create a strong sense of community and camaraderie among like-minded individuals, enriching both personal life and professional development.

Activities planned for the tenure  
- Meet-Greet  
- Squid Game  
- TEDx Talk  
- Amaze-Mas with Secret Santa  
- Children’s Day picnic  
- Club Canvass  

The club plans rigorously and executes vivaciously, so events hold the potential to be etched in students’ hearts. Pulse is always looking to spice up everyone’s college lives at Bennett University.

Events celebrated by Pulse Club in 2022  
1. Bennett Royale (Mini Mela) – A small-scale casino simulation event involving multiple games over three days and two stalls during Uphoria.  
2. Wrap Up Like A Mummy Race (Halloween) – The game was played in teams of two; one person wrapped the other using a toilet-paper roll, and the time taken to complete this activity decided the winning team.

Club Head  
1. Arjit Kumar Singh – Secretary  
2. Jayesh Bayla – Joint Secretary

NOMADS  
Club Nomads at Bennett University is a vibrant community that ignites the spirit of adventure. From short trips to nearby destinations to epic long journeys across continents, Nomads explore the world together. They indulge in adrenaline-pumping adventures, creating lifelong memories while fostering a strong sense of camaraderie.

Purpose  
The primary objective of the Club Nomads at Bennett University is to instil a passion for exploration, adventure, and bonding among its members. Its mission is to offer students the chance to partake in captivating brief and extended journeys, participate in exhilarating escapades, and unveil novel vistas. This club nurtures individual development, cultural appreciation, and the forging of enduring connections through collectively enjoyed travel escapades.

Major Activities  
The Club Nomads at Bennett University offers a wide-ranging scope, centered on travel, adventure, and personal development. It encompasses diverse travel experiences, from local getaways to global expeditions, fostering cultural engagement and environmental awareness. Members benefit from personal growth, forging lasting friendships, and developing skills while promoting wellness and balance. Cultural exchange opportunities abound, providing a platform for networking and enriching cross-cultural understanding.

Activities planned for the Tenure  
- Cultural Exploration  
- Adventure Tourism  
- Wildlife Safaris  
- Spiritual Journeys  
- Desert Adventures  
- Culinary Tours  
- Hill Station Retreats  
- Adventure Tourism  
- Festivals and Cultural Events

CEREBRUM – the official Literary & Debating Society at Bennett University – is modelled on the largest part of the brain that governs emotions, speech and the five senses. The society turns creative energy into a thriving hub of artistic excellence, welcoming every writer and performer who explores the world and celebrates life through ink and paper.

Purpose  
CEREBRUM represents the scholastic, academic and performative interests of the student cohort by acting as a platform for self-expression through literature and public speaking. It crafts a sense of community by bringing together individuals from across disciplines to converge and collaborate on activities they are enthusiastic about.

Major Activities  
An active quizzing circle, a lively debating society, thoughtful book reviews and soulful spoken-word sessions ensure CEREBRUM has something for everyone. Events are largely bilingual and promote linguistic plurality by fostering appreciation for—and encouraging submissions in—regional languages. Activities range from scholarly (e.g., MUN) to entertaining (e.g., Pop-Quiz), catering to wide student interests.

Activities planned for the tenure  
Open Mic  
Debate Night  
AALEKH 2024 (with LRC)  
Multilingual Poetry Presentation  
Round Table Conference  
LitComm  
BUMUN 3.0 (flagship event)  
Club Canvass  

Cerebrum is an on-campus home to artists, performers, and anyone who celebrates life. From an active quizzing department and poetry enthusiasts to debaters, readers, writers, orators, MUN participants and organizers—diverse, active departments constantly create art under its umbrella, making Cerebrum the most versatile club on campus and a place to quench intellectual thirst. All bookworms are welcome, and public-speaking enthusiasts will find their space too.

Instagram Handle: @cerebrum_bu

All the book worms are welcome to this club. And don’t you worry if you like public speaking. We have a space for everyone!

Instagram Handle: @cerebrum_bu

Events celebrated by CEREBRUM in 2022

SL NO | Name of the event | Event rendition  
1 | BUMUN | It is the flagship event of Cerebrum where we create a model of the United Nations and debate on the problems of the world. The various sustainable goals that countries are working upon are brought to the table and the delegates discuss upon those. All of our events are related to literature, debating, writing, books, speaking. We use words, not swords to bring the desired change.

Club heads

1 | Kushagra Joshi | Secretary  
2 | Shantanu Vyas | Joint Secretary

PANACHE

Club Panache channels style, confidence, charisma and originality. It showcases fashion, beauty and lifestyle through fresh, creative initiatives.

Purpose  
By uniting fashion, beauty and lifestyle, the club promotes the art of living. Its aim is to help individuals become their best, most confident selves so they can pursue any goal.

Panache – Bennett University’s fashion club – welcomes models, stylists, content-creators, make-up artists and every student passionate about style.

Major activities  
- Organise and participate in on-campus and off-campus fashion events, including university fests and other major happenings.  
- Provide members with wide-ranging exposure and opportunities in the fashion field.

Activities planned for the current tenure  
- Halloween  
- Uphoria  
- Ethnic Day  
- Red-themed Christmas Day  

Club motto  
Articulate emotions and unravel challenges through the power of style.  
As Miuccia Prada said: “What you wear is how you present yourself to the world… Fashion is instant language.”

Panache hosts fashion and theme shows, offers styling tips & tricks, and encourages students to express autonomous fashion by organising and participating in events throughout the year.

(Events celebrated by Panache Club in 2022 are listed separately.)

Events celebrated by Panache Club in 2022

1. Pink Day  
   Organised with Sunset Movie Club. Everyone wore pink all day; in the evening “Mean Girls” was screened for the campus. Tagline: “On Wednesdays we wear pink.”

2. Bollywood Day  
   Filler event of the annual festival Uphoria, in collaboration with Ansh Club. Participants dressed as Bollywood characters and competed in a dialogue-guessing contest; the best two performances were awarded.

3. Plethora of Emotions  
   Main fashion-show event of Uphoria. Models expressed emotions through style; four colleges from Delhi and Noida participated.

4. Halloween Day  
   Celebrated with the Student Council. Panache held “The Spooky Way” fashion walk featuring the spookiest student outfits.

Club heads  
1. Arshiya Sharma – Secretary  
2. Pushp Sharma – Joint Secretary

THE GIRLS’ HEALTH CLUB  
Girls Health Club is an Uttar Pradesh government’s initiative and a part of Mission Shakti 3.0, aimed at the empowerment of women in various fields. GHS ensures the well-being of girls physically, mentally, and emotionally. It further aims to cover various aspects of nutrition, health, and cleanliness.

Purpose  
The Club serves as a platform for girls to enhance their knowledge, skills, and confidence while addressing important health-related issues. It helps in the overall development and empowerment of women in personal life. Various activities conducted by the Girls Health Club (GHC) on social issues encourage girls to express their thoughts and ideas while promoting social consciousness. GHC acts as a pal to understand and solve the problems of the girls.

Major Activities  
- Community Engagement  
- Supportive Network  
- Intersectionality and Inclusivity  
- Educational Campaigns  
- Women’s empowerment  
- Nutrition and Fitness Awareness  

Activities planned for the Tenure  
- Interactive Session/Feedback Session  
- Gynec Meet-up Camp  
- Cervical Cancer Vaccination Awareness Camp  
- Street Performance on Mental Health and Nutrients Intake of Women in Domestic Household  
- Donation Drive  
- Valentine’s Special Poster Making  
- Valentine’s Special – Period Pain Simulator Booth  
- Celebrating Womanhood – student/faculty  
- Interactive Yoga/Meditation Session  
- Event Dedicated to the Ground Level Worker  
- Club Canvass  

It is a UP government initiative for women empowerment and a part of Mission Shakti 3.0. Through the diverse activities of the club, it aims to create an environment for women to grow in all aspects.

Events celebrated by Girls Health Club in 2022

Girls Health Club – 2022 Activities & Events

Through the diverse activities of the club, it is initiated to create an environment for women to grow in all aspects.

Events celebrated by Girls Health Club in 2022

SL NO | Name of the event | Event rendition  
1 | No shush (mini mela) | No shush was a three-day stall event under mini mela during Uphoria, which was based on myths regarding periods. We made people play five different games which were interesting.  
2 | Survey for 2 days (Sustainable menstruation) | It was a two-day event where we conducted a survey regarding sustainable menstruation products from different people including every respected gender (period inclusivity). It included handmade posters by the GHC team and many responses on Google Form regarding menstruation.  
3 | Sustainability week (Sustainable menstruation) | It is another two-day event which started from Ansh’s Nukkad Natak based on the condition of menstruation in our country and awareness regarding this topic. We then had a guest speaker, a gynecologist, for enlightenment on the topic of sustainable period products, followed by Qadira Hygiene’s demonstration on sustainable pads.

Club Head

SL NO | Name | Position  
1 | Swarnima Sharma | Secretary  
2 | Rashi Moraiya | Joint Secretary  
3 | Gayatri Purohoit | Joint Secretary

SUNSET MOVIE  
The university’s cine club that draws the biggest crowds for film screenings.  

Club Canvass  
Team Sunset works on the idea: “As the sun sets we rise again, and with the magic of night we make your mood vibrant.”  

Purpose  
A platform to explore, review and discuss films. Activities include movie screenings and Bollywood-themed events.  

Commitment  
“We at Sunset are always ready to book halls and keep screenings for the janta at Bennett. ‘Ye club nahi dekha to kya hi dekha?’”

About SUNSET  
We organize movie screenings and Bollywood-themed events.  
“We at Sunset are always ready to book halls and keep screenings for the janta at Bennett. ‘Ye club nahi dekha to kya hi dekha?’”

Instagram Handle: @Sunsetmovie.bennett

Events celebrated by SUNSET in 2022  
1. Movie Screenings – Mean Girls, Zindagi Na Milegi Dobara, SRK Night (Om Shanti Om, Ra.One, DDLJ), The Exorcism of Emily Rose  
2. Pink Day – collaboration with Panache Wear; screened Mean Girls  
3. Samosa with Sunset – collaboration with Cerebrum; Bollywood & Hollywood movie trivia  
4. Halloween Night of Horrors – screened The Exorcism of Emily Rose

Club Heads  
1. Shaurya Toma – Secretary  
2. Soha Inhas – Joint Secretary

Gallery

BOOK CLUB

The Book Club inculcates the love for books in the digital era. The club organises assorted events such as book reading sessions, discussions with authors, book fairs and other such events.

YOGA CLUB

Yoga being an ancient wellness technique to keep your mind, body and soul fit, is nurtured all over and Bennett University is no exception. The varsity has set up a student’s club to motivate them to practice yoga regularly.

Club Canvass  
Building a yoga culture at Bennett.  
The yoga club is a place for all the yoga enthusiasts to come together and practice a healthy morning yoga routine.

Instagram Handle: @yogaclub_bu

Events celebrated by YOGA CLUB in 2022

The yoga club is a place for all yoga enthusiasts to come together and practice a healthy morning yoga routine.

Instagram Handle: @yogaclub_bu

Events celebrated by YOGA CLUB in 2022

1. ABHYAS – Everyday morning yoga sessions before classes start throughout the semester.  
2. International Yoga Day – A gala day with external experts instructing on International Yoga Day.

Club Leadership  
1. Pratyush Chauhan – Secretary

ART OF LIVING  

Living a good life is a form of art. Art of Living is an endeavour of evolving, creating and searching for varied dimensions on which life can be led peacefully as an individual and in society. Universities play an imperative role in shaping students’ lives, and the Art of Living mantra nurtures mental health and peace.

Club Canvass  
AOL believes in spreading Love, Peace, Positivity and Happiness

Club Canvass

AOL believes in spreading Love, Peace, Positivity and Happiness  
This club aims to build a culture of meditation as it eliminates stress, increases immunity & raises the energy level  
We stand for poverty alleviation, women empowerment and mental health  
This club collaborates with Art of Living Centres across the country  

Instagram Handle: @Art_of_living_bu  

Events celebrated by Art of Living Club in 2022  
1. Nirvana – A (15+30) minutes event consisting of 15 minutes of pranayam along with 30 mins of meditation. This event is a collaboration between the Art of Living Club and The Yoga Club.  

Club heads  
1. Anvita Sharma – Secretary  
2. Vinti Sharma – Joint Secretary

Radio Club  
Bennett University operates a community radio channel, the Radio Club. Its programmes focus on health, education, environment, agriculture and rural development.

TECHNOTIX

If you are a robotics geek, join the club to experience the best.

Club Canvass  
The unsatiable appetite of fabricating wonders from seemingly plain objects is the feast that will be served to all RoboGeeks here in Technotix.

Reveal your heart to create, after all the first inspiration in creation of WALL-E is binocular at some baseball game.

The club routinely organises educational workshops and informative and educative events.

Instagram Handle: @technotix_bennett

TECHNOTIX is a university club that routinely organises educational workshops and informative, educative events.

Instagram Handle: @technotix_bennett

Events celebrated by TECHNOTIX in 2022

1. Community Event  
   A community event held on 23 April 2022 at a Government Senior Secondary School in JJ Cluster Colony, Bawana, Delhi. 42 students participated. After weeks of planning, budgeting and resource preparation, the students were taught to build an obstacle-avoidance robot from scratch.

2. Robo Race  
   Participants designed and raced self-made robocars in inter-college fellowships at Bennett University.

3. Retro Arcade (mini mela), Bluemoon  
   A robotics showcase held during the Bluemoon event.

Club heads

1. Shiv Gupta – Secretary  
2. Dhruv Wadhwa – Joint Secretary

Postgraduate  
- M.A. Mass Communication  
- PG Diploma in TV & Digital Journalism

ALEXIS at Bennett University is a student club dedicated to leaving a positive impact on society, promoting social responsibilities and making a meaningful difference in the community by addressing the vital aspects of well-being: social welfare and mental health. It is not merely a club but an outlet and a platform for students who want to work for society and leave a mark.

Purpose  
The name Alexis has a Greek origin and means “helper.” The club organises workshops, awareness campaigns, and public events to promote a supportive, compassionate environment in which personal development, social responsibility, and mental well-being advance together. Members experience the happiness and satisfaction of giving back to society.

Major Activities  
By joining, members become part of a community dedicated to serving social life and mental welfare, working together to create beneficial ripples throughout the community. Everyone can increasingly contribute to others.

Blood donation drives  
Mental health workshops  
Fundraisers for charity  
Art therapy workshops  

Activities planned for the tenure  
Blood Donation Drive  
World Food Day Stalls  
Orphanage Visit  
Handicraft Week  
Clothes Donation  
Old Age Home Visit  
Plantation Drive  
Valentine’s Day  
Club Canvass  

Alexis is the driver of change at Bennett. The club is a community of people working toward doing their part to make the world a better place. Alexis spreads awareness of social issues, hosts fundraisers and educational seminars, and conducts events that educate and provide volunteer opportunities for good causes.

Alexis has two sub-segments:  
BARC – for animal welfare  
Ruhaniyat – focuses on mental-health-related topics  

Events celebrated by Alexis club in 2022

Alexis Club comprises two sub-segments:

- BARC – dedicated to animal welfare  
- Ruhaniyat – focuses on mental-health topics  

Events celebrated by Alexis Club in 2022

1. Blood Donation – a joint initiative with NSS BU and Noida Blood Bank that collected 400+ units.  
2. Plantation Drive – a collaboration with NSS to plant trees and plants behind Block C4.  
3. Break the Stigma – an open discussion and expression forum for the LGBTQ community.  
4. Cleanliness Drive – repeated post-event and routine campus drives to promote hygiene.  
5. Mock CID – a murder-mystery game with clues hidden around campus for participants to solve.  
6. Spiral Dip – a tie-dye T-shirt workshop held jointly with Silhouette.  
7. Cloth Donation – collection from Bennett students and onward donation through an NGO to aid the underprivileged.  
8. NGO Visits – student trips to understand NGO operations and community impact.  
9. Orphanage & Old-Age Home Visits – Bennett students interact with residents to share experiences and support.

FOOD & CUISINE

Welcome to a world of flavour, friendship, and fulfilment! The Food and Cuisine Club is more than just a club; it’s a delicious odyssey! Founded by a group of dedicated foodies, the mission is to bring together like-minded individuals who appreciate the art of cooking, savour diverse cuisines, and explore the world through food.

Purpose

Purpose  
Food clubs often engage in discussions about culinary history, food sustainability, and nutrition. Guest speakers or experts may be invited to provide insights. They arrange cooking classes or workshops led by professional chefs or culinary experts to help members improve their cooking skills and knowledge.

Major Activities  
Last academic year they conducted a cake-decoration workshop and a chaat stall at UphoriaXZenevia, and served fruit chaat, bhel, and a no-bake cake at the Silhouette picnic.

Activities planned for the tenure  
- All-India Food Event showcasing food from every corner of India  
- Test of Tasting – a challenge for your taste buds  
- Mexican Fiesta – a vibrant, mouth-watering journey through Mexican flavours  
- Cook-off Challenge with a mystery ingredient  
- Multiple collaborative events

Club Canvass  
Food & Cuisine is your go-to destination for authentic cuisine. The club manages college events and you will find its stalls at every fest. Bollywood Night will again promote inter-club collaboration and unity. We aim to provide the best cuisine and fun games.

Instagram Handle  
@foodclub_bu

Events celebrated by Food & Cuisine Club in 2022  
SL NO | Name of the event | Event rendition  
1 | Food stall and game stall at mini mela in Uphoria | Different food stalls at mini mela involving bhelpuri, golgappe and food-related games over three days during Uphoria

Robotics Club
The club helps the students to form a brief understanding of various concepts and dynamics of Robotics. Students attend workshops, hold competitions and take part in different events to strengthen their technical skills.

Global Program

GeeksforGeeks Society

Introduction

Founded in 2020, the GeeksforGeeks-BU chapter has already organized 25+ major events by 2021 and is now one of the fastest-growing student chapters at Bennett University. The society is committed to providing the best platform for academic excellence and overall personal growth, fostering professionalism and practical experience. Its guiding belief—"great oaks grow from little acorns"—underpins its mission to nurture students into the protagonists of their own lives, a promise that has made GFG-BU one of the most popular and well-established chapters on campus.

AIM/ASPIRATIONS and MOTO

AIM / ASPIRATIONS and MOTO  
“What we learn with joy, we never forget.” – Alfred Mercier  
GeeksforGeeks-BU makes students industry-ready through skill-based learning. The chapter runs mock-interview drives, hackathons, technical workshops, and expert interactions while promoting holistic development.

Freshers’ Carnival  
1. A Gala with GeeksforGeeks – casual Q&A orientation introducing Bennett University, GeeksforGeeks, and campus life; 200+ attendees.  
2. Quizzel – fun quiz on sports & entertainment; enthusiastically received.  
3. Gaming Fest – 3-day tournament for freshers alongside BU gamers; titles: Chess, BGMI, Valorant; exciting prizes awarded.

Worldwide Hackathon in collaboration with DefHacks  
Def Hacks Worldwide 3.0 hosted by GeeksforGeeks at Bennett University, 25-27 June 2021.  
- 36-hour innovation sprint to build solutions for real-world problems.  
- Open to high-school and college/university students.  
- 2000+ participants from 43+ countries.  
- Guest speakers: Viral B. Shah (co-creator, Julia), Michael Seiber (Y-Combinator), Joshua Starmer (StatQuest).  
- Prizes & perks: $13,500+ cash, internships, networking with top tech minds; mini-events and fun activities throughout.

Geek-I-Knack

Geek-I-Knack, organized by the GeeksforGeeks Society of Bennett University in collaboration with 30 other GeeksforGeeks Student Chapters, was a comprehensive mock-interview drive created to help Indian engineering students prepare for placement interviews.

DAY 1 – Opening Ceremony with Chief Guest Mr. Sandeep Jain, Founder of GeeksforGeeks, followed by a 3-hour Coding Round that drew 12 000+ participants.  
DAY 2 – The top 100 students faced a Technical Interview Round conducted by 30+ industry experts from Google, Microsoft, Amazon and other companies.  
DAY 3 – All 100 finalists underwent an HR Interview Round.  

Every participant received personalized feedback from the experts, making the event an impactful confidence-building experience.

Geek-I-Knack  
Industry experts gave every participant individualized feedback. The event proved impactful and left attendees more confident.

Bennett Talks  
GeeksforGeeks organized “Bennett Talks: A 3-day interactive session by Bennett Alumni.” Three standalone sessions were delivered by:

- Nikhil Ramakrishnan – Software Development Engineer, Commvault; Google Summer of Code 2018  
- Kashish Suneja – Software Engineer, Microsoft (44 LPA)  
- Nandna Varshney – MBA student, XLRI; interned at Hewlett Packard Enterprises and HCL Technologies  
- Yash Raj Saxena – Associate Data Scientist, RxLogix  

Students from Bennett and other institutions attended. The talks were praised for their quality and relevance and are now counted among the most successful and informative events in Bennett’s history.

Conclusion  
GFG-BU emphasizes learning through experience. The chapter offers resources, guidance, and opportunities, driven by a coordinated team open to every geek.

Global Program

Augmented & Virtual Reality  
Bennett University is the one offering graduation (BTech/BCA) programmes in AR/VR Specialisation. The varsity has engaged CEOs and CXOs of well-known organisations like Simulanis, Yeppar, Whitebears Studio, Probability Gaming, Scientific Games to contribute to the curriculum design of this specialisation.  

In this digital world, there is an increasing trend of integration with emerging technologies. In the same way, integration of Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) to facilitate the superposition of virtual environment on physical world is becoming more popular these days.

Virtual Reality (VR) involves virtual environments which are digital with a complete cut from the physical environment. These environments can be used in games, training, and demonstration. There are several digital agencies which provide customized training solutions based on business needs. The VR example involves police training, sports training, live broadcast.

Augmented Reality (AR) involves real environments with the addition of digital elements. The live view can be captured using the camera. The digital elements will be added on top of the live view. Similar to VR, AR can also be used in games, training, and demonstration. The most popular example of AR is the game Pokemon Go.

Mixed Reality (MR) involves a mix of aspects from both virtual and real environments with the involvement of digital elements. The virtual objects can be mapped to the physical environment. It differs with AR where the virtual objects are placed on a separate layer on top of the physical world. Mixed Reality can be much more useful in AI-based training involving the mapping or interaction of digital elements with the physical world.

USPs of Specialisation:

In this specialisation, students undergo numerous projects and hackathons to gain knowledge and prepare them market ready for jobs such as, AR-VR game developer, UX/UI Designer, Mixed reality artist, technical 3D artist, etc.

The goal of this specialization is to create a workforce for AR/VR project development including Mixed Reality (MR) and Extended Reality (XR).

The aim of this specialisation is to train the students for utilising their knowledge in gaming, healthcare, manufacturing and logistic industries.

This specialization will equip students with the required skillset that may motivate them to create their own start-ups utilizing augmented and virtual reality.

Augmented & Virtual Reality Specialization

Overview  
This specialization equips students with the skill-set needed to launch AR/VR start-ups.

Campus Resources  
Insta360 Camera, Oculus Quest 2, Microsoft HoloLens, HTC Vive, green-screen, tripods, high-end gaming rigs, Apple Mac Lab, Nao Robot V6 and other head-mounted devices.

Market Outlook  
Domestic leaders (Tata, Reliance, Airtel) and global giants (Meta, Microsoft) are heavily investing; strong growth is projected.

Program Structure  
Core  
- Augmented Reality Foundations  
- Augmented Reality and ARCore  
- 3D Models for Augmented and Virtual Reality  
- Virtual Reality: Interface, Application and Design  

Elective  
- VR and 360 Video Production  
- Animation and Rendering Techniques  
- Building Social VR with 3D Characters  
- Special Topics in AR and VR

ALTReality – Augmented & Virtual Reality Club of Bennett University

Conducted Event
• VR Hack-2022  
  30 September – 01 October 2022  
  Internal | National

Club Brief  
ALTReality is the AR/VR club of Bennett University that designs and runs events, competitions and hackathons centred on AR/VR technology. As one of the most project-driven clubs on campus, it continuously builds showcase projects for competitions and launches. The club mentors budding developers into professionals and provides a dedicated learning platform for AR/VR—an opportunity still rare in India.

Mission  
Being project-based, ALTReality emphasises hands-on work: members implement projects individually or in teams and carry them forward to hackathons and flagship events such as Techfest.

Vision  
AR/VR will revolutionise how people experience the world, yet very few engineers currently specialise in the field. ALTReality aims to cultivate appreciation for AR/VR’s future impact and attract more individuals to participate in the early stages of this technological shift.

IEEE is the trusted voice for engineering, computing, and technology information around the globe. IEEE's core purpose is to foster technological innovation and excellence for the benefit of humanity. IEEE will be essential to the global technical community and technical professionals everywhere. Since the advent of IEEE Bennett University, members of the student chapter at BU have been contributing to this goal. IEEE is the world’s largest technical professional organization dedicated to advancing technology for the benefit of humanity. IEEE BU and its members inspire a global community through its highly cited publications, conferences, technology standards, and professional and educational activities.

IEEE BU (IEEE Bennett University Student Chapter) inspires a global community through highly-cited publications, conferences, technology standards, and professional and educational activities. It is a student chapter where students develop themselves; core and participating members form a harmonious, supportive community. With the largest number of events on campus, life is never stagnant for IEEE BU. Activities range from technical webinars to talk shows.

Core members receive plentiful learning opportunities and are trained in management, design, content writing, and more; their overall development is a key focus. This year IEEE BU launched IEEE Women in Engineering (WIE) at Bennett University, expanding its reach. WIE pledges to work toward gender-diversified panels at all IEEE meetings, conferences, and events.

Major successful and exclusive events include Hack Fiesta (IEEE Annual Hackathon), Mystery Manor, Bennett University’s only talk show, Project Expo, and a Research Paper on AI Workshop. IEEE BU is strongly committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion; hatred and discrimination have no place in its communities. It is “a family of people, for the development of Bennettians, by Bennettians.”

Currently IEEE BU is one of the largest chapters at Bennett University, with 50+ team members and sub-divisions such as IEEE Women in Engineering.

About IEEE WIE: IEEE Women in Engineering is a global network of IEEE members and volunteers dedicated to promoting women engineers and scientists and inspiring girls worldwide to pursue academic interests and careers in engineering and science.

Club Brief  
BU HackerEarth club is a platform for students to practice and improve programming skills, compete in coding challenges and Hackathons. The vision is to equip students with necessary skill sets, develop competency in cutting-edge technologies, make them industry-ready, and enable placement in highly reputed companies. The chapter organizes coding-skill development programs, training programs, expert talks, and uses social media to announce events; all events are hosted on the HackerEarth platform.

About GeeksforGeeks  
GeeksforGeeks is focused on making students industry-ready through skill-based education, offering online courses, tutorials, practice problems, interview experiences, and guidance sessions.  

About GeeksforGeeks Society of Bennett University  
Guided by “What we learn with joy, we never forget – Alfred Mercier,” the GeeksforGeeks Society of Bennett University was established.

GeeksforGeeks Society of Bennett University  
Founded on 20 July 2020, the society serves as a dynamic hub where computer-science enthusiasts meet, interact, and learn together.  
Activities include mock-interview drives, hackathons, technical workshops, invited talks, and coding competitions for 2000+ members.

Cipher’s Club

Events Hosted  
1. 3 Oct 2021 – Cryptic Hunt – Internal – National  
2. 12 Nov 2021 – Cyber-security Webinar (Mr. Talha Jawad) – Internal – National  
3. 30 Dec 2021 – The Cipherio Night – Internal – National  
4. 17 Feb 2022 – Cipher Conference: Cyber-crime & Careers in Cyber-security (Mr. Rakshit Tandon) – Internal – National

Club Brief  
Aim: Create a safe, intriguing college environment that teaches cyber-security skills and equips students to defend against modern computer exploits.  

Vision: Build a student-run community of “ciphers” who demystify hacking and broaden perspectives on cyberspace in an approachable way.  

Why this club  
- Dedicated space for cyber-security specialization, complementing the AI- and data-science-focused clubs.  
- Represents Bennett University as a white-hat hacker collective in hackathons.  
- Prioritizes Bennettian security: raises awareness of phishing, third-party-app risks, and the limits of two-factor authentication.

Conducted Events Details

S.No. | Date | Name of Event | Internal/External | National/International  
1 | 28th January 2021 | Source Code | Internal | National  
2 | 30th October 2021 | Introduction to C++ & Basic Programming Concepts | Internal | National  
3 | 9th February 2019 | Tech Hunt | Internal | National  
4 | 27th August 2019 | Orientation CodeWars Lite | Internal | National  
5 | 6th February 2021 | CodeWars Springtime | Internal | National  
6 | 3rd September 2019 | CodeWars September | Internal | National

CodeChef provides information about every event that is held on CodeChef's official website, spreads awareness to coders, and motivates them to take part. We supply guidelines and tips on how to participate and ace events and contests. The club’s goal is to enable students to indulge in competitive programming and to write optimized code. Our vision is to help everyone understand the need for competitive programming and to enable them to write optimized, efficient code. As an official branch of CodeChef we can conduct events for our to-be developers on topics such as placements in different companies and the steps required to reach them. We have all the facilities needed to help you solve questions in multiple languages. Whether it’s a job interview or admission to your preferred university for higher studies, strengthening coding skills and knowledge of DSA is the first step. A 5-star rating on CodeChef earns special recognition that can help in interviews. We can also organize placement talks to guide students. Events are organized from basic to advanced levels so students understand the fundamentals of competitive programming. We cater to the whole group, not just a single section. Our collaboration with the official CodeChef platform allows us to organize our own events easily, and CodeChef provides rewards to the top coders of these events.

CLUB BRIEF

CSI exists to uplift the computer-science community and create a positive impact among its members. The close-knit core team continuously launches campaigns, events and study groups. Through the chapter, students receive boundless opportunities and exposure far beyond textbook syllabi. The journey so far has included workshops, hackathons, learning weekends, competitions and educational visits, all of which continue to be delivered.

Residential Facilities
The University campus offers houses for its academic and other staff in the safety of its premises. The campus is a lush-green 68-acre sprawling area, so living within the premises, away from the pollution, noise and disturbances of the main city, is a blessing for those who choose to live here.

Houses  
Student Achievements

Hostels at Bennett University  
Bennett University offers thoughtfully designed on-campus and off-campus (Nalanda Living) hostel accommodations that provide safe, comfortable, and modern living spaces.  
With triple-sharing rooms for boys and girls, each unit at on-campus hostels includes essential amenities like air conditioning, ample storage, and cosy beds. Hygiene is maintained through regularly cleaned common washrooms and 24/7 hot water.

Recreational common rooms provide games and entertainment, promoting relaxation and overall wellbeing of students. The campus ensures seamless living with round-the-clock operations, power backup, and centralised cooling.

Hostels accommodate 10,000 students. Triple-sharing rooms for boys and girls, furnished with beds, cupboards, and air conditioning. Common washrooms are cleaned regularly and 24/7 hot water is available. Common entertainment rooms offer pool, carrom, TT, and TV. Power backup is provided via DG sets and centralized temperature control is maintained. Routine maintenance keeps utilities running smoothly. A dedicated safety team conducts daily campus checks. Students have access to sports, wellness, and fitness facilities for holistic living.

Modern Accommodation  
Experience comfort and camaraderie in modern hostels designed to house 10,000 students. State-of-the-art amenities and a lively community create vibrant campus life.

Cozy Hostel Rooms  
Well-furnished triple-sharing rooms feature comfortable beds, air conditioners, and cupboard space, fostering unforgettable moments and lifelong memories.

BU Basiks: The Essential Store  
BU Basiks supplies everyday essentials—stationery, fruits, dairy products, munchies, cosmetics, and more—making convenience just a step away.

Gobble: The Campus Cafeteria  
Gobble serves homely breakfast, lunch, and dinner, keeping students fuelled throughout their busy college lives.

Hassle-Free Laundry Services

Hassle-Free Laundry Services  
Students enjoy hassle-free laundry services available throughout the day. This convenient facility ensures their clothes stay fresh and clean, allowing them to focus more on studies and campus life.

Sports & Fitness Facilities  
Relax and unwind while staying fit and healthy. The Sports Complex at Bennett University offers 16+ sports facilities, including fully equipped gyms, to help you stay active and healthy.

24/7 Wellness Centre  
The Wellness Centre at the university offers round-the-clock medical facilities with experienced male and female nurse attendants. A qualified general physician is available during the day and on-call at night.

Hangout Spots for Food & Fun  
From Southern Stories to Maggi Point to Domino's, Bennett University's vibrant hangout spots offer a perfect blend of flavours and fun, where friendships are brewed and lasting memories are made.

Bennett University Life – Infrastructure

Modern Infrastructure at Bennett University  
Bennett University continually expands its facilities for academics and student welfare. The campus now includes a moot court, media labs, scientific labs, research centres, hostels, eating joints, a hostel mess, 24 × 7 medical support through a wellness centre, modern gyms, and well-stocked libraries. A smoke-free, eco-conscious environment with round-the-clock CCTV surveillance ensures a safe, sustainable, and inspiring space for every student.

RF SPUTTERING UNIT  
Learn about thin-film deposition techniques using RF Sputtering Unit.

FTIR

Gain practical experience and technical skills through hands-on lab-based learning.

Experience engineering principles in action through practical workshop training.

Radio Studio  
Gain real-world broadcasting experience and storytelling skills at the radio studio.

Television Studio  
Gain hands-on experience with multicamera setup, television sets, green screen, and production workflows.

Central Library  
The library is well-stocked with the latest collection of books, journals, e-resources, and study spaces.

Law Library  
The university offers a spacious dedicated law library to meet the research and reference needs of law students.

Moot Court  
The campus boasts of a moot court which is a simulated courtroom for legal advocacy training.

Cafeteria – Where food is served with love  
The two-storeyed main cafeteria of the campus has an ample seating capacity and serves varied cuisines.

Non-veg Cafeteria  
Keeping in mind the different food preferences of students, the university runs a separate non-vegetarian cafeteria.

Air-Conditioned Hostel Blocks  
To make study tenure peaceful and secure, Bennett University has modern, air-conditioned hostel blocks with 24×7 support services.

Sports Complex  
Sports complex inside the university always deserves special mention for its indoor and outdoor facilities that promote fitness and competitive sports.

24×7 ATM on Campus  
Round-the-clock ATM facility is available within the campus for banking convenience.

Media Labs  
To make the learning at Times School of Media skill-based and industry-oriented, the university provides state-of-the-art media labs.

Wellness Center  
To ensure students’ well-being, Bennett provides a well-equipped wellness centre with medical rooms and trained staff.

Tuck Shop  
A convenient on-campus tuck shop caters to daily essentials and quick snacks.

Sports at Bennett University

At Bennett University, sports are an integral part of student life. The campus offers a wide range of world-class facilities to encourage physical fitness, teamwork, and discipline. Students have access to cricket and football grounds, badminton and basketball courts, kabaddi and volleyball courts, a swimming pool, lawn tennis and squash courts, gymnasiums, and indoor games like chess, carrom, snooker, and pickleball. Whether for recreation or competition, the university offers an ideal environment to pursue sporting excellence and maintain an active lifestyle.

Unleashing Potential Through Sports

Pickleball  
Fast, fun, and fiercely competitive on every serve...

Cricket  
Bat, ball, and brilliance—cricket builds leadership, focus, and sportsmanship...

Football  
Chase goals, craft strategies, and feel the adrenaline on every kick...

Kabaddi  
Hold your breath—strength, skill, and strategy collide in every raid...

Badminton  
Fast-paced rallies and feathered smashes keep the spirit flying high...

Carrom  
Sharp aim, smooth strikes—precision and patience rule the carrom board...

Tennis  
Serve hard, rally long—tennis turns practice and power into finesse...

Squash  
Quick steps, sharp angles—squash is speed, skill, and smart returns...

Volleyball  
Jump high, dig deep—volleyball fuels teamwork, energy, and court drama...

Basketball  
Dribble, pass, shoot—basketball blends agility, speed, and winning spirit...

Table Tennis  
Spin, smash, and speed—table tennis sharpens reflexes and tests precision...

Chess  
Every move counts—chess sharpens minds and builds strategic thinkers...

Snooker/Pool

Spin, smash, and speed—table tennis sharpens reflexes and tests precision.

Chess  
Every move counts—chess sharpens minds and builds strategic thinkers.

Snooker/Pool  
Silent concentration, smooth shots—snooker is chess on a green table.

Foosball  
Fast flicks, loud cheers—foosball is pure fun in every spin.

Swimming  
Dive in, glide strong—swimming builds endurance, confidence, and calm precision.

Gymnasium  
Push limits, lift strength—where discipline shapes body, mind, and resilience.

Recreational Rooms/Common Rooms  
Unwind after a hectic day with any calming sport.

Transportation Facility

Transportation Facility at Bennett University

The centrally managed transport department at Bennett University operates well-defined routes that include key pick-up points such as metro stations. All buses are air-conditioned, and due care is taken to ensure students' safe and comfortable journey. The university regularly updates its transport guidelines in line with the directives issued by the Central and State Governments, and the UGC.

Transport charges are based on the distance travelled to and from the university.

For details on availing transport services, refer to the transport policy here.

Click Here

For additional information and transport fee details, contact

Transport Helpline at : +91 9319993116

Bennett University Placements  
Powering Dreams. Shaping Careers.

At Bennett University, placements aren’t just about landing a job—they're about launching a career. Our Career Services Team works closely with students, helping them build the skills, confidence, and industry connections needed to succeed in a rapidly changing world.

Bennett University does not just provide theoretical academic education; it places great stress on making its students career-ready for the New Age economy.

With a strong network of leading recruiters, global partnerships, and mentorship, we don’t just prepare students for their first job—we prepare them for lifelong success.

B.Tech  
450+ Participating Organizations  
Highest package: ₹1.37 crore per annum

B.Tech  
Participating Organizations: 450+  
Highest Package: 1.37 Crore PA  
Average Package: 11.10 LPA  
Top Recruiters: Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Google, Dell, Deloitte, HCL, Infosys, KPMG, TCS, Goldman Sachs, Grant Thornton, Morgan Stanley, S&P Capital, Cisco, Tech Mahindra, Scaler-Academy, Coca-Cola, HSBC, Cadence

   – The fee covers 10 months; summer-break stay, if permitted, is charged separately.  
   – Subject to an annual increase of up to 7%.

   Distance-based, payable per semester; monthly rates and payment details are available on the University website.

Special notes
- Students staying in Nalanda Living (off-campus hostel) are treated as day scholars and are exempt from the annual sports charges while they reside there; if they move out, the applicable sports charges for the corresponding academic year(s) become payable.

Disclaimer  
Students opting for Nalanda Living, Bennett University’s preferred off-campus hostel, will be treated as day scholars but will be exempt from the annual sports charges (currently ₹26,000) while they reside at Nalanda. If a student moves out of Nalanda, the applicable sports charges for the corresponding academic year(s) must then be paid to the University.  

The hostel fee is charged per annum and covers 10 months, excluding the summer break. Any additional stay during the summer break, if permitted, may incur separate charges.  

Fee is subject to revision at the start of each academic year as per BU policy.

If they move out of Nalanda, the applicable sports charges for the corresponding academic year(s) become payable.  

Hostel fee is charged per annum and covers 10 months, excluding the summer break. Any additional stay during the summer break, if permitted, may incur separate charges.  

All fees are subject to revision at the start of the new academic year as per Bennett University policy.

Other Applicable Charges
• Security Deposit (One-time, Refundable): ₹30,000
• Examination Charges: ₹7,000 per academic year
• Co-curricular Charges: ₹3,000 per academic year
• Degree Digitalization Charges (One-time): ₹2,500
• Alumni Charges (One-time): ₹1,500
• Optional Transport Charges (Day Scholars only): Distance-based, payable per semester; monthly rates and payment details available on request.

Additional Information
1. Students in Nalanda Living (off-campus hostel) are treated as day scholars and are exempt from the annual sports charges (₹26,000) while residing there; charges become payable if they move out.
2. Hostel fee is charged per annum for a 10-month period, excluding summer break; separate charges apply for any permitted summer stay.
3. All fees are subject to revision at the start of each academic year as per university policy.

Optional Transport Charges (Day Scholars only): Payable per semester; distance-based.

Nalanda Living (preferred off-campus hostel): Residents are treated as day scholars and are exempt from annual sports charges (₹26,000) while staying at Nalanda; if they move out, applicable sports charges for the corresponding academic year(s) must be paid.  

All fees are subject to revision at the start of each academic year per University policy.

Optional Transport Charges (Day Scholars only): Charges are based on the distance from the university and are payable per semester. For monthly rates and payment details, Click Here.

Students opting for Nalanda Living, Bennett University’s preferred off-campus hostel, will be a day scholar but exempt from annual sports charges (currently ₹26,000) during their stay at Nalanda. However, if a student moves out of Nalanda, they will have to pay the applicable sports charges to the University for the corresponding academic year(s).

The hostel fee mentioned is charged per annum. However, this fee is applicable for a duration of 10 months, excluding the summer break period. Please note that the summer months are not covered under the standard hostel fee structure. Any additional stay during the summer break, if permitted, may be subject to separate charges.

Fee is subject to revision with the new academic year as per BU policy.

MBA  
B.Tech – Computer Science Engineering  

Bennett University Admission Scholarships are awarded for the first year of the Undergraduate programme. Continuation in subsequent years requires a CGPA ≥ 8 on a 10-point scale, no backlogs, and no disciplinary action.

If an applicant does not meet the Admission-scholarship criteria, he/she may still qualify for Academic Scholarships (details in the next section).

Scholarship slabs (based on Best-of-3* / CUET / JEE Main / IB / SAT)  
50 % off – XII ≥ 96 % OR CUET ≥ 90 OR JEE ≥ 90 percentile OR IB ≥ 40 OR SAT ≥ 1401  
30 % off – XII 91–95.99 % OR CUET 75–89.99 OR JEE 75–89.99 percentile OR IB 34–39 OR SAT 1351–1400  
20 % off – XII 86–90.99 % OR CUET 70–74.99 OR JEE 70–74.99 percentile OR IB 32–33 OR SAT 1301–1350  
10 % off – XII 81–85.99 % OR CUET 65–69.99 OR JEE 65–69.99 percentile OR IB 30–31 OR SAT 1201–1300  

*Best-of-3: Physics & Mathematics compulsory; third subject must be the next-highest scoring academic subject (vocational subjects such as Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc. are not considered).

Other (Special) Scholarships  
Chancellor’s Scholarship Fund – The Vineet Jain Scholarship  
Created to support deserving and needy students. Eligible categories:  
- Children of Armed Forces / Paramilitary personnel  
- Families affected by conflict or terrorism  
- Victims of natural calamities / disasters  
- First-generation college students from underserved regions  
- Promising women scholars and rural achievers  
- State / National-level athletes and para-athletes  
- State toppers and under-privileged high achievers  
- Students with special needs / disabilities  
- Annual family income below ₹ 7 lakh  

Scholarship Helpdesk: admissions@bennett.edu.in

Students with Special Needs/Disabilities  
Students with Annual Family Income Below ₹7 LPA  

Contact Information  
Scholarship Helpdesk: admissions@bennett.edu.in  

Sibling  
10 % tuition-fee concession for the period both siblings are enrolled, applicable only to children of the same parents (not cousins).  

Single Girl Child  
10 % tuition-fee concession for the entire duration of the programme.  

Alumni  
10 % tuition-fee concession for Bennett University graduates admitted to any postgraduate programme.  

Defence Personnel  
Wards of serving and retired Indian Armed Forces (Army, Navy, Air Force) and Paramilitary personnel (CISF, BSF, CRPF, ITBP, NSG, SSB) receive 5 % tuition-fee concession for the complete duration of the programme.

Terms & Conditions  
In cases where a student is eligible for multiple scholarships, the university will award the scholarship with the highest value, as per university guidelines.

For B.Tech programs, marks in Physics, Maths & any third subject in the Class XII examination conducted by CBSE or an equivalent examination from a recognized board will be considered for scholarship.  
For other programs, aggregate marks of the best 3 subjects (with English being mandatory for certain programs) in the Class XII examination conducted by CBSE or an equivalent examination from a recognized board will be considered for scholarship eligibility.  
For design programs, the scholarship will be awarded based on overall aggregate marks.  
For Boards other than CBSE, normalized scores as decided by the Scholarship and Admission Committee will be considered.  
Scholarships will be awarded strictly on merit.  
If the number of eligible students exceeds the available scholarships in a particular bracket, students with lower merit rankings will be moved to the next available lower scholarship bracket.  
Only valid scores from the respective board/examinations will be considered for scholarship eligibility.  
Scholarships are granted based on the specified criteria at the sole discretion of the Admission and Scholarship Committee.  
The management reserves the right to amend the scholarship policy at any time.

B.Tech EEC / ECE / ME  
The Bennett University Admission Scholarship is awarded for the first year of the Undergraduate program.  
Continuation of the Admission Scholarship in subsequent years is subject to obtaining a CGPA of 8 on a scale of 10, having no backlog, and no discipline case being recorded against the student.  
If a candidate does not meet the Admission Scholarship criteria, he/she may be eligible for Academic Scholarships as outlined in the next section.

Academic Scholarships (if the applicant does not qualify for an Admission scholarship)

Scholarship slabs – based on “Best-of-3” score (Physics & Mathematics compulsory; third subject must be the highest-scoring non-vocational subject; vocational subjects such as Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc. are not accepted)

Percentage of tuition-fee waiver  
50 % XII ≥ 95 | JEE-Main ≥ 80 | IB ≥ 40 | SAT ≥ 1401  
30 % XII 85–94.99 | JEE-Main 75–79.99 | IB 34–39 | SAT 1351–1400  
20 % XII 80–84.99 | JEE-Main 65–74.99 | IB 32–33 | SAT 1301–1350  
10 % XII 75–79.99 | JEE-Main 60–64.99 | IB 30–31 | SAT 1201–1300  

Other (Special) Scholarships

Sibling 10 % tuition-fee waiver for the period the siblings’ programmes overlap; applies only to children of the same parents, not cousins.  
Single girl child 10 % tuition-fee waiver for the entire duration of the programme.  
Alumni 10 % tuition-fee waiver for Bennett University alumni admitted to any postgraduate programme.  
Defence Personnel 5 % tuition-fee waiver for the entire programme duration for wards of serving/retired personnel of the Indian Army, Navy, Air Force and Paramilitary forces (CISF, BSF, CRPF, ITBP, NSG, SSB).

Terms & Conditions  
- If a student qualifies for more than one scholarship, the university will grant the single highest-value award.  
- Eligibility is determined as follows:  
  – B.Tech: Best-of-3 marks in Physics, Mathematics and any third subject in the Class-XII CBSE (or equivalent) board exam.  
  – Other programmes: Aggregate of Best-3 subjects in Class-XII (English compulsory for selected programmes) from CBSE or an equivalent recognised board.  
  – Non-CBSE boards: Normalised scores fixed by the Scholarship & Admission Committee will be used.  
- All awards are merit-based; if applicants in a bracket outnumber available scholarships, lower-ranked students are shifted to the next lower bracket, if one exists.  
- Only valid board/exam scores count; the Admission & Scholarship Committee decides all awards, and the university may change the policy at any time.

B.Tech – Engineering Physics  
Bennett University Admission Scholarship is given for the first year only.  
To keep the scholarship in later years the student must:  
- Maintain a CGPA ≥ 8/10,  
- Have no backlogs,  
- Have no disciplinary action on record.  

Candidates who do not qualify for an Admission Scholarship may still receive an Academic Scholarship (see next section).

Scholarship slabs (Year 1, based on Class-XII Best-of-3 score or IB/CUET equivalent)  
- 50 % fee waiver: ≥ 95 % or IB ≥ 40 or CUET ≥ 95  
- 30 % fee waiver: 85–94.99 % or IB 34–39 or CUET 85–94.99  
- 20 % fee waiver: 80–84.99 % or IB 32–33 or CUET 80–84.99  
- 10 % fee waiver: 75–79.99 % or IB 30–31 or CUET 75–79.99

Merit-based Scholarship Matrix  
Weight  | XII Score (Best of 3)* | IB Score | CUET  
50 %    | ≥ 95                 | ≥ 40     | ≥ 95  
30 %    | 85 – 94.99           | 34 – 39  | 85 – 94.99  
20 %    | 80 – 84.99           | 32 – 33  | 80 – 84.99  
10 %    | 75 – 79.99           | 30 – 31  | 75 – 79.99  

*Best of 3: Physics & Maths compulsory; third subject must be the next highest-scoring academic subject (vocational subjects such as Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc. are not considered).

Other (Special) Scholarships  

Chancellor Scholarship Fund – The Vineet Jain Scholarship  
Instituted to support deserving and financially constrained students.  
Eligibility Categories  
- Children of Armed Forces and Paramilitary personnel  
- Families affected by conflict or terrorism  
- Victims of natural calamities or disasters  
- First-generation college students from underserved regions  
- Promising women scholars and rural achievers  
- State/National-level athletes and para-athletes  
- State toppers and deserving students from underprivileged backgrounds  
- Students with special needs/disabilities  
- Students whose annual family income is below ₹ 7 lakh  

Contact: Scholarship Helpdesk – admissions@bennett.edu.in

Sibling Concession  
10 % tuition-fee waiver during the overlapping period of two siblings’ programmes; applicable only to children of the same parents (not cousins).

Single Girl Child Concession  
10 % tuition-fee waiver for the entire duration of the programme.

Alumni Scholarship  
10 % tuition-fee waiver for Bennett University alumni admitted to any postgraduate programme.

Defence Personnel Concession  
5 % tuition-fee waiver for the entire programme duration for wards of serving/retired Indian Armed Forces (Army, Navy, Air Force) and Paramilitary Forces (CISF, BSF, CRPF, ITBP, NSG, SSB) personnel.

Terms & Conditions
- When a student qualifies for more than one scholarship, the university will grant the single scholarship that carries the highest monetary value, in accordance with university guidelines.
- B.Tech programs: scholarship eligibility is based on the marks obtained in Physics, Mathematics and any third subject in the Class XII examination conducted by CBSE or an equivalent board recognised by the university.
- All other programs: eligibility is determined by the aggregate marks of the best three subjects in Class XII (CBSE or equivalent); English is mandatory for certain programs.
- Design programs: eligibility is based on the overall aggregate marks of Class XII.
- Boards other than CBSE: scores will be normalised as decided by the Scholarship and Admission Committee.
- Scholarships are awarded strictly on merit. If the number of eligible candidates in a bracket exceeds the available scholarships, lower-ranked candidates will be moved to the next lower scholarship bracket, if vacancies exist.
- Only valid board/examination scores will be considered.
- All scholarships are granted solely at the discretion of the Admission and Scholarship Committee.
- The university reserves the right to amend the scholarship policy at any time.

B.Tech Biotechnology – Bennett University Admission Scholarship
- The scholarship is awarded for the first year of the undergraduate program.
- Continuation in subsequent years requires:
  – a minimum CGPA of 8.0 on a 10-point scale,
  – no backlog in any subject,
  – no disciplinary action recorded against the student.
- Candidates who do not meet the Admission Scholarship criteria may still qualify for Academic Scholarships as detailed in the next section.

- Only valid board/examination scores are considered.

5% tuition-fee scholarship, applicable for the entire duration of the programme, to wards of:  
- Serving and retired personnel of the Indian Army, Navy and Air Force  
- Personnel of Paramilitary forces: CISF, BSF, CRPF, ITBP, NSG, SSB  

Terms & Conditions  
- A student eligible for more than one scholarship will receive only the highest-valued award.  
- B.Tech: Scholarship based on Class-XII marks in Physics, Mathematics and the best third subject (CBSE or equivalent board).  
- Other programmes: Scholarship based on aggregate of best three subjects in Class XII (English mandatory for certain programmes).  
- Design programmes: Scholarship based on overall Class-XII aggregate.  
- For boards other than CBSE, normalized scores determined by the Scholarship & Admission Committee will be used.  
- All scholarships are merit-based; if the number of eligible candidates exceeds available slots in a bracket, lower-ranked candidates are moved to the next lower bracket, if available.  
- Only valid board/examination scores will be considered.  
- Scholarships are awarded at the sole discretion of the Admission & Scholarship Committee.  
- The University reserves the right to modify the scholarship policy at any time.

B.Tech

Other programmes

Design programmes (including B.Des)  
Overall Class XII aggregate is used.

Boards other than CBSE  
Scores are normalized by the scholarship and admission committee.

Tie-break / quota overflow  
If eligible students outnumber awards in a bracket, lower-ranked candidates are dropped to the next lower bracket if places exist.

The Bennett University Admission Scholarships are awarded for the first year of the Undergraduate program. Continuation of Admission Scholarship in subsequent years is subject to obtaining CGPA of 8 on a scale of 10, having no backlog and no discipline case being recorded against the student.
