Scholarship Range  
XII (Best of 3)* / CUET / JEE Mains / IB / SAT  
50 % – >=96 / >=90 / >=40 / >=1401  
30 % – 91-95.99 / 75-89.99 / 34-39 / 1351-1400  
20 % – 86-90.99 / 70-74.99 / 32-33 / 1301-1350  
10 % – 81-85.99 / 65-69.99 / 30-31 / 1201-1300  

*Best of 3: Physics & Maths as compulsory subjects & any other third subject with highest score. Third subject should not be vocational—Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc.

Other (Special) Scholarships

Sibling  
10 % is applicable for the duration of overlap of the programs of siblings and applicable to wards of the same parents and not cousins.

Single girl child  
10 % is applicable for complete duration of program on tuition fee.

Alumni  
10 % tuition-fee scholarship is available for Bennett University alumni who enroll in a postgraduate program at the university.

Sports  
Up to 50 % scholarship on first-year tuition fees is available for candidates with state, national, or international-level sports participation or achievements. Scholarships are subject to document verification at the time of registration.

Defence Personnel  
Wards of serving and retired personnel from the Indian Armed Forces (Army, Navy, Air Force) and Paramilitary forces (CISF, BSF, CRPF, ITBP, NSG, and SSB) are eligible for a 5 % scholarship on tuition fees. This scholarship is applicable for the entire duration of the program.

Terms & Conditions  
- If a student qualifies for more than one scholarship, only the single highest-value award is granted.  
- B.Tech eligibility: marks in Physics, Mathematics and any third subject in Class XII (CBSE or equivalent board).  
- Other programmes: aggregate of the best three Class-XII subjects (English compulsory for some); for Design programmes, overall aggregate is used.  
- Non-CBSE boards: scores are normalised by the Scholarship & Admission Committee.  
- All awards are merit-based; if applicants outnumber scholarships in a bracket, lower-ranked students are dropped to the next lower bracket if one exists.  
- Only valid board/examination scores are accepted.  
- The Admission & Scholarship Committee decides all awards; the university may alter the policy at any time.

B.Tech Computer Science Engineering (Global) – Iowa State University, USA  
Scholarship vs. fee (USD) after award, effective 5th semester onward (3rd year):

CGPA ≥ 9.00  USD 4,500 → net fee USD 53,500  
CGPA 8.00–8.99 USD 3,500 → net fee USD 54,500  
CGPA 7.00–7.99 USD 2,500 → net fee USD 55,500  
CGPA 6.50–6.99 USD 2,000 → net fee USD 56,000  

*Conversion from USD to INR uses the prevailing exchange rate.

Scholarships / Discount Offerings from 5th Semester onward (3rd year)  
*USD–INR conversion at the prevailing rate

| 8–8.99 | 3,500                      | 54,500                        | 10,46,000                    | 46,74,504 + 10,46,000 ≈ 57,20,504 |

Bennett University > International Admissions  
Ms. Hitankshi Thukral – VP Marketing & Admissions (Domestic & International)

Welcome International Students  
Bennett University is dedicated to cultivating leaders of tomorrow and has taken a significant stride towards internationalizing higher education by opening admissions for the academic year 2025 to students worldwide. With a focus on diversifying academic perspectives and experiences, the university has implemented policies and initiatives that provide robust support for international integration, offering academic and social assistance while ensuring a positive, nurturing environment.

We are committed to delivering educational excellence through innovative technology, high-quality learning experiences, targeted recruitment strategies, scholarships, and a welcoming campus atmosphere.

Bennett University invites undergraduate and postgraduate applicants worldwide to join a globally connected academic community that values cross-cultural understanding and strategic, transparent International Admissions for 2025.

WHO CAN APPLY  
Undergraduate, Postgraduate and Diploma-seeking students from SAARC and non-SAARC countries.

HOW TO APPLY  
1. Register and complete the online application form.  
2. Pay the application fee and submit the form.  
3. Receive a unique Application Number and a confirmation e-mail with next-step instructions.  
4. Click here to apply for International Admissions 2025.

WHY CHOOSE BENNETT UNIVERSITY  
- Accessibility – 25 km from Delhi, close to the upcoming Noida International Airport, Jewar.  
- Quality Education – located in a major academic hub.  
- Cultural Diversity – rich heritage and cosmopolitan environment.  
- Career Opportunities – proximity to numerous multinational companies.  
- Modern Infrastructure – smart-city amenities and state-of-the-art facilities.  
- Residential Facilities – inclusive on-campus housing that fosters belonging for students of all backgrounds.

PROGRAMMES OFFERED  
B.Tech – AI, CSE, ECE/EEC/ME/EP, Biotechnology, Integrated B.Tech + M.Tech Biotechnology  
BCA  
BBA, BBA+MBA 5-year Integrated  
BA LLB (Hons), BBA LLB (Hons)  
BA Mass Communication  
BA Film, TV & Web Series  
BA Liberal Arts  
Postgraduate and Diploma programmes (details on eligibility, selection criteria, fee structure and scholarships available on the admissions portal).

BCA / BBA / BBA+MBA 5-yr Integrated  
BA LLB (Hons) / BBA LLB (Hons)  
BA Mass Communication / BA Film, TV & Web Series  
BA Liberal Arts  

Eligibility: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third subject with minimum 65 % marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24  
Selection: Merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (Physics and Maths compulsory; highest-score third subject)  

Eligibility: 60 % aggregate in grade 12 / A-level or equivalent (with Physics & Mathematics) and English Language Requirement (ELR)  
Selection: Same Best-of-3 rule (Physics & Maths compulsory; highest-score third subject)  

Eligibility: 55 % aggregate in grade 12 / A-level or equivalent (with Physics & Mathematics) and ELR  
Selection: Same Best-of-3 rule (Physics & Maths compulsory; highest-score third subject)  

Eligibility: 55 % aggregate in grade 12 / A-level or equivalent (with Physics & Biology / Biotechnology / Botany / Zoology) and ELR  
Selection: Best of 3 subjects score (Physics and Biology/Biotechnology/Botany/Zoology compulsory; highest-score third subject)  

Eligibility: 55 % aggregate in grade 12 / A-level or equivalent and ELR  
Selection: Best of 3 subjects score (English compulsory; two highest-score other subjects)

Eligibility  
- 55 % aggregate marks in Grade 12 / A-level or equivalent plus English Language Requirement (ELR).  

Selection  
- Based on Class-XII “Best-of-3” score (English compulsory + the two other highest-scoring subjects) and a personal interview.  

Programs covered by the above criteria  
MBA | LL.M. | MA Mass Communication | MCA | M.Tech | Integrated M.Tech + PhD  

Additional note for international applicants  
- Minimum 50 % in the qualifying examination with prerequisite subjects studied.  

Selection for international applicants  
- Weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU-MAT / CUET percentile + graduation performance + personal interview (applicable to Sept MAT 2024 & Dec MAT 2024 cycles).

Eligibility: Minimum 50 % in the qualifying examination for international applicants; must have studied the prerequisite subjects for the desired program.

Selection: Based on aggregate graduation percentage; for some programmes a personal interview is also required.

Programmes:
- PGD – Computer Science & Engineering  
- PGD – Computer Applications  

Fee Structure & Scholarships: Applicable for academic year 2025-26; detailed break-up available on request (separate rates for Asian & African countries and the rest of the world).

Fee Structure (Academic Year 2025-26) – detailed break-up available on request.

Asian & African Countries | Rest of the World

Bennett University Admission Scholarships  
- Awarded for the first year of the Undergraduate programme.  
- Continuation in later years requires:  
  – CGPA ≥ 8/10  
  – No backlog  
  – No disciplinary record  

Scholarship slabs for NRI & International Students  
Middle-East & Western Countries  

Based on Class XII (Best of 3) or IB Score:  
- 10 % tuition waiver: 60 – 74 % or IB 26 – 30  
- 20 % tuition waiver: 75 – 84 % or IB 31 – 35  
- 30 % tuition waiver: 85 % & above or IB 36 & above  

Disclaimer: Scholarships are granted solely at the discretion of the Admission and Scholarship Committee; the policy may be amended without notice.

Obtaining AIU Equivalence Certificate  
Students must submit to the Association of Indian Universities (AIU):  
1. Degree/Certificate plus year-wise academic transcript authenticated by the Indian Embassy abroad or the relevant foreign mission in India.  
2. Accreditation status of the last attended University/Institute.  
3. Copies of academic certificates from Higher Secondary onwards (where required).  

Payment: Service charge via Demand Draft favouring “Association of Indian Universities, New Delhi”.  

Responsibility: Certification for equivalence rests with the candidate; applications are processed only after the Equivalence Certificate is received.  

Full details: https://www.aiu.ac.in/

Conditions to qualify for NRI/International Students Quota: NRI/PIO/OCI

Conditions to qualify for NRI/International Students Quota

NRI/PIO/OCI

PIO (Person of Indian Origin) – A person who or whose any ancestor was an Indian national and who presently holds another country’s citizenship/nationality (i.e., holds a foreign passport).

OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) – A person registered as an “Overseas Citizen of India” cardholder under section 7A of the Citizenship Act, 1955.

NRI – A student whose mother or father, first-degree relative (real brother or sister), or grandparents (both paternal and maternal) are NRIs.  
NRI here means an Indian citizen who stays abroad for employment, business, vocation, or under circumstances indicating an intention to stay abroad for an uncertain duration.

Students who have completed 10+2 from foreign boards and fall under any of the above categories (i)–(v) will be treated under the NRI category for admission.

All foreign passport holders who have passed 10+2/graduation from a foreign board/university must obtain a 10+2/graduation equivalence certificate (including subject-wise percentage of marks) from AIU on or before registration.

Seamless student support & services

Bennett University provides specialised support and services to international aspirants, ensuring seamless communication at every step—from pre- to post-admission.

Pre-Arrival Service  
Induction Program  
English Language Support  
Zero Tolerance Policy  
Student Support & Services  

Even before deciding to join, prospective students receive assistance from our counsellors to:

- Decide on the program  
- Complete the application process  
- Receive guidance through the admissions process  
- Stay updated on the registration schedule

Deciding the program  
Completing the application process  
Hand-holding through admissions  
Updating with the registration schedule  
Introduction to School/s and Department/s  

During the initial days students meet fellow students and are introduced to:  
- Academics  
- Research & consulting facilities  
- Library, IT & Admin services  
- Student Council & Student-led Clubs  
- Career Services, Placements & Internship Opportunities  
- Health & Wellness facilities  
- Sports Facilities  

English-language support  
Students coming from universities where English is not the first language can join English-language programmes run by the Department of Student Affairs.

Safe & inclusive campus  
Bennett University has a zero-tolerance policy: ragging, sexual harassment, public display of affection, violence, racism and drug abuse are strictly prohibited.

International-student support  
Specialised services guide international aspirants through every step, from pre- to post-admission.

Spark The Student E-cell

Whatever steps an academic institution may take towards entrepreneurship development, nothing beats the charm and impact of a student-led Entrepreneurship Cell on campus. SPARK is a notable example of such a Cell. Reasons for SPARK’s popularity are not hard to imagine; from the highly exciting real-life entrepreneur story sessions to off-campus start-up visits, there is no dull moment at SPARK. Apparently, SPARK core team has been smart in choosing entrepreneurs and enterprises of the right kind. Such talks and visits add tremendous value to the classroom inputs.

Event by Spark
E-summit NEC competition
Bennett E-cell participated in the E-Summit NEC Competition at IIT Bombay to promote entrepreneurship and innovation.

E-Summit NEC Competition – IIT Bombay  
Organiser: Spark  
Bennett E-cell competed in this entrepreneurship & innovation contest. Activities: competition presentations, industry networking, workshops. Result: 2nd runner-up. Take-away: teamwork, resilience, innovative thinking; new avenues for future ventures.

Babson Global Student Challenge – Ideathon  
Focus: entrepreneurship & innovation. Students pitched business concepts to judges, received feedback and networked. Prizes rewarded creativity & problem-solving. Goal: address UNSDGs, catalyse learning, foster purpose-driven entrepreneurial solutions for society.

Karobaar Carnival – 3-day fest by Sparks  
Day 1: Clubs Crossroads – club idea pitches. Other highlights: Logo Showdown, interactive session with Khalid Wani. Features: diverse stalls, unique products, networking, celebration of entrepreneurship & creativity.

Startup Mahakumbh – Pragati Maidan, Delhi  
Premier congregation for startups & innovation. Startups showcased ideas, networked with professionals, absorbed ecosystem insights. Experts ran workshops/seminars on entrepreneurship, tech, business development. Pitch opportunities to investors & customers boosted visibility & growth prospects.

Start-ups seized the chance to pitch to investors and customers, boosting their visibility and growth prospects. Keynotes and talks from successful entrepreneurs inspired participants to pursue their entrepreneurial ambitions with zeal.

About Bennovate

Key Takeaways

Greater Noida | 14th - 15th November

Register Now

What is Bennovate 2025

Join E-Summit ’25 at Bennett University – Where Innovation Meets Entrepreneurship

Bennett Hatchery, under the Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship, Bennett University, proudly presents "Bennovate 4.0" – a prestigious Entrepreneurship Summit on 14th and 15th November 2025. Supported by Startup India, DPIIT, Government of India, StartinUP (UP Government) and WASME (World Association of Small and Medium Enterprises), the summit will feature a range of dynamic events tailored for young start-ups, school innovators, aspiring entrepreneurs, government officials, corporate leaders, investors, and academia.

Be part of a platform where ideas transform into impact.

Spark Tank

'SparkTank' is Bennett University’s novel B-plan competition where start-ups and school students will get an opportunity to pitch their business plans and innovative ideas to top investors/VCs.

B-plan Competition Apply Now

prize

Why Should You Be Part of Bennovate 4.0?

It offers networking opportunities in the competitive and challenging start-up ecosystem.

It facilitates knowledge exchange as you interact with industry leaders.

The E-summit promotes a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship, deepening the understanding of entrepreneurship in the nation.

It provides an ideal stage for budding entrepreneurs to connect with esteemed mentors and potentially secure their first seed round.

The event offers an opportunity to secure funding and gain mentorship from renowned investors.

Spark Tank Winners at Bennovate 4.O

1st Infyn Technologies (₹ 1,00,000)

2nd Newrup Tech Solutions (₹ 75,000)

3rd Mistrijee Technologies (₹ 50,000)

Key Takeaways

Experience the latest innovations/innovative startups





Gain a detailed understanding of startup ecosystem

Opportunity to secure funding and receive mentorship from renowned investors from reputed industry and R&D organizations





Prospects to network with VCs, academicians, and researchers

Participation certificates to all the registered participants





Opportunity to meet government officials from start-up ecosystem

Bennett University > Faculties > Prof. (Dr.) Abhay Bansal

Prof. (Dr.) Abhay Bansal

Prof. (Dr.) Abhay Bansal

Dean, School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology & Dean, International Affairs and Corporate Outreach

SchoolSchool of Computer Science Engineering and Technology

Emailabhay.bansal@bennett.edu.in

Prof. (Dr.) Abhay Bansal is the Dean, School of Computer Science Engineering & Technology and Dean, International Relations & Corporate Outreach at Bennett University, The Times Group. He holds a BE (Computer), ME (IT), MBA, PhD from the University of Rajasthan and completed his Post-Doctoral Research at the University of Florida, USA.

With an illustrious career spanning 31 years in both industry and academia, Dr. Bansal has made remarkable contributions in IT Industry, research and education. He has authored over 140+ Scopus indexed research papers, including 40+ papers in SCI-indexed high impact journals. His research contributions extend to Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Supply Chains, Cloud computing, and emerging technologies, significantly impacting the global academic landscape. Under his guidance, 10 PhD scholars have successfully completed their research.

Dr. Bansal has actively collaborated on academic and research projects with top global universities, including Stanford University, MIT, Cambridge University, Monash University, University of South Florida, University of Queensland, and Drexel University.

In addition to his academic contributions, Dr. Bansal is an AWS Certified Solutions Architect & Cloud Practitioner, a Microsoft Certified Professional, and an authorized AWS trainer, having trained over 2000+ students in the past five years. His expertise in cloud computing and emerging technologies has helped shape the careers of aspiring professionals.

Dr. Bansal has played a key role in professional organizations, serving in leadership positions within IEEE UP Section, Computer Society of India, Institution of Engineers. Organized more than 20 IEEE Sponsored International Conferences and acting as a reviewer for prestigious journals. His dedication to fostering technological innovation and academic excellence has earned him global recognition.

With a vision to create a robust learning environment and contribute to the Indian education system, Dr. Bansal has visited the world’s most prestigious institutions, including MIT, Stanford University, Cornell University, Harvard University, Cambridge University, Oxford University, Imperial College London, University of Melbourne, Australian National University, University of Canberra, York University etc. These visits have strengthened international academic collaborations and brought best practices to Indian education.

A distinguished Fellow IET(UK), IETE, IEI and Senior Member of various professional bodies, including IEEE (USA), ACM, IETE, CSI.

Dr. Bansal has received numerous prestigious awards. Some of his notable accolades include the Best Citizen of India Award (2012), Lifetime Achievement Award (2020), Global Eminent Researcher Award (2021), and International Scientist Award on Engineering (2021).

Dr. Bansal has travelled to 25+ countries, actively engaging in research, academic partnerships, and global education initiatives, contributing significantly to the advancement of science and technology worldwide. His dedication to academic excellence, research, and innovation continues to inspire students and professionals globally.

Our Leadership

Please select

Chancellor

Pro Chancellor

Vice Chancellor

Chief Operating Officer

Dean of School’s

Head-IQAC & Dean Academic Affairs

Controller of Examination

Chief Financial Officer

Dean R&D

Head-CIE

VP & Head HR

VP Admissions

VP Placements

Prof. (Dr.) Alka Munjal

Head IQAC, Dean Academic Affairs Bennett University

Dr. Munjal is a distinguished academician and strategic leader with over 35 years of experience spanning hicher education, academic administration, and consultancy. She brings her an exceptional record of achievements in institution building, quality assurance. policy development, and academic governance.

Prior to joining Bennett University, Dr. Munjal served as Director of the Apeejay School of Management, Delhi, and held senior leadership positions for over two decades at Amity University Uttar Pradesh, including Dean — Student Academic Affairs & Support Services,

Director — Academics, and Professor — Finance & Strategic Management. She v.as also the Founder Director of Amity School of Business and has previously served at Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra.

A Ph.D. in Strategy and Finance from Jamia Millia Islamia, Dr. Munjal has consistently contributed to curriculum innovation. NEP-aligned reforms, NAAC/NBA accreditation leadership, and has served on quality assurance committees including AACSB, QAA. and WASC. Her scholarly output includes numerous research papers, books, and case studies in the domains of strategy, finance, corporate governance, CSR, and sustainability.

She has been invited as a panelist, keynote speaker, and MDP facilitator at leading institutions and public sector organizations including NT PC, BHEL, ONGC, SAIL, GAIL, Indian Airlines, and the Ministry of Defence. Her leadership in academic planning, digital learning enablement, and learnercentric systems has been widely recognized.

As Dean — Academic Affairs at Bennett University, Dr. Munjal will provide strategic direction to academic planning, institutional quality enhancement, and student-centric program delivery, while strengthening the university’s academic ecosystem in line with global standards and national frameworks.

email :alka.munjal@bennett.edu.in

Our Leadership

VP Admissions

VP Placements

Dr. Vineet Dahiya

Controller of Examinations, Bennett University

Dr. Dahiya is a seasoned academic and administrative professional with over 16 years of experience in reputed universities, where he has successfully led examination operations, academic governance, and institutional process automation. He holds a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from Manav Rachna International Institute of Research and Studies, an MTech in Power Systems and Drives from YMCA College of Engineering, Faridabad, and B.E. in Electrical Engineering from MD University, Rohtak.

Prior to joining Bennett University, Dr. Dahiya served as Controller of Examinations at K.R. Mangalam

University, Gurugram. He has also held several key academic and administrative positions, including Deputy Controller of Examinations, Academic Coordinator and Associate Professor at institutions such as Amity University, KIIT College of Engineering, and Manav Rachna International University.

Throughout his career, he has successfully overseen examination operations, ensuring strict adherence to academic regulations and institutional policies. He has played a key role in implementing technology-driven systems to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and transparency within examination and academic processes. Known for his professionalism and attention to detail, Dr. Dahiya excels in coordinating with faculty, maintaining confidentiality, and streamlining workflows. His expertise and leadership align strongly with the responsibilities of managing and improving academic and examination functions in higher education institutions/Universities. He has played a pivotal role in implementing digital transformation initiatives in examination and evaluation systems, including online and hybrid assessment platforms, digital evaluation frameworks and ERP-integrated workflows. His initiatives have ensured timely result declaration, enhanced transparency and improved operational efficiency across academic processes. An advocate of academic integrity and NEP-aligned reforms, he has successfully introduced flexible assessment systems, credit-based evaluations and data-driven result analytics. His leadership in deploying National Academic Depository (NAD) integration and AI-driven automation tools has contributed to institutional excellence and compliance with national standards.

Dr. Dahiya has authored and co-authored numerous papers in IEEE, Springer and Elsevier-indexed journals with contributions in renewable energy systems, artificial intelligence applications and sustainable engineering.

email : coe@bennett.edu.in

VP Placements

Mr. S Chandramouli

Chief Financial Officer, Bennett University

Mr. S. Chandramouli is a Chartered Accountant with over 25 years of post-qualification experience, bringing deep expertise in finance, management, and strategic planning. Throughout his distinguished career, he has led diverse financial functions with a strong command over accounting, taxation, audits, project accounting, and cost control across multiple sectors.

A seasoned finance professional, Mr. Chandramouli has consistently demonstrated excellence in budgeting, business planning, and implementing job

and process costing frameworks for various manufacturing setups. His core competencies extend to corporate finance, working capital management, project financing, fund allocation, and credit rating processes. He is adept at stakeholder engagement, working closely with auditors, statutory bodies, legal authorities, and internal leadership teams, and has conducted numerous business review and strategic planning meetings.

Over the years, Mr. Chandramouli has grown from a Finance Executive to the role of Chief Financial Officer, showcasing strong leadership, cross-functional collaboration, and operational efficiency. His versatile industry experience spans merchant banking, IT and ITeS, engineering, chemicals manufacturing, industrial catering, and higher education. Before joining Bennett University, he served as CFO for the SRM Group in Chennai.

With his extensive experience, strategic insight, and operational acumen, Mr. Chandramouli plays a vital role in steering the financial strategy and institutional growth of Bennett University.

Mr. Senthil Kumar

Chief Operating Officer, Bennett University

Mr. Senthil Kumar is a highly experienced professional with over 24 years of expertise in strategic negotiations, sales, business planning, and development. His specialization includes business development, institutional building, marketing management, partnerships, networking, education management, international alliances, project management, and CRM.

Currently the Chief Operating Officer (COO) at Bennett University, Mr. Senthil was previously the Chief Growth Officer at VMRF Deemed University in Tamil Nadu, where he managed strategic alliances, international branding, communications, admissions, outreach, and industry-academia collaborations. He also served as Senior Director at Global University System (GUS), focusing on business development and growth, strategic planning, and international sales for UPES and Pearl Academy, maintaining key relationships with institutional partners.

Mr. Senthil has worked with several prestigious institutions, including SRM University Group, OP Jindal Global University, and firms like Rainmaker and Career Launcher. Academically, he holds a Master of Computer Applications from Bharathidasan University and is pursuing a PhD in management from Gandhinagar National Law University (2020-24).

email : senthil.kumar@bennett.edu.in

VP Placements

Dr. Raj Singh

Vice Chancellor, Bennett University

Dr. Raj Singh brings over 15 years of distinguished academic leadership experience, having served as Vice Chancellor at multiple reputed universities across India. His career is marked by an unwavering commitment to academic excellence, institutional advancement, and student-centered innovation.

Dr. Singh began his career in the Indian Air Force, where he served for over a decade (1981–1994), before transitioning to the field of higher education. Dr. Singh holds a master’s degree in economics, a CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) designation, and a

PhD in Strategic Finance. His professional journey has been shaped by diverse engagements as a teacher, researcher, consultant, trainer, and academic administrator.

He has served as Vice Chancellor at institutions such as JAIN (Deemed-to-be University), GD Goenka University, and Amity University in Jaipur and Gwalior. Prior to these roles, he was the Pro-Vice Chancellor at Amity University Noida and the founding Head of ICFAI Business School (IBS), New Delhi.

He has served on academic advisory boards of institutions such as IIT Delhi and Delhi School of Economics, and chaired expert committees of statutory bodies such as NAAC and NBA. He has trained over 6,000 educators across school and higher education sectors, and has contributed to policy and quality initiatives through his roles with the Quality Council of India, ASSOCHAM, and the Ministry of Sports and Youth Affairs.

Renowned as an educationist and policy contributor, Dr. Singh has played a pivotal role in shaping academic governance frameworks and implementing National Education Policy (NEP)-aligned educational models. His contributions to faculty development, research integration, and industry-academia partnerships have earned wide recognition across the education sector.

In his current role, he is steering Bennett University toward delivering high-quality, future-ready education while nurturing a culture of academic rigor, innovation, and ethical leadership. Dr. Singh brings to this role a legacy of academic leadership, strategic vision, and institution building, shaped by over decades of diverse experience across higher education and the Indian Air Force.

email : raj.singh@bennett.edu.in

Ms. Revati Jain

Pro Chancellor, Bennett University

Ms. Revati Jain is a Director of Bennett Institute of Higher Education (BIHE) which is the sponsoring body of Bennett University. In her capacity as an Executive council member of the University, she provides guidance to the operating team of the University on key strategic initiatives besides helping the University adopt global best practices in the Higher Education space.

She is also an Executive Director on the Board of Bennett, Coleman & Co Limited (BCCL) since 2017 and a member of the CSR Committee of the Board. BCCL

is by far, India’s largest media conglomerate which publishes India’s largest circulating English dailies, besides various regional newspaper publications. The Company and its subsidiaries also operate some of India’s most well known and iconic brands like Radio Mirchi, Gaana, Times Now, ET Now, ET Money, Femina and Filmfare across business segments like radio, television, digital, internet and magazines.

In addition to being involved in the higher education foray of the Group, she was previously associated with “The Times Foundation” as a Governing Council member, where she actively redefined its overall purpose and operations in close coordination with the late Chairperson, Ms. Indu Jain. She is now actively associated with the “The Times of India Relief Fund”, “Times Research Foundation” and the “Times Eye Research Foundation” as a Governing Council member besides being on the Board of the All-India Heart Foundation which operates the National Heart Institute in Delhi.

Revati Jain, who graduated from Jesus & Mary College, New Delhi in 1986, is a multi-faceted personality, straddling diverse roles of an Entrepreneur, Spiritualist, Social Citizen, fitness enthusiast, Mother and Women’s Empowerment advocate with equal aplomb. She was the Chairperson of ASSOCHAM’s Women’s Foundation in 2016-17.

email : pro-chancellor@bennett.edu.in

Our Leadership

Mr. Vineet Jain

Chancellor, Bennett University & MD, Times Group

Mr. Vineet Jain  
Chancellor, Bennett University & Managing Director, Times Group (Bennett, Coleman & Co. Ltd.)

Mr. Vineet Jain is the Managing Director of India’s largest media group, Bennett, Coleman & Co. Ltd, also known as the Times Group. He has transformed the Times Group from the No. 1 publishing house into India’s largest, diversified, most respected and multi-faceted media conglomerate. As Bennett, Coleman’s strategist, marketing visionary and editorial architect, he has spearheaded the Group’s highly successful forays into the internet, radio, broadcast television and outdoor media. He is also responsible for revamping the Group’s stable of daily newspapers into youthful, contemporary and cutting-edge brands with market leadership in most parts of India.

Apart from taking the Group’s flagship English daily,

The Times of India and its business daily The Economic Times to No. 1 position across markets in the country, Mr. Jain has widened the scope and reach of print into the metro news and lifestyle space with brands such as Mumbai Mirror, Bombay Times, Delhi Times etc., apart from forays into regional languages across the country. He has also transformed the flagship events of the company — Filmfare Awards, Femina Miss India, and The Economic Times Awards for Corporate Excellence — into larger-than-life ground and television properties that are the toast of India’s crème de la crème. The newest offering is TOIFA or the Times of India Film Awards, which was first held in Vancouver, Canada, in 2013. His biggest contribution is to drive the Times Group aggressively into new media — internet, radio and television. In a short span of three years, Radio Mirchi and Times Now are No 1 brand in their respective domains. With the launch of ET Now in 2009 — the Times Group’s business channel, Movies Now – the English Movie channel, ZOOM – the Bollywood News channel, and the recent Romedy Now — the broadcast operations of the group have attained critical mass. Mr. Jain is also a director on the board of the Press Trust of India Ltd. and many of the Times Group companies. Mr. Jain is the recipient of the prestigious Rajiv Gandhi Award for Corporate Excellence and Indian Telly Award for his contribution to Indian TV Broadcasting Industry in 2009.

He was also adjudged IMPACT Person of the Year 2013 for his industry-shaping initiatives that have redefined media consumption in the digital age, setting a benchmark with young and vibrant media products. He is also recipient of Entrepreneur of the Year Award 2013 from Bombay Management Association in recognition of The Times Group’s successful diversification from print to other forms of media – television, internet & radio. He also won Media Person of the Year award from International Advertising Association (IAA) in 2015. Mr. Jain is strongly committed to delivering value through customer delight in all group companies. Mr. Jain also enjoys travelling and sports like skiing and tennis.

email : Chancellor@bennett.edu.in

VP Admissions

VP Placements

Dr. Rakesh K Singh

VP Placements, Bennett University

Dr. Rakesh K Singh brings over two decades of robust executive leadership experience across diverse sectors including Banking/Financial Services, FMCG, Consumer Durables, and Higher Education. Currently serving as the Vice President & Head of Corporate Relations at Bennett University, Dr. Singh has held pivotal roles in esteemed organizations.

Prior to his tenure at Bennett University, Dr. Singh held the position of Director of Marketing & Corporate Resource Centre at two Prominent Universities in India.

Prior to his tenure at Bennett University, Dr. Singh held the position of Director of Marketing & Corporate Resource Centre at two Prominent Universities in India.

With a solid academic foundation, Dr. Singh holds a Ph.D., an MSc in Chemistry, and an MBA with Specialization in Marketing. His academic prowess and extensive industry experience position him as a thought leader in his field.

Throughout his career, Dr. Singh has made significant contributions to various sectors in Corporate, including large MNC’s, where he served in key capacities at organizations such as L&T, Killick Nixon Ltd., ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Ltd., Bajaj Allianz General Insurance Ltd. , and Bharti AXA Life Insurance Co Ltd.

Driven by a passion for excellence and a commitment to fostering strong industry-academia partnerships, Dr. Singh continues to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of corporate relations at Bennett University. His strategic vision and leadership skills make him a valuable asset to the organization, driving innovation and growth in the ever-evolving landscape of higher education.

email : placements@bennett.edu.in

Bennett University > About Us > Industry Advisory Board

Industry Advisory Board

The Industry Advisory Board at Bennett University comprises distinguished leaders from global industries who provide strategic guidance to enhance the university’s impact as a premier teaching, research, and experience-led institution. The Board advises leadership on priorities and supports the advancement of world-class programs across Engineering, Management, Law, Media, AI, Liberal Arts, Design, and Applied Sciences.

Industry Advisory Board Members

Mr. Anurag BanaSenior Legal Council

International Bar Association

View More

Mr. Anurag Bana

ORGANIZATION: International Bar Association

Designation: Senior Legal Council

Mr. Anurag BanaSenior Legal Council

International Bar Association

View More

Mr. Anurag Bana

ORGANIZATION: International Bar Association

Designation: Senior Legal Council

Anurag Bana is an Indian qualified lawyer with a BCL from the University of Oxford and over 19 years of experience, as a legal advisor, practitioner and researcher having worked across global jurisdictions, in the arena of International law and policy sector. His work trajectory spans a judicial clerkship with a Supreme Court of India Judge, internships at international organizations including the United Nations Commission on International Trade Law and the Permanent Bureau of the Hague Conference on Private International Law. He has practiced in Indian law courts and worked briefly in the corporate sector with Skoda Auto in Czech Republic.

Anurag is an influencer and thought leader in the emerging areas of legaltech, disruptive innovation, internet of things, digital assets and attorney-client privilege and confidentiality issues. He has published and presented several specialist papers on technology law related issues covering cyber security, blockchain technology, digital identity and social media laws

As the Chair of the IBA Social Media Working Group, he led the development and adoption of the international social media principles for the legal profession by the IBA in 2014. He is the IBA representative at the UK Attorney General’s International Pro Bono Committee; a fellow of the European Law Institute, a founding member of the Chinese European Legal Association; a member of the Advisory Group on Diversity and Inclusion of the Society for Computers and Law and; life member of the Oxford Union Society.

Anurag is in the expert panels of the following organizations:

United Nations Centre for Trade Facilitation and Electronic Business (UN/CEFACT);

United Nations Commission on International Trade Law Working Group IV (Electronic Commerce) Identity Management & Trust Services;

United Nations Commission on International Trade Law Working Group IV (Electronic Commerce) Identity Management & Trust Services;

United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (Judicial Integrity Initiative) Expert Group on Social Media Use by Judges;

Hague Conference on Private International Law Experts’ Group on Use of Video-link and Modern Technologies in the Taking of Evidence Abroad;

Commonwealth Virtual Currencies Expert Working Group;

Europol Platform for Experts – International Legal Policy Expert

Mr. Haigreve KhaitanSenior Partner

Khaitan and Company

View More

Mr. Haigreve Khaitan

ORGANIZATION: Khaitan and Company

Designation: Senior Partner

Haigreve is a Senior Partner and heads the Corporate/M&A and Private Equity practice at the Firm. He is an elected member of the National Executive Committee of the Firm which is responsible for the firm’s strategic growth and development. He advises companies, boards of directors and financial institutions on a wide range of corporate matters, including mergers and acquisitions, private equity investments, corporate governance, corporate restructuring and other corporate and securities laws matters.

He has been involved in some of the most high-profile and complex M&A and private equity transactions in India and is sought after for his expertise by some of the most prominent Indian promoters, Indian and international companies, Indian regulatory agencies as well as some of the largest global funds investing in India. He advises a range of large Indian conglomerates and multinational clients in various business sectors, including financial services, manufacturing, retail, infrastructure, steel, software and information technology.

Haigreve has been consistently recommended by many of the world’s leading law chambers and accreditation bodies. Sources also praise him as an “outstanding lawyer” who is “extremely good and very sharp when it comes to large transactions’’. He has been acknowledged for his experience and expertise by:

Asian Legal Business amongst the top 15 M&A lawyers in Asia.

Chambers and Partners as a ‘’Star Individual’’ for M&A, ‘’Eminent Practitioner’’ for Private Equity and “Band 1 Lawyer” for Private Wealth Law.

The Legal 500 as a ‘’Leading Expert’’ for Corporate and M&A;

IFLR 1000 as a ‘’Market Leading Lawyer” for Corporate M&A; and Banking & Finance.

India Business Law Journal amongst top 100 lawyers in India.

He was responsible for Pleon’s UK operations, and headed the corporate and technology B2B practices. As president of ICCO, Nitin is making the global PR industry relevant to different markets, and potential talent. He regularly speaks at the AMEC Global Summit, ICCO Global Summit and Sabre Awards. Nitin teaches and charts course syllabus at prominent mass communication colleges in India to increase awareness about the profession at the grass-root level.

He has been recognized on several platforms for his contribution to the industry including the Individual Achievement award at the Sabre APAC in 2021 and the ‘Global PR Leader of the Year’ at the 2015 ICCO Global Awards.

Nitin lives in New Delhi with his wife and two children.

Mr. Vineet Vij

A business leader who provides tactical & practical vision that is vital in corporate decision making while preserving overall legal health of Tech Mahindra & group companies worldwide

Role description

Role description

Vineet is currently serving as a Global General Counsel of Tech Mahindra Limited, a US$ 5.3 Billion Indian MNC providing IT, ITeS, Consulting & BPO Services to clients worldwide. In capacity as the corporate officer in-charge of the Legal function, he is responsible for the handling entire Legal & Regulatory affairs of Tech Mahindra and its group & portfolio companies worldwide and advising & supporting multi-billion cross-border Commercial transactions, Mergers & Acquisitions, Disputes & Litigations, Ethics & Global Compliance, Legal Policy & Regulatory Affairs, IPR & strategic legal support.

Areas of expertise

His work experience of over 25 years spans across wide variety of practice areas including Commercial & Contract laws, Litigation & Dispute Resolution, Cross-border Mergers & Acquisitions, Global Regulatory Compliances, Financial & Banking, Real Estate and SEZ & STPI regulations, Company law, IT, Data Protection, Privacy, Cyber & IPR laws, Criminal law, Telecom, Litigation & Arbitration, Employment laws, Bankruptcy laws, Competition laws & Corporate affairs and Indirect Taxation laws.

Professional experience

Professional experience

After working as practicing lawyer in Delhi High Court and the district courts in the initial years, Vineet embarked on the journey of becoming an in-house counsel with Punj Lloyd Group in a secretarial-cum-legal role, which exposed him to various facets of the law that eventually formed a significant part of his responsibilities. This was followed with an 8 years’ stint with the TATA Group (Tata Unisys, Tata Infotech, TCS) and working closely with Tata Sons and Tata Services, which further provided exposure to global deals & transactions. At American Express, working on complex legal challenges he got the opportunity to solve regulatory, legal & compliance issues in the banking, finance sector and NBFC sector. Vineet has also worked with HCL Technologies for over a decade and his path at HCL was punctuated by his significant role in handling billion-dollar contracts, complex litigations & arbitrations, establishing SEZ/STPI/Free-trade Zones, ensuring Global Compliances, resolving Customs & Indirect Taxation issues, filing of Patents, Copyright, Trademarks & resolving IPR-related issues, handling public affairs functions and strategic legal assistance for HCL Corporation and Foundation. He provided active legal & regulatory support in HCL’s entry in the software business segment - the biggest IP acquisition the Indian IT/ITeS space has ever seen.

Currently, as the Group General Counsel for Tech Mahindra and its group companies the across globe, he leads a team of 100 lawyers who are located in different parts of the world and are taking care of the legal & regulatory aspects concerning the Tech Mahindra Group & portfolio companies and Tech Mahindra Foundation & its CSR activities. Vineet acts as a bulwark against legal challenges and roadblocks faced by the group to pave way for exponential business growth in tandem with risk mitigation and compliance with country law & legal obligations.

Education and Memberships

Education and Memberships

Vineet graduated with a B.Com (Hons) degree from prestigious Shri Ram College of Commerce and L.L.B. from Delhi University. He holds additional qualification & degrees in Company Law, Labour Laws, Industrial Relations & Personnel Management, IT & Cyber Laws. With the wide-ranging areas of practice, he also serves on the Board of Studies and Advisory Boards of leading law school/universities, editorial board of legal journal & magazines and member of various trade associations and advocacy groups such as CII, FICCI, CII, ASSOCHAM and INBA.

Pro bono work

Vineet believes that his role as a Group General Counsel is not only restricted to handling legal affairs for the Tech Mahindra Group, but also extends towards lending a hand to alleviate social imperils. Noting the lack of access to legal aid and resources among marginalized communities, he places great emphasis on providing legal advice and ensuring access to justice for such people.

As the Group General Counsel for Tech Mahindra, Vineet feels that it is an important responsibility for the entire team to contribute towards the overall organizational goal of undertaking CSR activities. He has fully espoused the company’s CSR policy and works tirelessly to make the CSR activities a success and a longstanding initiative. Atos is a leader in digital transformation with circa 120,000 employees in 72 countries and annual revenue of € 12.2 Billion.

Current Roles:

SVP, Head of HR for Atos in India

+ Head of HR Global Delivery Centres Worldwide (India, Poland, Philippines, Malaysia, Romania, Bulgaria...)

Additional Responsibilities:

+ Manages Global HR Budget

+ Member of Board of Directors for Atos entities in India

+ Member of India Governance Council

Previous Organizations and Roles:

Director (M&A), Human Resources (Asia Pacific and MEA)

Mr. Yogi Sriram Advisor to CEO & MD, Group Human Resources

Larsen & Toubro Limited

View More

Mr. Yogi Sriram

ORGANIZATION: Larsen & Toubro Limited

Designation: Advisor to CEO & MD, Group Human Resources

HR leader with over 44 years of experience in multiple HR functions across diverse industries. My goal is now to contribute to industry with my experience in leading HR for over 4 decades for large companies and more particularly after leading the HR function of India's most successful and large engineering and construction conglomerate L&T. At L&T, I have contributed by conceptualizing & implementing a Performance Management System called FAIR, developing a high potential programme with over 19000 assessments of leaders in 2 decades and several other first of the kind HR Policies, particularly in Leadership Development & Talent Management

Alumni of TISS and FMS Delhi, Sriram has worked in leadership positions with organizations such as L&T, Taj, British Petroleum etc.

Robert Bosch Chassis systems India Pvt Ltd

Mr. Avinash Chintawar

ORGANIZATION: Robert Bosch Chassis systems India Pvt Ltd

Designation: Managing Director

Experienced Managing Director with a dedicated 35years of working with biggest automotive supplier - Robert Bosch. He has Vast experience at Various divisions of Bosch in India. Strong international experience dealing with leading a large manufacturing division in Germany in high precision fuel injection products, international production network and supply chain. He was part of the core team in winning a EFQM excellence award in Germany and CII Business excellence award in India.

He has handled many international projects including setting up of India's largest export Oriented unit (EOU) and product relocations to India for export market.

He is a language enthusiast, with a good skills in German language and well versed with many Indian languages.

Mr. Rajat Mathur Managing Director

Morgan Stanley, India

View More

Mr. Rajat Mathur

ORGANIZATION: Morgan Stanley, India

Designation: Managing Director

Rajat Mathur is a Managing Director of Human Resources and is currently the Head of HR for Morgan Stanley, India. Rajat has over 25 years of experience in Human Resources with the last 15 years being at the helm of the function both locally and overseas. Rajat has been with Morgan Stanley for 10+ years and has served as Asia Head of Talent for Morgan Stanley in addition to his current role.

Previously he was Director of Human Resources for Deutsche Bank- Global Offshoring Centres. His experience also includes running his own Consulting and Adventure Company focused on developing Life skills in the youth community. When he is not climbing mountains, he also writes books, having already published a book called “Dear Dad” and been a contributing author for the book “A Wonderful Boss”.

Mr. Rajendra Mehta

ORGANIZATION: Suzlon Limited.

Designation: President and CHRO

Comprehensive experience of 27 years in developing and executing strategic Human Resources agenda of organisations. Most of these agenda are driven for profitable growth of business. Extensive exposure to large corporations with business spread across geographies, in India and overseas as well.

Coached by Dr Ram Charan have been recognized for expertise in implementing world-class practices to transform organizations into high-performing and profitable entities. Coordinate with global management consultancies to develop productivity enhancement programs to yield superlative business results. A licensed practitioner of Thomas Profiling System and an expert in implementing Gallup, Leadership Circle and GPTW frameworks, I identify and hire leadership talent across generic and niche skills. Ensure sustained development of employees to build organisational capabilities as well as to retain and promote talent.

Mr. Vynsley Fernandes Managing Director and CEO

NXT Digital (Hinduja Group)

View More

Mr. Vynsley Fernandes

ORGANIZATION: NXT Digital (Hinduja Group)

Designation: Managing Director and CEO

Vynsley Fernandes is a veteran media executive and a leading technocrat, with three decades of experience in delivering and managing assignments globally - for DTH (Direct-to-Home) & HITS (Headend-In-The-Sky) platforms; Internet Service Providers, digital cable networks; and news & entertainment channels. Prior to his current role at Hinduja Global Solutions, part of the global Hinduja Group; he has managed high-profile assignments at blue-chip organisations including 21st Century Fox (News Corporation), STAR Television, TATA SKY and INX Network. With an academic background in Mass Communications and Media, Vynsley is considered a thought leader in the media & entertainment space. He continues to represent the industry at various international and national forums and has several accolades to his credit including being recognized with the prestigious “CEO of the Year” award presented by World Leadership Congress in 2021.

Mr. Rishikesh Raval President - Group Human Resources & Corporate Communications

Zydus Group

View More

Mr. Rishikesh Raval

ORGANIZATION: Zydus Group

Designation: President - Group Human Resources & Corporate Communications

Rishikesh has had an incredible impact in the area of human behaviour, organization building and cultural transformation in India with leading MNCs and large Indian Business organizations . For more than two decades, he has immersed himself in the practice of People Processes, Organization Development, and Change Management. Applauded for his work in the area of Programmed Change Management and facilitation of multiple projects around cultural transformation, he has endured to design the decisive 'predictable organizational architecture' while preserving result driven distinctiveness of the enterprise.

In earlier years of career, Rishikesh worked with Sarabhai Group, Glaxo India, Pfizer Ltd. in Human Resources function for Manufacturing vertical.

Mr. Avinash Gupta Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer

Dun & Bradstreet

View More

Mr. Avinash Gupta

ORGANIZATION: Dun & Bradstreet

Designation: Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer

Avinash is a highly experienced, execution-focused senior leader with a strong, global background and brings more than 3 decades of experience and a vast network of business relationships. Avinash has worked extensively in financial services including M&A, equity and debt financing, private equity placement and advisory across the EMENA and Asia.

Prior to joining Dun & Bradstreet in 2021, Avinash worked with Rabo Equity Advisors where he was the Managing Director since 2018. Over the years, he has held various leadership positions including recently as Head of Deloitte India's Financial Advisory Services business. He has worked in senior positions across geographies and products in various institutions such as HSBC, UBS, Barings, Citibank, Falcon Capital, and Alpen Capital. In his initial years in the USA, he also worked as an equity analyst covering the Oil Services sector at Wedbush Morgan Securities (Los Angeles) and Howard Weil (New Orleans).

Avinash has an MBA from the A.B. Freeman School of Business, Tulane University (Deans List with full fellowship) and a B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology, BHU Varanasi.

Avinash sits on the National Executive Committees of Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FICCI) and Associated Chambers of Commerce and Industry of India (ASSOCHAM).

Being one of the best private universities in India, Bennett University offers diverse programmes that are supported by distinguished faculties, possessing academia-industry experience and exposure. The undergraduate, postgraduate and certification level programmes give an edge to aspirants to pave the future path on their desired field after pursuing it from the best UGC approved university for professional programmes.

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence

Shaping tomorrow’s Innovators

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence

Why B.Tech AI from Bennett University

Shaping tomorrow’s Innovators

Apply Now

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence

Shaping tomorrow’s Innovators

Apply Now

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence

Why B.Tech AI from Bennett University

B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence nurtures the next generation of students, with the potential to revolutionize the AI landscape through rigorous training in the aspects of fundamental research and application. The B.Tech AI equips students with comprehensive knowledge of the fundamental mathematical and computational aspects of AI. The program involves practical learning with state-of-the-art AI tools, technologies, and projects, and nurtures an innovation-driven environment through Hackathons and AI Competitions.

Industry Focussed

Lectures by international faculty, and industry experts. 20 % component of each subject is research-focused with industry certification, research papers, start-ups, Hackathons, AI Competitions, and projects with societal impact.

Learn from the Best

90% of faculty with Ph.D.s from IITs and other acclaimed institutions with prior industry experience.

Cutting Edge Research Labs

High-end teaching & research labs with the most advanced equipments and tools for AI research.

Thriving Start-Up Culture

Foster AI start-up culture with the potential to evolve thesis/project into an AI start-up venture

Interdisciplinary Approach

14 specializations in different domains of Agritech, Digital Healthcare, Data Science, Fintech, Legal – Tech, Media, Psychology, Quantum Information Processing, Robotics, Biotech pharma, Environment Sustainability, Material Science, Wireless networks and Signal Processing.

About

About B.Tech Artificial Intelligence

About

About B.Tech Artificial Intelligence

B.Tech AI program promotes interdisciplinary research in the field of Artificial Intelligence and provides students with a comprehensive understanding of real-world challenges. It emphasizes both theoretical and practical applications of AI, enabling students to apply the latest AI developments and use technology to address pressing issues in fields such as Agritech, Digital Healthcare, Data Science, Fintech, Legal – Tech, Media, Psychology, Quantum Information Processing, Robotics, Biotech pharma, Environment Sustainability, Material Science, Wireless networks and Signal Processing.

Curriculum

The B.Tech AI curriculum is designed with a meticulous and interdisciplinary approach to equip students with all the necessary skills and knowledge that are crucial in the field of Artificial Intelligence. By taking a holistic approach, the curriculum covers not only the technical aspects but also the practical applications of these technologies. The curriculum is tailored to meet the industry's demands and prepares students to excel in the rapidly evolving field of Artificial Intelligence .

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence AY 2024-28 – Click here >>

Specializations

Besides the major subject, the students will have the option of pursuing a minor from the second year in a range of relevant disciplines where the advent of Artificial Intelligence is already making a meaningful impact such as Agri tech, Digital Healthcare, Fin tech, Legal Tech, Biotech Pharma, Media, Digital Psychology, Quantum Information Processing, Robotics, Signal Processing, Material Processing and Data Science

There are a total of 6 courses for the minor, resulting in 18 credits. Read More >>

Elective Courses

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence offers 4 elective courses, allowing students flexibility according to their interests Read More >>

Learning Outcomes

Upon completing the B.Tech in AI , students will:

Possess deeper understanding of the mathematical and statistical aspects that form the fundamental pillars of Artificial Intelligence to unravel the “black-box” nature of Artificial Intelligence, to develop responsible and cognizable Artificial Intelligence frameworks.

Master essential Artificial Intelligence concepts, including Machine Learning, Inference Engines, Speech Recognition, Computer Vision, Natural Language Understanding, Robotics, and Human-Computer Interaction.

Integrate human cognition, Artificial Intelligence, and data engineering knowledge to design holistic systems.

Integrate human cognition, Artificial Intelligence, and data engineering knowledge to design holistic systems.

Possess the understanding of fundamental aspects of Artificial Intelligence to drive research towards the development of next-generation Artificial Intelligence tools.

Develop real-time, robust Artificial Intelligence-based systems tailored to specific software, hardware, and data constraints.

ELIGIBILITY

B.Tech AI Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process

Eligibility

Domestic

Passed XII standard from any recognized Education Board with Physics, Chemistry / IT, and Mathematics with minimum 65% marks OR IB Board with a minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects.

NRI

Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third subject with minimum 65% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24

Selection

Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score).

Note : Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc

OUR ASK

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence Frequently Asked Questions

What is the course curriculum of the B.Tech Artificial Intelligence program?

The course curriculum of B.Tech Artificial Intelligence has fine a balance of theoretical research and practical applications, equipping students to leverage and contribute to the state-of-the-art in AI development. The curriculum follows an interdisciplinary approach with the option to choose from 10 minors in different domains. Each subject in the curriculum has a 20% research-focused component, including industry certifications, research papers, self-learning, start-ups, and projects with societal impact. Overall, this comprehensive approach equips students with the skills and knowledge needed to succeed in the rapidly-evolving field of AI.

What are the career prospects after completing B.Tech AI ?

What are the career prospects after completing B.Tech AI ?

Graduates of B.Tech Artificial Intelligence are in high demand across different industries. AI graduates can be recruited in IT, Finance, Gaming, VR industries, to name a few. The demand for AI professionals is growing rapidly as industries are integrating AI in their processes. Some of the popular career choices are Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, Robotics Engineer, AI Researcher, Software Developer, and Business Intelligence Developer, Software Engineer, NLP Engineer, Big Data Scientist, Business Intelligence Developer, to name a few.

What are the skills required to excel in the B. Tech AI program?

To excel in the B. Tech Artificial Intelligence program, students should have strong analytical skills, problem-solving ability, critical thinking, and creativity. Additionally, proficiency in programming languages like Python and C++, knowledge of machine learning algorithms, data structures, and computer vision concepts are essential.

Is it necessary to have a background in Computer Science to pursue B.Tech AI ?

Having a background in Computer Science is not necessarily a requirement to pursue a B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence. However, it could be beneficial to have some foundational knowledge in programming, algorithms, and data structures.

What is the difference between B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and B.Tech Computer Science?

What is the difference between B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and B.Tech Computer Science?

B.Tech AI and B.Tech Computer Science are two different academic programs with distinct focuses. B.Tech AI primarily deals with the study of intelligent agents, which includes computer programs that can perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition, decision-making, and language translation. On the other hand, B.Tech Computer Science (CS) is an interdisciplinary field that involves the study of computer systems, software engineering, programming languages, algorithms, and data structures. While both programs have some overlap in their coursework, B.Tech AI focuses more on specialized topics such as machine learning, robotics, natural language processing, and cognitive computing, while B.Tech CSE covers a broader range of topics that include software development, database systems, networking, and computer architecture. Ultimately, the choice between these two programs depends on student’s interests and career goals.

Bennett University > About Us > Industry Advisory Board

Industry Advisory Board

The Industry Advisory Board at Bennett University comprises distinguished leaders from global industries who provide strategic guidance to enhance the university’s impact as a premier teaching, research, and experience-led institution. The Board advises leadership on priorities and supports the advancement of world-class programs across Engineering, Management, Law, Media, AI, Liberal Arts, Design, and Applied Sciences.

Industry Advisory Board Members

International Bar Association

Mr. Anurag Bana

ORGANIZATION: International Bar Association

Designation: Senior Legal Council

Mr. Anurag BanaSenior Legal Council

International Bar Association

Mr. Anurag Bana

ORGANIZATION: International Bar Association

Designation: Senior Legal Council

Anurag Bana is an Indian qualified lawyer with a BCL from the University of Oxford and over 19 years of experience, as a legal advisor, practitioner and researcher having worked across global jurisdictions, in the arena of International law and policy sector. His work trajectory spans a judicial clerkship with a Supreme Court of India Judge, internships at international organizations including the United Nations Commission on International Trade Law and the Permanent Bureau of the Hague Conference on Private International Law. He has practiced in Indian law courts and worked briefly in the corporate sector with Skoda Auto in Czech Republic.

Anurag is an influencer and thought leader in the emerging areas of legaltech, disruptive innovation, internet of things, digital assets and attorney-client privilege and confidentiality issues. He has published and presented several specialist papers on technology law related issues covering cyber security, blockchain technology, digital identity and social media laws

As the Chair of the IBA Social Media Working Group, he led the development and adoption of the international social media principles for the legal profession by the IBA in 2014. He is the IBA representative at the UK Attorney General’s International Pro Bono Committee; a fellow of the European Law Institute, a founding member of the Chinese European Legal Association; a member of the Advisory Group on Diversity and Inclusion of the Society for Computers and Law and; life member of the Oxford Union Society.

Anurag is in the expert panels of the following organizations:

United Nations Centre for Trade Facilitation and Electronic Business (UN/CEFACT);

United Nations Commission on International Trade Law Working Group IV (Electronic Commerce) Identity Management & Trust Services;

United Nations Commission on International Trade Law Working Group IV (Electronic Commerce) Identity Management & Trust Services;

United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (Judicial Integrity Initiative) Expert Group on Social Media Use by Judges;

Hague Conference on Private International Law Experts’ Group on Use of Video-link and Modern Technologies in the Taking of Evidence Abroad;

Commonwealth Virtual Currencies Expert Working Group;

Europol Platform for Experts – International Legal Policy Expert

Khaitan and Company

Mr. Haigreve Khaitan

ORGANIZATION: Khaitan and Company

Designation: Senior Partner

Haigreve is a Senior Partner and heads the Corporate/M&A and Private Equity practice at the Firm. He is an elected member of the National Executive Committee of the Firm which is responsible for the firm’s strategic growth and development. He advises companies, boards of directors and financial institutions on a wide range of corporate matters, including mergers and acquisitions, private equity investments, corporate governance, corporate restructuring and other corporate and securities laws matters.

He has been involved in some of the most high-profile and complex M&A and private equity transactions in India and is sought after for his expertise by some of the most prominent Indian promoters, Indian and international companies, Indian regulatory agencies as well as some of the largest global funds investing in India. He advises a range of large Indian conglomerates and multinational clients in various business sectors, including financial services, manufacturing, retail, infrastructure, steel, software and information technology.

Haigreve has been consistently recommended by many of the world’s leading law chambers and accreditation bodies. Sources also praise him as an “outstanding lawyer” who is “extremely good and very sharp when it comes to large transactions’’. He has been acknowledged for his experience and expertise by:

Asian Legal Business amongst the top 15 M&A lawyers in Asia.

Chambers and Partners as a ‘’Star Individual’’ for M&A, ‘’Eminent Practitioner’’ for Private Equity and “Band 1 Lawyer” for Private Wealth Law.

The Legal 500 as a ‘’Leading Expert’’ for Corporate and M&A;

IFLR 1000 as a ‘’Market Leading Lawyer” for Corporate M&A; and Banking & Finance.

India Business Law Journal amongst top 100 lawyers in India.

Who’s Who Legal as a “Thought Leader’ for M&A and Governance in India.

AsiaLaw Profile as an ‘Elite Practitioner’ for Corporate M&A and Private Equity

Haigreve is on the Board of Directors of various companies including Jio Platforms Limited, Torrent Pharmaceuticals Ltd, Mahindra & Mahindra Limited, CEAT Limited, JSW Steel Limited, Tech Mahindra Limited, Inox Leisure Limited and Borosil Renewables Limited.

Kishore Jayaraman is President for Rolls-Royce India and South Asia. Since taking over the role nine years ago, Kishore has been instrumental in driving strategic focus and significant expansion across the Rolls-Royce Defence, Civil Aerospace and Power Systems businesses.

A strong proponent of technology innovation, he led the establishment of Rolls-Royce’s digital and engineering capabilities in India that now contribute to global development projects.

Prior to joining Rolls-Royce, Kishore held diverse leadership roles across markets at GE for 23 years, and his last assignment was as President and CEO of GE Energy, South Asia.

Prior to joining Rolls-Royce, Kishore held diverse leadership roles across markets at GE for 23 years, and his last assignment was as President and CEO of GE Energy, South Asia.

With an MS in Mechanical Engineering and an MBA, Kishore serves on several boards and is at the helm of industry committees and associations, whilst being a champion for sustainability and strategic ecosystem evolution for the present and future.

Kishore is based in New Delhi, India.

Mr. Manoj Madhavan

ORGANIZATION: Boston Scientific, India Hub

Designation: Managing Director

Manoj Madhavan took over as the Managing Director for Boston Scientific, India Hub in July 2019 and also leads the Boston Scientific operations in Bangladesh, Nepal, Bhutan, Maldives & Sri Lanka. He is a member of the Boston Scientific Asia Pacific regional leadership team.

Manoj has over 17 years of experience in leadership roles across sales, marketing and general management. He had joined Boston Scientific Global in March 2014 as part of the Global Leadership Development Program. Over the last 6 years, he has worked in various marketing leadership roles in the US, Australia & New Zealand. Prior to taking over the business for the India Hub, Manoj was the Country Leader for Boston Scientific Philippines and ASEAN Marketing Leader for the Urology & Pelvic Health Business, where he scripted a business turnaround and delivered one of the fastest growth rates in that business in the Asia Pacific region. Under his leadership, BSC Philippines team won the ‘Regional Excellence Award’ in 2018 for delivering outstanding performance across all key commercial metrics.

In past, Manoj had tenured with Johnson & Johnson as the Business Manager for Tier 2/3 Markets in India. Before Johnson & Johnson, Manoj spent a few years in Financial Services as well.

In past, Manoj had tenured with Johnson & Johnson as the Business Manager for Tier 2/3 Markets in India. Before Johnson & Johnson, Manoj spent a few years in Financial Services as well.

Manoj is an MBA from MDI, Gurgaon and INSEAD. He is extremely passionate about patient outcomes and likes to work on enhancing market access and collaborating with governments and public organizations to ensure improved access to healthcare.

Ms. Megha Tata

Designation: Chief Executive Officer

Megha Tata joined Cosmos Maya as Chief Executive Officer on Oct 2022. Megha has held leadership roles at organisations such as BTVI, HBO, Turner International and Star TV, in an illustrious career spanning more than 28 years. An industry veteran, she leads multiple national as well as international industry forums and an esteemed member of the National Media and Entertainment Committee of FICCI, and is also the second woman to ever become the President of the India Chapter of the International Advertising Association.

Megha has been instrumental in launching several marquee projects for the network in India, including the local launch of streaming service discovery+, now India’s largest aggregated real-life infotainment OTT service. Under her leadership, Discovery has forayed into original Indian content, with discovery+ launching a slew of exciting originals, including the critically acclaimed 'Into the Wild with Bear Grylls’ episodes with Rajnikanth and Akshay Kumar. Her knowledge and deep-rooted understanding of the business have aided the network to achieve new heights in the animation genre with Discovery Kids smart strategy and marquee content line-up for the Indian audience. Rebranding Discovery’s sports channel - Eurosport in March 2020 is a journey that's worth a lot of learnings.

In 2020, Discovery India retained its position as the ‘Most Trusted Real-Life Entertainment Brand’ by TRA Research Private Limited for the 10th year in a row, with content teams being singled out for innumerable accolades including; ‘Network of the Year’ as well as ‘Best Entertainment Programme’ and ‘Best Infotainment Programme’ for Man vs Wild with Bear Grylls and Prime Minister Modi at the 25th Asian Television Awards.

Megha has been bestowed with multiple industry accolades during her career. She is recognized amongst the top 50 influential women in media, marketing and advertising and was also conferred ‘Women of the decade in media’ by the Women Economic Forum in 2018.

In addition to being actively involved in industry initiatives, Megha is energized by engaging with young minds through guest lectures at leading educational institutes including IIT, IIM, ISB, UBS, and is passionately involved as a Volunteer with Isha Foundation - a non-profit organization started by Sadhguru.

Nitin joined Avian WE (then known as Avian Media) as its CEO in 2006. The firm was then two years old and operated out of an apartment in Delhi with 10 employees and a handful of clients. In a few years, Nitin set the template for year-on-year growth. Today Avian has over 200 employees in six offices in India. Its key clients include Airbnb, Amazon, Child Investment Fund Federation, Deloitte, Facebook, Intuit, Johnson & Johnson, Lockheed Martin, Mastercard, McDonald’s, Pernod Ricard, Philips, Reckitt, Samsung, Sony India, Viacom and VMware.

Nitin and his business partner also founded Chase India, now a subsidiary of Avian WE, in 2011 to work on public policy mandates for Indian and multinational companies. Before joining Avian, Nitin worked at Brodeur/Pleon (now Ketchum), a Europe-based public relations and communications consultancy that belongs to the Omnicom Group, in London. He was responsible for Pleon’s UK operations, and headed the corporate and technology B2B practices. Nitin began his communications career at Genesis PR (now BCW) after completing his MBA from the Birla Institute of Management Technology.

Nitin takes a keen interest in the development of the public relations industry. Since he became the PRCAI president in 2015 he has focused on improving the public perception of the profession in India. As president of ICCO, Nitin is making the global PR industry relevant to different markets, and potential talent. He regularly speaks at the AMEC Global Summit, ICCO Global Summit and Sabre Awards. Nitin teaches and charts course syllabus at prominent mass communication colleges in India to increase awareness about the profession at the grass-root level.

He has been recognized on several platforms for his contribution to the industry including the Individual Achievement award at the Sabre APAC in 2021 and the ‘Global PR Leader of the Year’ at the 2015 ICCO Global Awards.

Nitin lives in New Delhi with his wife and two children.
