Role description

Role description

Vineet is currently serving as a Global General Counsel of Tech Mahindra Limited, a US$ 5.3 Billion Indian MNC providing IT, ITeS, Consulting & BPO Services to clients worldwide. In capacity as the corporate officer in-charge of the Legal function, he is responsible for the handling entire Legal & Regulatory affairs of Tech Mahindra and its group & portfolio companies worldwide and advising & supporting multi-billion cross-border Commercial transactions, Mergers & Acquisitions, Disputes & Litigations, Ethics & Global Compliance, Legal Policy & Regulatory Affairs, IPR & strategic legal support.

Areas of expertise

His work experience of over 25 years spans across wide variety of practice areas including Commercial & Contract laws, Litigation & Dispute Resolution, Cross-border Mergers & Acquisitions, Global Regulatory Compliances, Financial & Banking, Real Estate and SEZ & STPI regulations, Company law, IT, Data Protection, Privacy, Cyber & IPR laws, Criminal law, Telecom, Litigation & Arbitration, Employment laws, Bankruptcy laws, Competition laws & Corporate affairs and Indirect Taxation laws.

Professional experience

Professional experience

After working as practicing lawyer in Delhi High Court and the district courts in the initial years, Vineet embarked on the journey of becoming an in-house counsel with Punj Lloyd Group in a secretarial-cum-legal role, which exposed him to various facets of the law that eventually formed a significant part of his responsibilities. This was followed with an 8 years’ stint with the TATA Group (Tata Unisys, Tata Infotech, TCS) and working closely with Tata Sons and Tata Services, which further provided exposure to global deals & transactions. At American Express, working on complex legal challenges he got the opportunity to solve regulatory, legal & compliance issues in the banking, finance sector and NBFC sector. Vineet has also worked with HCL Technologies for over a decade and his path at HCL was punctuated by his significant role in handling billion-dollar contracts, complex litigations & arbitrations, establishing SEZ/STPI/Free-trade Zones, ensuring Global Compliances, resolving Customs & Indirect Taxation issues, filing of Patents, Copyright, Trademarks & resolving IPR-related issues, handling public affairs functions and strategic legal assistance for HCL Corporation and Foundation. He provided active legal & regulatory support in HCL’s entry in the software business segment - the biggest IP acquisition the Indian IT/ITeS space has ever seen.

Currently, as the Group General Counsel for Tech Mahindra and its group companies the across globe, he leads a team of 100 lawyers who are located in different parts of the world and are taking care of the legal & regulatory aspects concerning the Tech Mahindra Group & portfolio companies and Tech Mahindra Foundation & its CSR activities. Vineet acts as a bulwark against legal challenges and roadblocks faced by the group to pave way for exponential business growth in tandem with risk mitigation and compliance with country law & legal obligations.

Education and Memberships

Education and Memberships

Vineet graduated with a B.Com (Hons) degree from prestigious Shri Ram College of Commerce and L.L.B. from Delhi University. He holds additional qualification & degrees in Company Law, Labour Laws, Industrial Relations & Personnel Management, IT & Cyber Laws. With the wide-ranging areas of practice, he also serves on the Board of Studies and Advisory Boards of leading law school/universities, editorial board of legal journal & magazines and member of various trade associations and advocacy groups such as CII, FICCI, CII, ASSOCHAM and INBA.

Pro bono work

Vineet believes that his role as a Group General Counsel is not only restricted to handling legal affairs for the Tech Mahindra Group, but also extends towards lending a hand to alleviate social imperils. Noting the lack of access to legal aid and resources among marginalized communities, he places great emphasis on providing legal advice and ensuring access to justice for such people.

As the Group General Counsel for Tech Mahindra, Vineet feels that it is an important responsibility for the entire team to contribute towards the overall organizational goal of undertaking CSR activities. He has fully espoused the company’s CSR policy and works tirelessly to make the CSR activities a success and a longstanding initiative. He also drives members of the legal team to participate in volunteering in company’s CSR activities, besides their individual initiatives in line with the legal aid and awareness.

Mr. Rajendra Mehta

ORGANIZATION: Suzlon Limited.

Designation: President and CHRO

Comprehensive experience of 27 years in developing and executing strategic Human Resources agenda of organisations. Most of these agenda are driven for profitable growth of business. Extensive exposure to large corporations with business spread across geographies, in India and overseas as well.

Coached by Dr Ram Charan have been recognized for expertise in implementing world-class practices to transform organizations into high-performing and profitable entities. Coordinate with global management consultancies to develop productivity enhancement programs to yield superlative business results. A licensed practitioner of Thomas Profiling System and an expert in implementing Gallup, Leadership Circle and GPTW frameworks, I identify and hire leadership talent across generic and niche skills. Ensure sustained development of employees to build organisational capabilities as well as to retain and promote talent.

Mr. Vynsley Fernandes Managing Director and CEO

NXT Digital (Hinduja Group)

View More

Mr. Vynsley Fernandes

ORGANIZATION: NXT Digital (Hinduja Group)

Designation: Managing Director and CEO

Mr. Vynsley Fernandes Managing Director and CEO

NXT Digital (Hinduja Group)

View More

Mr. Vynsley Fernandes

ORGANIZATION: NXT Digital (Hinduja Group)

Designation: Managing Director and CEO

Vynsley Fernandes is a veteran media executive and a leading technocrat, with three decades of experience in delivering and managing assignments globally - for DTH (Direct-to-Home) & HITS (Headend-In-The-Sky) platforms; Internet Service Providers, digital cable networks; and news & entertainment channels. Prior to his current role at Hinduja Global Solutions, part of the global Hinduja Group; he has managed high-profile assignments at blue-chip organisations including 21st Century Fox (News Corporation), STAR Television, TATA SKY and INX Network. With an academic background in Mass Communications and Media, Vynsley is considered a thought leader in the media & entertainment space. He continues to represent the industry at various international and national forums and has several accolades to his credit including being recognized with the prestigious “CEO of the Year” award presented by World Leadership Congress in 2021.

Mr. Rishikesh Raval President - Group Human Resources & Corporate Communications

Zydus Group

View More

Mr. Rishikesh Raval

ORGANIZATION: Zydus Group

Designation: President - Group Human Resources & Corporate Communications

Rishikesh has had an incredible impact in the area of human behaviour, organization building and cultural transformation in India with leading MNCs and large Indian Business organizations . For more than two decades, he has immersed himself in the practice of People Processes, Organization Development, and Change Management. Applauded for his work in the area of Programmed Change Management and facilitation of multiple projects around cultural transformation, he has endured to design the decisive 'predictable organizational architecture' while preserving result driven distinctiveness of the enterprise.

In earlier years of career, Rishikesh worked with Sarabhai Group, Glaxo India, Pfizer Ltd. in Human Resources function for Manufacturing vertical.

Mr. Avinash Gupta Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer

Dun & Bradstreet

View More

Mr. Avinash Gupta

ORGANIZATION: Dun & Bradstreet

Designation: Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer

Avinash is a highly experienced, execution-focused senior leader with a strong, global background and brings more than 3 decades of experience and a vast network of business relationships. Avinash has worked extensively in financial services including M&A, equity and debt financing, private equity placement and advisory across the EMENA and Asia.

Prior to joining Dun & Bradstreet in 2021, Avinash worked with Rabo Equity Advisors where he was the Managing Director since 2018. Over the years, he has held various leadership positions including recently as Head of Deloitte India's Financial Advisory Services business. He has worked in senior positions across geographies and products in various institutions such as HSBC, UBS, Barings, Citibank, Falcon Capital, and Alpen Capital. In his initial years in the USA, he also worked as an equity analyst covering the Oil Services sector at Wedbush Morgan Securities (Los Angeles) and Howard Weil (New Orleans).

Avinash has an MBA from the A.B. Freeman School of Business, Tulane University (Deans List with full fellowship) and a B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology, BHU Varanasi.

Avinash sits on the National Executive Committees of Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FICCI) and Associated Chambers of Commerce and Industry of India (ASSOCHAM).

Programs

Programs

Being one of the best private universities in India, Bennett University offers diverse programmes that are supported by distinguished faculties, possessing academia-industry experience and exposure. The undergraduate, postgraduate and certification level programmes give an edge to aspirants to pave the future path on their desired field after pursuing it from the best UGC approved university for professional programmes.

Bennett University > About Us > Industry Advisory Board

Industry Advisory Board

The Industry Advisory Board at Bennett University comprises distinguished leaders from global industries who provide strategic guidance to enhance the university’s impact as a premier teaching, research, and experience-led institution. The Board advises leadership on priorities and supports the advancement of world-class programs across Engineering, Management, Law, Media, AI, Liberal Arts, Design, and Applied Sciences.

Industry Advisory Board Members

Mr. Anurag BanaSenior Legal Council

International Bar Association

View More

Mr. Anurag Bana

ORGANIZATION: International Bar Association

Designation: Senior Legal Council

Mr. Anurag BanaSenior Legal Council

International Bar Association

View More

Mr. Anurag Bana

ORGANIZATION: International Bar Association

Designation: Senior Legal Council

Anurag Bana is an Indian qualified lawyer with a BCL from the University of Oxford and over 19 years of experience, as a legal advisor, practitioner and researcher having worked across global jurisdictions, in the arena of International law and policy sector. His work trajectory spans a judicial clerkship with a Supreme Court of India Judge, internships at international organizations including the United Nations Commission on International Trade Law and the Permanent Bureau of the Hague Conference on Private International Law. He has practiced in Indian law courts and worked briefly in the corporate sector with Skoda Auto in Czech Republic.

Anurag is an influencer and thought leader in the emerging areas of legaltech, disruptive innovation, internet of things, digital assets and attorney-client privilege and confidentiality issues. He has published and presented several specialist papers on technology law related issues covering cyber security, blockchain technology, digital identity and social media laws

As the Chair of the IBA Social Media Working Group, he led the development and adoption of the international social media principles for the legal profession by the IBA in 2014. He is the IBA representative at the UK Attorney General’s International Pro Bono Committee; a fellow of the European Law Institute, a founding member of the Chinese European Legal Association; a member of the Advisory Group on Diversity and Inclusion of the Society for Computers and Law and; life member of the Oxford Union Society.

Anurag is in the expert panels of the following organizations:

United Nations Centre for Trade Facilitation and Electronic Business (UN/CEFACT);

United Nations Commission on International Trade Law Working Group IV (Electronic Commerce) Identity Management & Trust Services;

United Nations Commission on International Trade Law Working Group IV (Electronic Commerce) Identity Management & Trust Services;

United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (Judicial Integrity Initiative) Expert Group on Social Media Use by Judges;

Hague Conference on Private International Law Experts’ Group on Use of Video-link and Modern Technologies in the Taking of Evidence Abroad;

Commonwealth Virtual Currencies Expert Working Group;

Europol Platform for Experts – International Legal Policy Expert

Mr. Haigreve KhaitanSenior Partner

Khaitan and Company

View More

Mr. Haigreve Khaitan

ORGANIZATION: Khaitan and Company

Designation: Senior Partner

Haigreve is a Senior Partner and heads the Corporate/M&A and Private Equity practice at the Firm. He is an elected member of the National Executive Committee of the Firm which is responsible for the firm’s strategic growth and development. He advises companies, boards of directors and financial institutions on a wide range of corporate matters, including mergers and acquisitions, private equity investments, corporate governance, corporate restructuring and other corporate and securities laws matters.

He has been involved in some of the most high-profile and complex M&A and private equity transactions in India and is sought after for his expertise by some of the most prominent Indian promoters, Indian and international companies, Indian regulatory agencies as well as some of the largest global funds investing in India. He advises a range of large Indian conglomerates and multinational clients in various business sectors, including financial services, manufacturing, retail, infrastructure, steel, software and information technology.

Haigreve has been consistently recommended by many of the world’s leading law chambers and accreditation bodies. Sources also praise him as an “outstanding lawyer” who is “extremely good and very sharp when it comes to large transactions’’. He has been acknowledged for his experience and expertise by:

Asian Legal Business amongst the top 15 M&A lawyers in Asia.

Chambers and Partners as a ‘’Star Individual’’ for M&A, ‘’Eminent Practitioner’’ for Private Equity and “Band 1 Lawyer” for Private Wealth Law.

The Legal 500 as a ‘’Leading Expert’’ for Corporate and M&A;

IFLR 1000 as a ‘’Market Leading Lawyer” for Corporate M&A; and Banking & Finance.

India Business Law Journal amongst top 100 lawyers in India.

Who’s Who Legal as a “Thought Leader’ for M&A and Governance in India.

AsiaLaw Profile as an ‘Elite Practitioner’ for Corporate M&A and Private Equity

Haigreve is on the Board of Directors of various companies including Jio Platforms Limited, Torrent Pharmaceuticals Ltd, Mahindra & Mahindra Limited, CEAT Limited, JSW Steel Limited, Tech Mahindra Limited, Inox Leisure Limited and Borosil Renewables Limited.

Mr. Kishore JayaramanPresident - India & South Asia

Rolls-Royce

View More

Mr. Kishore Jayaraman

ORGANIZATION: Rolls-Royce

Designation: President - India & South Asia

Kishore Jayaraman is President for Rolls-Royce India and South Asia. Since taking over the role nine years ago, Kishore has been instrumental in driving strategic focus and significant expansion across the Rolls-Royce Defence, Civil Aerospace and Power Systems businesses.

A strong proponent of technology innovation, he led the establishment of Rolls-Royce’s digital and engineering capabilities in India that now contribute to global development projects.

Prior to joining Rolls-Royce, Kishore held diverse leadership roles across markets at GE for 23 years, and his last assignment was as President and CEO of GE Energy, South Asia.

Prior to joining Rolls-Royce, Kishore held diverse leadership roles across markets at GE for 23 years, and his last assignment was as President and CEO of GE Energy, South Asia.

With an MS in Mechanical Engineering and an MBA, Kishore serves on several boards and is at the helm of industry committees and associations, whilst being a champion for sustainability and strategic ecosystem evolution for the present and future.

Kishore is based in New Delhi, India.

Mr. Manoj MadhavanManaging Director

Boston Scientific, India Hub

View More

Mr. Manoj Madhavan

ORGANIZATION: Boston Scientific, India Hub

Designation: Managing Director

Manoj Madhavan took over as the Managing Director for Boston Scientific, India Hub in July 2019 and also leads the Boston Scientific operations in Bangladesh, Nepal, Bhutan, Maldives & Sri Lanka. He is a member of the Boston Scientific Asia Pacific regional leadership team.

Manoj has over 17 years of experience in leadership roles across sales, marketing and general management. He had joined Boston Scientific Global in March 2014 as part of the Global Leadership Development Program. Over the last 6 years, he has worked in various marketing leadership roles in the US, Australia & New Zealand. Prior to taking over the business for the India Hub, Manoj was the Country Leader for Boston Scientific Philippines and ASEAN Marketing Leader for the Urology & Pelvic Health Business, where he scripted a business turnaround and delivered one of the fastest growth rates in that business in the Asia Pacific region. Under his leadership, BSC Philippines team won the ‘Regional Excellence Award’ in 2018 for delivering outstanding performance across all key commercial metrics.

In past, Manoj had tenured with Johnson & Johnson as the Business Manager for Tier 2/3 Markets in India. Before Johnson & Johnson, Manoj spent a few years in Financial Services as well.

In past, Manoj had tenured with Johnson & Johnson as the Business Manager for Tier 2/3 Markets in India. Before Johnson & Johnson, Manoj spent a few years in Financial Services as well.

Manoj is an MBA from MDI, Gurgaon and INSEAD. He is extremely passionate about patient outcomes and likes to work on enhancing market access and collaborating with governments and public organizations to ensure improved access to healthcare.

Ms. Megha TataChief Executive Officer

Cosmos Maya

View More

Ms. Megha Tata

ORGANIZATION: Cosmos Maya

Designation: Chief Executive Officer

Megha Tata joined Cosmos Maya as Chief Executive Officer on Oct 2022. Megha has held leadership roles at organisations such as BTVI, HBO, Turner International and Star TV, in an illustrious career spanning more than 28 years. An industry veteran, she leads multiple national as well as international industry forums and an esteemed member of the National Media and Entertainment Committee of FICCI, and is also the second woman to ever become the President of the India Chapter of the International Advertising Association.

Megha has been instrumental in launching several marquee projects for the network in India, including the local launch of streaming service discovery+, now India’s largest aggregated real-life infotainment OTT service. Under her leadership, Discovery has forayed into original Indian content, with discovery+ launching a slew of exciting originals, including the critically acclaimed 'Into the Wild with Bear Grylls’ episodes with Rajnikanth and Akshay Kumar. Her knowledge and deep-rooted understanding of the business have aided the network to achieve new heights in the animation genre with Discovery Kids smart strategy and marquee content line-up for the Indian audience. Rebranding Discovery’s sports channel - Eurosport in March 2020 is a journey that's worth a lot of learnings.

In 2020, Discovery India retained its position as the ‘Most Trusted Real-Life Entertainment Brand’ by TRA Research Private Limited for the 10th year in a row, with content teams being singled out for innumerable accolades including; ‘Network of the Year’ as well as ‘Best Entertainment Programme’ and ‘Best Infotainment Programme’ for Man vs Wild with Bear Grylls and Prime Minister Modi at the 25th Asian Television Awards.

Megha has been bestowed with multiple industry accolades during her career. She is recognized amongst the top 50 influential women in media, marketing and advertising and was also conferred ‘Women of the decade in media’ by the Women Economic Forum in 2018.

In addition to being actively involved in industry initiatives, Megha is energized by engaging with young minds through guest lectures at leading educational institutes including IIT, IIM, ISB, UBS, and is passionately involved as a Volunteer with Isha Foundation - a non-profit organization started by Sadhguru.

Mr. Nitin ChandelManaging Director

BNY Mellon Technology, India

View More

Mr. Nitin Chandel

ORGANIZATION: BNY Mellon Technology, India

Designation: Managing Director

Nitin Chandel is the Head of BNY Mellon Technology in India and has offices in Chennai and Pune. Based out of Pune, Nitin manages and leads the global technology team of close to 5,500 across India. He drives technology transformation, focusing on digitization and infrastructure resiliency. Additionally, he also oversees recruitment and retention of top technology talent.

Prior to this, Chandel was Senior Vice President at Visa for its Developer Platform and led a global team from India. He built out Visa's India Technology Center in Bengaluru. He has also held several senior technology roles at Microsoft, where he led Bing Ads development teams both in Bengaluru and in the company's corporate headquarters in Redmond, Washington, U.S. His past experience includes the following –

Led engineering for Visa Developer, a strategic initiative for Visa aimed at opening up access to Visa's network through APIs

Architected, designed, and developed big data systems for very large-scale transactional e-commerce systems, dealing with multi terra-bytes of data processing on a daily basis

Led teams building the full stack for very high scale web applications, including the app stack, API interfaces, backend data and billing systems. ~ 30-40 million transactions per day

Large, distributed, and scalable systems architecture experience with the ability to look broadly across the technology and business process ecosystem

Building and transforming teams into high performing, agile, high energy teams which deliver start-up levels of productivity, with the design and quality of a mature product team

A track record of attracting, developing, and retaining top talent. Ability to manage and motivate large teams and organizations

Designed and modelled large scale e-commerce systems with hands on experience with the data tier

Mr. Nitin MantriGroup CEO

Avian WE

View More

Mr. Nitin Mantri

ORGANIZATION: Avian WE

Designation: Group CEO

Nitin Mantri is a dynamic leader in the world of communications. He’s the Group CEO of Avian WE and the President of International Communications Consultancy Organization (ICCO). He was the President of the Public Relations Consultants Association of India (PRCAI) for five years - from 2015 to 2020. Recently he was also appointed to the Global Advisory board of the PRCA and is the Co-Chair of the PRCA APAC and PRCA Global Ethics Council.

Nitin joined Avian WE (then known as Avian Media) as its CEO in 2006. The firm was then two years old and operated out of an apartment in Delhi with 10 employees and a handful of clients. In a few years, Nitin set the template for year-on-year growth. Today Avian has over 200 employees in six offices in India. Its key clients include Airbnb, Amazon, Child Investment Fund Federation, Deloitte, Facebook, Intuit, Johnson & Johnson, Lockheed Martin, Mastercard, McDonald’s, Pernod Ricard, Philips, Reckitt, Samsung, Sony India, Viacom and VMware.

Nitin’s experience of over two decades in diverse sectors and his ability to mitigate crisis and train the C-suite in managing the media helped Avian win clients for life. Nitin and his business partner also founded Chase India, now a subsidiary of Avian WE, in 2011 to work on public policy mandates for Indian and multinational companies. Before joining Avian, Nitin worked at Brodeur/Pleon (now Ketchum), a Europe-based public relations and communications consultancy that belongs to the Omnicom Group, in London. He was responsible for Pleon’s UK operations, and headed the corporate and technology B2B practices. Nitin began his communications career at Genesis PR (now BCW) after completing his MBA from the Birla Institute of Management Technology.

Nitin takes a keen interest in the development of the public relations industry. Since he became the PRCAI president in 2015 he has focused on improving the public perception of the profession in India. As president of ICCO, Nitin is making the global PR industry relevant to different markets, and potential talent. He regularly speaks at the AMEC Global Summit, ICCO Global Summit and Sabre Awards. Nitin teaches and charts course syllabus at prominent mass communication colleges in India to increase awareness about the profession at the grass-root level.

He has been recognized on several platforms for his contribution to the industry including the Individual Achievement award at the Sabre APAC in 2021 and the ‘Global PR Leader of the Year’ at the 2015 ICCO Global Awards.

Nitin lives in New Delhi with his wife and two children.

Mr. Sanjay SindhwaniChief Executive Officer

Indian Express Online Media

View More

Mr. Sanjay Sindhwani

ORGANIZATION: Indian Express Online Media

Designation: Chief Executive Officer

Sanjay Sindhwani, currently CEO, Indian Express Online Media, has over 25 years in the Media Industry across Print, Television and Digital.

Designation: Chief Executive Officer

Sanjay Sindhwani, currently CEO, Indian Express Online Media, has over 25 years in the Media Industry across Print, Television and Digital.

As CEO of Indian Express Online Media, Sanjay is working on growing the strong portfolio of Express brands viz. IndianExpress.com, FinancialExpress.com, Loksatta.com, Jansatta.com, Inuth.com to greater heights. He is specially focused on diversifying the revenue streams and improving profitability. As a part of the diversification exercise, Sanjay led the acquisition of MyInsuranceClub.com an insurance aggregator into the IE family. He also launched several new IPs like India Gaming Summit, BrandWagon Conclave and Awards, India Education Summit plus many more in Healthcare and Pharma, Small and Medium Enterprises and Automotive space. He is now focused on growing the subscription business for the IE Digital.

Prior to joining Indian Express, he was with EconomicTimes.com for 12 years as the Business Head. He conceptualized and launched ETPrime, the premium subscription product for The Economic Times. He also responsible for launching ET in multiple Indian languages and on mobile as early as 2007; created ETMarkets, ETRise, and several other verticals, and steered ET Digital into the No. 1 position in the Business News category with huge margin over its competitors. He also started several new IPs like ETPrime Women’s Leadership Awards, ET Campus Star – a program to identify and celebrate talent across college campuses, ET Markets Global Summit to engage different audience segments and acquire new readers into the ET fold.

Between his two 12 year stints at ET Sanjay also dabbled with Television @ Television Eighteen.

Between his two 12 year stints at ET Sanjay also dabbled with Television @ Television Eighteen.

His first stint at ET started on the editorial side with ‘The Economic Times’ print, where he spent over 5 years before being smitten by the internet bug and moved into setting up and launch the economictimes.com website in 1999. He was the first Analyst to identify and highlight the issues and challenges in the Unit Scheme 64, months before the downfall of scheme was officially accepted and acknowledged.

Over the years he has moved across functions like edit, product, business-development, new initiatives, strategy, and P&L management.

Follow Sanjay on twitter @Sindhwani_IE or connect @ https://www.linkedin.com/in/sindhwani/

Ms. Satyavati BereraChief Operating Officer

PwC India

View More

Ms. Satyavati Berera

ORGANIZATION: PwC India

Designation: Chief Operating Officer

Satyavati Berera, COO of PwC India, has over 35 years of diverse experience across several sectors and is also a member of PwC India's leadership team.

Satya started her career as an article trainee in 1980 and went on to become an Audit Partner in 1995. Before becoming the COO in 2016, she played key leadership roles at the firm such as the Consulting Leader, Risk Advisory Services Leader and also the Managing Partner for the firm's North region. Satya has extensively worked across sectors in the areas of Assurance, Consulting and Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) Services. She was actively involved in designing and implementing control strengthening and process improvement solutions for a variety of Indian and multinational companies. In her current role as the COO, in addition to managing the firm's operations she is driving the firm's transformation agenda.

For the last several years, Satya has been closely associated with Mobile Creches, a not-for-profit organisation devoted to the education of young children from marginalised communities. Satya graduated from Lady Shri Ram College in Economics and is a Fellow member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI).

Mr. Sunil GoyalChief Executive Officer

Sopra Banking Software

View More

Mr. Sunil Goyal

ORGANIZATION: Sopra Banking Software

Designation: Chief Executive Officer

Sunil Goyal is the Deputy Chief Executive Officer of Sopra Steria India, a part of the €4.3 billion Sopra Steria Group, headquartered in Paris. Sopra Steria Group is one of the top 5 French IT Services company in France having a strength of 46,000+ professionals worldwide operating from 30 countries.

Sunil started his career as a telecom support professional at Unitel and then managed sales at Wipro. He soon branched out to start on his own and co-founded Momentum India with almost no seed capital in 1993.

Under the leadership of Sunil and the co-founder, the company soon became the largest channel partner for Wipro InfoTech for their IT products in India. They were also instrumental in raising USD 1.5 million funding from Citibank when they diversified into software in the year 2000. Momentum was subsequently acquired by Sopra Group in July 2005.

At Sopra Steria India, Sunil is responsible for all functions including Software Delivery, HR, IT, Finance and Administration. Sopra Steria has centers in Noida, Chennai, Pune and Bengaluru, with an employee strength of 4200+ people. His Passion has always been People and he has been instrumental in setting up a high performance driven culture at the

**Industry Advisory Board**

The Industry Advisory Board at Bennett University brings together distinguished leaders from global industries to provide strategic guidance, enhance the university’s impact, and advance world‑class programs across Engineering, Management, Law, Media, AI, Liberal Arts, Design, and Applied Sciences.

**Key Member**

- **Mr. Anurag Bana** – Senior Legal Council, International Bar Association  

---

Mr. Anurag Bana – Senior Legal Council, International Bar Association  

Anurag Bana is an Indian‑qualified lawyer with a BCL from the University of Oxford and more than 19 years of experience as a legal advisor, practitioner and researcher. He has worked across global jurisdictions in international law and policy, including a judicial clerkship with a Supreme Court of India judge, internships at the United Nations Commission on International Trade Law and the Permanent Bureau of the Hague Conference on Private International Law, and practice in Indian courts. He also worked briefly in the corporate sector with Skoda Auto in the Czech Republic.

Anurag is an influencer and thought leader in emerging areas such as legaltech, disruptive innovation, the Internet of Things, digital assets, and attorney‑client privilege and confidentiality. He has published and presented specialist papers on technology law covering cybersecurity, blockchain technology, digital identity, and social media laws.

He served as Chair of the IBA Social Media Working Group, leading the development and adoption of the International Social Media Principles for the legal profession in 2014. He is the IBA representative on the UK Attorney General’s International Pro Bono Committee; a fellow of the European Law Institute; a founding member of the Chinese European Legal Association; a member of the Advisory Group on Diversity and Inclusion of the Society for Computers and Law; and a life member of the Oxford Union Society.

**Expert Panels**  
- United Nations Centre for Trade Facilitation and Electronic Business (UN/CEFACT)  
- United Nations Commission on International Trade Law Working Group IV (Electronic Commerce)  
- Identity Management & Trust Services

United Nations Commission on International Trade Law Working Group IV (Electronic Commerce) – Identity Management & Trust Services  
United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (Judicial Integrity Initiative) – Expert Group on Social Media Use by Judges  
Hague Conference on Private International Law – Experts’ Group on Use of Video‑link and Modern Technologies in the Taking of Evidence Abroad  
Commonwealth Virtual Currencies – Expert Working Group  
Europol Platform for Experts – International Legal Policy Expert  

**Mr. Haigreve Khaitan**  
Organization: Khaitan & Company  
Designation: Senior Partner  

Haigreve is a Senior Partner and heads the Corporate/M&A and Private Equity practice at the firm. He is an elected member of the National Executive Committee, responsible for the firm’s strategic growth and development. He advises companies, boards of directors, and financial institutions on a wide range of corporate matters, including mergers and acquisitions, private equity investments, corporate governance, corporate restructuring, and other corporate and securities law matters.  

He has been involved in some of the most high‑profile and complex M&A and private equity transactions in India and is sought after for his expertise by prominent Indian promoters, Indian and international companies, regulatory agencies, and large global funds investing in India. He advises large Indian conglomerates and multinational clients across sectors such as financial services, manufacturing, retail, infrastructure, steel, software, and information technology.

Haigreve has been consistently recommended by many of the world’s leading law chambers and accreditation bodies. Sources praise him as an “outstanding lawyer” who is “extremely good and very sharp when it comes to large transactions.” He has been acknowledged for his experience and expertise by:

- Asian Legal Business, which listed him among the top 15 M&A lawyers in Asia.  
- Chambers and Partners, naming him a “Star Individual” for M&A, an “Eminent Practitioner” for Private Equity, and a “Band 1 Lawyer” for Private Wealth Law.  
- The Legal 500, which recognized him as a “Leading Expert” for Corporate and M&A.  
- IFLR 1000, which named him a “Market‑Leading Lawyer” for Corporate M&A and Banking & Finance.  
- India Business Law Journal, which placed him among the top 100 lawyers in India.  
- Who’s Who Legal, which listed him as a “Thought Leader” for M&A and Governance in India.  
- AsiaLaw Profile, which described him as an “Elite Practitioner” for Corporate M&A and Private Equity.

Haigreve sits on the Board of Directors of several companies, including Jio Platforms Limited, Torrent Pharmaceuticals Ltd, Mahindra & Mahindra Limited, CEAT Limited, JSW Steel Limited, Tech Mahindra Limited, Inox Leisure Limited, and Borosil Renewables Limited.

Kishore Jayaraman  
President – India & South Asia, Rolls‑Royce  

Kishore Jayaraman is the President for Rolls‑Royce India and South Asia. Since taking over the role nine years ago, he has driven strategic focus and significant expansion across the Rolls‑Royce Defence, Civil Aerospace, and Power Systems businesses. A strong proponent of technology innovation, he led the establishment of Rolls‑Royce’s digital and engineering capabilities in India, which now contribute to global development projects.  

Prior to joining Rolls‑Royce, Kishore held diverse leadership roles across markets at GE for 23 years. His most recent assignment was as President and CEO of GE Energy, South Asia.

**Nitin Mantri**  
Group CEO, Avian WE  
Dynamic leader in communications. Serves as Group CEO of Avian WE and President of the International Communications Consultancy Organization (ICCO). Former President of the Public Relations Consultants Association of India (PRCAI) from 2015 to 2020. Recently appointed to the Global Advisory Board of PRCA and Co‑Chair of PRCA APAC and PRCA Global Ethics Council.

Nitin joined Avian WE (formerly Avian Media) as CEO in 2006. At that time the firm was two years old, operating out of a Delhi apartment with 10 employees and a handful of clients. Under Nitin’s leadership the company grew year‑on‑year, and today Avian WE employs over 200 people across six Indian offices. Its key clients include Airbnb, Amazon, Child Investment Fund Federation, Deloitte, Facebook, Intuit, Johnson & Johnson, Lockheed Martin, Mastercard, McDonald’s, Pernod Ricard, Philips, Reckitt, Samsung, Sony India, Viacom, and VMware.

Nitin’s more than two decades of experience across diverse sectors and his ability to mitigate crises and train C‑suite leaders have helped Avian secure long‑term clients. He and his business partner founded Chase India in 2011—now a subsidiary of Avian WE—to provide public‑policy consulting for Indian and multinational firms. Prior to Avian, Nitin worked at Brodeur/Pleon (now Ketchum), a Europe‑based public‑relations consultancy owned by Omnicom Group, in London, where he oversaw UK operations and led corporate and technology B2B practices. He began his communications career at Genesis PR (now BCW) after earning an MBA from the Birla Institute of Management Technology.

Nitin is actively involved in advancing the public‑relations industry. Since becoming president of the PRCAI in 2015, he has focused on improving the profession’s public perception in India. As president of ICCO, he works to make the global PR industry relevant to diverse markets and to nurture new talent. He regularly speaks at the AMEC Global Summit, ICCO Global Summit, and Sabre Awards, and teaches courses at prominent mass‑communication colleges in India to raise awareness of the field at the grassroots level.

**Ms. Satyavati (Satya) Berera – Chief Operating Officer, PwC India**

- 35+ years of diverse experience across multiple sectors; a key member of PwC India’s leadership team.  
- Career path: Article trainee (1980) → Audit Partner (1995) → COO (2016).  
- Held senior leadership roles such as Consulting Leader, Risk Advisory Services Leader, and Managing Partner for PwC’s North region.  
- Expertise spans Assurance, Consulting, and Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) services.  
- Known for designing and implementing control‑strengthening and process‑improvement solutions for a range of Indian and multinational companies.  
- As COO, she oversees firm operations and drives PwC India’s transformation agenda.

For the last several years, Satya has been closely associated with Mobile Creches, a not‑for‑profit organisation devoted to the education of young children from marginalised communities. Satya graduated from Lady Shri Ram College in Economics and is a Fellow member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI).

Sunil Goyal is the Deputy Chief Executive Officer of Sopra Steria India, part of the €4.3 billion Sopra Steria Group headquartered in Paris. The group is one of the top five French IT services companies, with over 46,000 professionals worldwide across 30 countries.

Sunil began his career as a telecom support professional at Unitel, then managed sales at Wipro. He co‑founded Momentum India in 1993 with almost no seed capital. Under his leadership, the company became the largest channel partner for Wipro InfoTech in India and raised USD 1.5 million from Citibank when diversifying into software in 2000. Momentum was acquired by Sopra Group in July 2005.

At Sopra Steria India, Sunil oversees all functions, including software delivery, HR, IT, finance, and administration. The company operates centers in Noida, Chennai, Pune, and Bengaluru, employing over 4,200 people. He is passionate about people and has driven a high‑performance culture at the company.

Sunil Shah  
Chief Executive Officer, Societe Generale Global Solution Centre  
- Global Head of SG Global Solution Centres in India and Romania; CEO of SG Global Solution Centre in India.  
- Oversees operations in Bangalore, Chennai, and Bucharest, delivering IT, banking operations, finance, and research services to SG’s entities worldwide.  
- 25+ years of experience in IT and BFSI.  
- Joined SG Global Solution Centre in 2011; appointed CEO in 2018.  
- Prior roles: led the Asset Management IT team at Fidelity for over seven years; worked at Infosys for more than a decade managing large programs for financial‑services firms and consulting assignments across India and globally.  
- Education: B.Sc. in Computer Science Engineering, University of Mysore; MBA, INSEAD (Singapore & France).  

Additional roles:  
- Chief Executive Officer, Sopra Banking Software Solutions India Pvt. Ltd. (SBSI), a subsidiary of the Sopra Steria Group.  
- SBSI employs 900+ software professionals and develops/supports banking software sold in Europe, Africa, and the Middle East.  
- Former Co‑Chair of NASSCOM’s regional council at Noida.  

Thomas Kuruvilla  
Managing Partner, Arthur D. Little – Middle East  

**Mr. Thomas Kuruvilla**  
Managing Partner, Arthur D. Little – Middle East  

Thomas Kuruvilla is a member of the Global Board of Arthur D. Little and oversees the firm’s operations in the Middle East. He also serves as Head of the Strategy and Organization practice in South Asia and the Middle East. Since joining in 1998, he has led Arthur D. Little’s Singapore operations and spearheaded numerous public‑sector transformational engagements in economic development and digital transformation across industries such as transport, telecoms, health care, and real‑estate/smart city. He has supported family conglomerates in the Middle East and serves on their boards.

**Academic and advisory roles**  
- Part‑time faculty at IIM Ahmedabad, Bangalore, and Udaipur, teaching programmes on International Business Strategy, Family Conglomerate Strategy & Governance, and Strategy/Management Consulting.  
- Member of the Industry Advisory Panel at S P Jain School of Global Management.  
- Speaker at conferences on Corporate Governance, International Business Strategy, Turnaround Management, and Corporate Venturing.

**Consulting specialities**  
Strategy development & implementation, organization evaluation & design, due diligence, mergers & acquisitions, turnaround and performance management across public and private sectors—including family conglomerates. Industries: Telecoms, Maritime, Financial Services. Functional areas: Strategy & Organization, M&A, BPR.

Mr Kuruvilla holds a Bachelor’s degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from Kerala University and an MBA from the Melbourne Business School, Australia, and the Asian Institute of Management, Philippines. Before joining Arthur D. Little, he worked with Larsen & Toubro, India’s largest engineering company. Thomas was also selected as a Pilot Officer with the Indian Air Force.

**Mr Vikas Vasal** – Partner and Global Leader – Tax, Grant Thornton International Ltd.  
He is the Global Head of Tax and National Managing Partner – Tax for Grant Thornton India. Vasal brings more than 25 years of experience in business, tax and regulatory matters across industries. From 2011 to 2014, he worked with a large consulting firm in New York, USA, on the International Tax Team. He serves on the National Executive Committee for Tax and is active in business chambers such as CII and FICCI. Vasal is a frequent speaker at business and professional conferences in India and abroad on economic policy, tax and regulatory issues, and contributes regularly to leading business newspapers. He is a Chartered Accountant and Cost Accountant, and an alumnus of Shri Ram College of Commerce.

**Mr Vineet Vij** – General Counsel, Tech Mahindra.  
He provides tactical and practical vision that is vital to corporate decision‑making while preserving the overall legal health of Tech Mahindra and its group companies worldwide.

**Role description**

Vineet is currently serving as Global General Counsel of Tech Mahindra Limited, a US$ 5.3 billion Indian MNC that provides IT, ITeS, consulting, and BPO services worldwide. As the corporate officer in charge of the legal function, he manages the entire legal and regulatory affairs of Tech Mahindra and its group and portfolio companies globally, and advises on and supports multi‑billion cross‑border commercial transactions, mergers & acquisitions, disputes & litigation, ethics & global compliance, legal policy & regulatory affairs, IPR, and strategic legal support.

**Areas of expertise**

With over 25 years of experience, Vineet’s practice spans a wide range of areas: commercial & contract law; litigation & dispute resolution; cross‑border mergers & acquisitions; global regulatory compliance; financial & banking; real estate and SEZ & STPI regulations; company law; IT, data protection, privacy, cyber & IPR law; criminal law; telecom; litigation & arbitration; employment law; bankruptcy law; competition law; corporate affairs; and indirect taxation law.

Professional experience  

Vineet began his legal career practicing in the Delhi High Court and various district courts. He then served as in‑house counsel for Punj Lloyd Group in a secretarial‑cum‑legal capacity, where he was exposed to many facets of law that became a significant part of his responsibilities.  

He spent eight years with the Tata Group (Tata Unisys, Tata Infotech, TCS), working closely with Tata Sons and Tata Services, gaining exposure to global deals and transactions. At American Express, he tackled complex regulatory, legal, and compliance issues in the banking, finance, and NBFC sectors.  

For over a decade, Vineet worked at HCL Technologies, handling billion‑dollar contracts, complex litigations and arbitrations, establishing SEZ/STPI/Free‑trade Zones, ensuring global compliance, resolving customs and indirect taxation issues, filing patents, copyrights, trademarks, and addressing IPR matters. He also managed public affairs functions and provided strategic legal assistance for HCL Corporation and Foundation, and supported HCL’s entry into the software business segment—an unprecedented IP acquisition in the Indian IT/ITeS space.  

Currently, he serves as Group General Counsel for Tech Mahindra and its global group companies. He leads a team of 100 lawyers spread worldwide, overseeing legal and regulatory matters for the Tech Mahindra Group, portfolio companies, Tech Mahindra Foundation, and its CSR activities. He acts as a bulwark against legal challenges, facilitating exponential business growth while mitigating risk and ensuring compliance with country laws and legal obligations.

**Pro Bono Work**

Vineet believes that his role as a Group General Counsel extends beyond handling legal affairs for the Tech Mahindra Group. He actively works to alleviate social imperils by providing legal advice and ensuring access to justice for marginalized communities, addressing the lack of legal aid and resources among these groups.

As the Group General Counsel for Tech Mahindra, Vineet feels it is an important responsibility for the entire team to contribute toward the overall organizational goal of undertaking CSR activities. He has fully espoused the company’s CSR policy and works tirelessly to make the CSR activities a success and a longstanding initiative. He also drives members of the legal team to participate in volunteering in the company’s CSR activities, in addition to their individual initiatives in line with legal aid and awareness.

**Computer Science Engineering**
- B.Tech Computer Science Engineering  
  *Unlock next generation computing technologies…*  
  *Why B.Tech Computer Science Engineering from Bennett University*

**B.Tech Computer Science Engineering – Bennett University**

- **Program Overview**  
  B.Tech in Computer Science Engineering (CSE) at Bennett University is a forward‑looking, industry‑driven and research‑oriented program that equips students with the skills needed for emerging technologies such as AI, advanced communications, and innovative healthcare solutions.

- **Rankings & Reputation**  
  • 2nd best B.Tech program in India (Times Engineering Ranking 2020)

- **Placements**  
  • Highest package: Rs. 1.37 Cr./yr  
  • Average package: Rs. 9.42 Lac/yr  
  • Recruiters include Adobe, Amazon, Meta, Google, Microsoft, Deloitte, and more

- **Facilities**  
  • Futuristic high‑end research laboratories in partnership with Dell EMC, Amazon, Apple, and NVIDIA

- **Start‑up & Innovation Culture**  
  • Students develop start‑ups in FinTech, Blockchain, Travel, and EdTech under the mentorship of Bennett Hatchery

- **Research & Projects**  
  • Project‑based learning with live projects, research paper writing, and publication under faculty mentorship

- **Faculty**  
  • Approximately 100 % of faculty hold PhDs from IITs and other acclaimed institutions

- **Admissions 2025**  
  • The 2025 cohort will welcome a diverse group of aspirants eager to empower the world through technology

**About the Program**  
B.Tech Computer Science Engineering at Bennett University is positioned as a premier engineering education that blends cutting‑edge research, industry exposure, and a vibrant start‑up ecosystem to produce leaders in technology.

**B.Tech Computer Science Engineering**

**Course Overview**  
The curriculum is forward‑looking and industry‑aligned, covering futuristic technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Robotics, Machine Learning (ML), Internet of Things (IoT), Genetic Research, Bioinformatics, Big Data, DevOps, Blockchain, Augmented & Virtual Reality, Drones, Quantum Computing, Product Design Technology, Cyber Security, Full Stack Development, Cloud Computing, Automation, Gaming, and more. The program balances theoretical concepts with hands‑on training, designed around the latest market trends, industry needs, and research outcomes.

**Program Details**  
- **Duration:** 4 years  
- **Specializations:** 16  
- **Credits:** 160  
- **Start Date:** August/September 2026 (tentative)

**Placement Highlights**  
- **Companies:** 450+ for placements  
- **Highest Package:** ₹1.37 Cr per year  
- **Top 10 % Placement Package:** 21.84 LPA  
- **Above 10 LPA:** 3 B.Tech students  
- **Highest Internship Stipend:** ₹4.2 Lac per month

**Board of Studies**

The Board comprises industry and academia representatives from companies such as Google Research, SenRa, Terra Drone, RedBay, Lenovo, Automation Anywhere, Ui Path, AWS Academy, Unity Technologies, Simulanis, Yepper, White Bears Studio, Probability Gaming, Scientific Games, NVIDIA, Aindra Systems, IBM India, Sense AI, Here Tech, TCS, Towards Blockchain, Bankchainasset.com, DigiLedge, QoS Technologies Pvt Ltd, Data Security Council of India, and Empass Learning.

**Faculty**

All faculty members hold Ph.D. degrees from premier institutions and possess extensive international exposure.

**Curriculum Overview**

- Designed in consultation with industry experts (Google Research, NVIDIA, IBM India, TCS, AWS Academy).  
- Offers 14 specialisations.  
- Flexible and modular options for minors, honours, open electives, and audit courses.  
- Internship opportunities: 8‑week, 16‑week, and one‑year final‑year internships.  
- Students may participate in competitions, hackathons, clubs, societies, and project‑based experiential learning programmes.

**Curriculum Years**

- B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2025‑29  
- B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2024‑28  
- B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2023‑27  
- B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2022‑26

- 2019‑23  
- 2018‑22  

**Specialisation Electives**  
- Each specialisation offers its own list of electives.  
- Students may choose up to three electives from their specialisation list.  

**Minors**  
- A pack of 4 courses providing interdisciplinary exposure.  
- Offered between the 3rd and 6th semesters, one credit per semester.  
- Total: 16 credits.  

**Honours**  
- A pack of 6 courses that deepen knowledge in the same or other CSE specialisations.  
- Offered between the 4th and 7th semesters: two courses in the 5th & 6th semesters, one each in the 4th and 7th semesters.  
- Total: 18 credits.  

**Open Electives**  
- University‑wide courses from different schools (Law, Media, Management, Engineering, CSET).  
- Popular options include Mobile Journalism, Start‑ups, and Copyright Law.  
- Total: 12 credits.  

**Specialisations**  
- **Artificial Intelligence**  
  - Curriculum designed with input from CEOs/CXOs of NVIDIA, Aindra, Here Tech, Amazon, and others.  
  - Electives: Cognitive Modelling, Advanced Computer Vision and Video Analytics, Reinforcement Learning, Image and Video Processing, Natural Language Processing.  
  - Partnerships with NVIDIA, SAP, Hitachi, and Times Internet enable cutting‑edge projects.  

- **Cloud Computing**  
  - (No additional details provided.)

**Cloud Computing**

Cloud Computing is in high market demand for its scalability, on‑demand solutions, and flexible pricing. To meet this demand, the university offers specialised electives and certification options, including:
- Cloud Services Development and Operations  
- Git and GitHub for Version Control  
- AWS Cloud Support Associate  
- Developing Solutions for Microsoft Azure  
- Google Associate Cloud Engineer  
- …and other related courses.

---

**Blockchain**

The Blockchain specialisation curriculum was developed in consultation with CEOs and CXOs of BankchainAsset.com, Digiledge, and other subject‑matter experts. Electives offered as part of the B.Tech syllabus include:
- Blockchain Technologies  
- Smart Contracts and Solidity Programming  
- Digital Currencies and Blockchain  
- Modern Cryptography  
- …and additional topics.

Samyak Jain (B.Tech CSE 2017‑21) launched *Instadapp*, a start‑up for managing blockchain‑based assets, and secured $1.4 million in funding in 2019.

---

**Cyber Security**

With growing dependence on online transactions and social activities, there is a high demand for robust cyber‑security frameworks. The curriculum, designed by CEOs and CXOs from the Data Security Council of India (DSCI), QoS Technologies, IBM, and other researchers, covers:
- Cyber Security with Blockchain  
- Malware Analysis for Mobile Devices  
- Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics  
- Penetration Testing and Ethical Hacking  
- …and other advanced topics.

To provide students with practical experience, the university has partnered with DSCI, Fortinet, CompTIA, and Cisco for cutting‑edge projects.

**Data Science**

Data scientists play a key role in smart cities, the automation industry, BFSI, and Fintech. Bennett University has developed a curriculum with input from CEOs and CXOs of Microsoft, Google, TCS, and other researchers. The curriculum includes elective courses on:
- Structural Equation Modelling  
- Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics  
- Data Visualisation  
- Big Data Analytics and Business Intelligence  
- Satellite Data Analytics  

**Gaming**

As of 2022, the gaming industry is worth $200 billion and encompasses dozens of job disciplines and thousands of exciting jobs worldwide. Its significance to culture, entertainment, and technological growth makes it one of the most interesting sectors in today’s tech landscape.

**Robotic Process Automation**

This specialisation teaches students to use software‑based tools that automate manual, rule‑based human tasks. CEOs and CXOs from Automation Anywhere and UIPath helped design the curriculum. Elective courses include:
- Advanced Robotic Process Automation  
- Digital Process Automation Architecture  
- Enterprise Process Automation Framework  

Students also have access to a Bot Lab by Automation Anywhere and a Speech Devices Lab by Amazon.

**DevOps**

*(No additional content provided.)*

DevOps  
DevOps practices help merge development, quality assurance, and operations (deployment and integration) into a single, continuous set of processes. The key topics covered in this specialisation comprise Introduction to Advanced DevOps concepts, Cloud Services, Integration, Deployment and Testing, among others. Students get the opportunity to study advanced subjects such as Cloud Services Development and Operations, Software Craftsmanship in DevOps, Test Automation in DevOps, Agile Software Development, and many others from the choice of subjects available as specialisation electives.

Full Stack  
The specialisation helps students understand the industry trends on accelerating the processes of design, development, and deployment. The specialisation electives available for Full Stack are Front‑End Web UI Frameworks and Tools: Bootstrap, Front‑End Web UI Frameworks, and Tools: React, Mobile App Development with React Native, and Application Development with Cloud Platforms, among many others.

Drones  
The drone specialisation builds critical thinking and problem‑solving skills in addition to technical skills, which benefit students along any career path they choose. Students who pick up the specialisation can contribute to safe infrastructure maintenance, law‑enforcement support, and agriculture management.

**Quantum Computing**

Today, industries, academia, private and government organisations around the world are spending billions of dollars on quantum research and development. Leading companies such as Google, Microsoft, IBM, Air‑Bus, and Intel are racing to achieve quantum supremacy. In line with this, Bennett University has designed a curriculum for Quantum Computing that covers topics including Quantum Computing Foundations; Machine Learning with Quantum Computing; Quantum Computing for Data Analysis; Quantum Cryptography; Quantum Information Theory; Quantum Computer Architecture; Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics; and Cloud Infrastructure and Services.

**IoT and Robotics**

IoRT is not simply IoT aided by robotics or robotics driven by IoT, but rather an amalgamation of the two disciplines. It evolved from the realization that these fields overlap significantly and can greatly benefit from a combined focus. Robotics involves both hardware and software components, and understanding both is particularly valuable. Robotics is a holistic process of designing an autonomously working structure that logically comprehends its surroundings and responds to them.

**Augmented & Virtual Reality**

Augmented & Virtual Reality

The market wants professionals who can use Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR) and Extended Reality (ER) to superimpose the virtual environment on the physical world. To prepare the students for it, Bennett University has brought CEOs and CXOs of well‑known organisations such as Simulanis, Yeppar, Whitebears Studio, Probability Gaming, Scientific Games to design a suitable curriculum. Courses on VR Gaming, VR and 360, Video Production, 3D Interaction Design in Virtual Reality and Game Mechanics are offered as part of the Specialisation Electives. For project development, resources such as Apple Mac Lab, Nao Robot V6, and other head‑mounted devices are available within the campus. Students completing the course will become market‑ready for jobs such as AR‑VR game developer, UX/UI Designer, mixed‑reality artist and technical 3D artist.

Product Design Technology

The specialisation brings together the creativity and practicality needed to bridge the gap between design engineering and product design. Product design technology empowers students to create and visualise innovative concepts and transform those concepts into working products. The specialisation focuses on imparting technical skills such as using computational analysis to design the structure of products.

Mobile Technologies

Mobile Technologies specialization encompasses iOS technology. Swift is a powerful and intuitive programming language for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, and watchOS. Writing Swift code is interactive and fun; the syntax is concise yet expressive, and Swift includes modern features developers love. Swift code is safe by design and produces software that runs lightning‑fast.

UI/UX

Course Outcome

Students are expected to develop the ability to apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, and engineering appropriate to the discipline.

**Course Outcomes**

- Students are expected to develop the ability to apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, and engineering appropriate to the discipline.  
- Students are expected to apply mathematical foundations, algorithmic principles, and computer‑science theory in modelling, design, and conduct of experiments, as well as in data interpretation and analysis.  
- Students are expected to develop the ability to identify, formulate, and solve real engineering problems and understand the global impact of engineering solutions.  

**Projects**

- In the 8th semester, students must take up either an Industrial Project, an R&D project, or a start‑up project worth 12 credits.  
- A Capstone Project of 5 credits in the 7th semester is an advanced course to prepare the students for industry.  

**Internships**

- Two internships of 8 and 16 weeks are part of the B.Tech CSE syllabus.  
- The 8‑week internship is available after the 3rd year, during summer vacation.  
- The 16‑week internship is available in the last semester.  
- Students can optionally do additional internships at the end of the 1st year and the 2nd year during summer vacations.  
- There is also an option for the students to spend one year in an industry internship in the final year of study.  
- B.Tech CSE students have interned at Microsoft India, Amazon, Adobe India, Here Maps, Innovaccer, and many more.  

**Skills**

- Strong sense of professionalism  
- Leadership  
- Soft skills  
- Group discussions  
- Public speaking

---

**Bennett University Highlights**

- Deep industry engagement hones essential skills for a competitive work environment.  
- Provides access to education and career opportunities, producing qualified experts needed in the ICT industry.  
- Three B.Tech. CSE students secured internships at ₹14 lakh per annum.

Bennett University is a member of Dell EMC’s External Research and Academic Alliance Program.  
The Dell EMC‑Data Analytics Lab houses 32 Dell machines with the latest configuration and provides faculty training, collaborative projects and exclusive workshops.

The partnership focuses on developing systems thinking, problem‑solving skills and lifelong learning in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning. Over 860 B.Tech CSE students have been certified on Microsoft technology.

Students receive industry‑recognized Fortinet certification opportunities, equipping them to protect networks from global cyber threats.

Through the AWS Educate program more than 450 students gained hands‑on experience with AWS Cloud Services (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) via 50+ industry‑ready courses on the AWS Educate portal. Additionally, 400+ students completed the AWS Academy Cloud Foundation Course powered by Amazon Web Services.

Amazon (AA) has established a BotLab in the CSE department and trained faculty to create Robotic Process Automation bots for various processes.

Bennett University has been selected as a host institution/business incubator under the Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) scheme “Support for Entrepreneurial and Managerial Development of SMEs through Incubators”.

The alliance promotes research collaborations, admissions, and student/faculty mobility across the School of Law, Media, Engineering, and Management.

Its focus is on developing AI models, solutions, case studies, and joint research. Drishya AI will provide projects and internships to students.

NVIDIA: Bennett‑NVIDIA Centre of Excellence for AI, featuring an NVIDIA supercomputer for deep learning on campus. Five CSE faculty are NVIDIA Ambassadors, leading training on AI technologies.

**Cisko**  
Access to education and career opportunities and provision of the adequate number of qualified experts in demand within the ICT industry. Three B.Tech. CSE students were selected for an internship at Rs 14 lac per annum.  

**Dell**  
Bennett University is a member of Dell EMC’s External Research and Academic Alliance Program. The Dell EMC‑Data Analytics Lab contains 32 Dell machines with the latest configuration. Dell supports faculty training, collaborative projects and exclusive workshops.  

**Microsoft**  
The partnership focuses on developing and strengthening systems thinking, problem‑solving skills and readiness for lifelong learning in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning. Over 860 B.Tech. CSE students have been certified on Microsoft technology.  

**Fortinet**  
Provides industry‑recognised Fortinet certification opportunities to students, equipping them with in‑demand skills for protecting networks from global cyber threats.  

**AWS**  
Through the AWS Educate program, more than 450 students benefited from hands‑on AWS Cloud Services (IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS) by exploring over 50 industry‑ready courses hosted on the AWS Educate portal. Over 400 students completed the AWS Academy Cloud Foundation Course powered by Amazon Web Services.  

**Automation Anywhere**  
Automation Anywhere has set up a BotLab in the CSE department and trained faculty to create Robotic Process Automation bots for various processes.  

**MSME**  
Bennett University has been selected as a Host Institution/Business Incubator under the MSME (Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises), Government of India, to carry out the scheme “Support for Entrepreneurial and Managerial Development of SMEs through Incubators”.

**B.Tech Computer Science Engineering – Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process**

**Eligibility**

*Domestic*  
- Passed XII standard from any recognised education board with Physics, Mathematics, and any other third subject, with a minimum of 55% marks.  
- OR IB Board with a minimum score of 24 credits, 3 higher‑level and 3 standard‑level subjects.

*NRI*  

**Eligibility for NRI admission**  
- Pass with at least **60 % aggregate marks** in Grade 12th / A Level or equivalent, including **Physics & Mathematics**.  
- Meet the **English Language Requirement (ELR)**.

**Selection criteria**  
- Selection is based on the merit of the **Class XII “Best of 3” subjects** score.  
- The three subjects must be **Physics** and **Mathematics** (compulsory) plus the **highest‑scoring third subject** (excluding Hindi).  
- The third subject must **not be vocational** (e.g., Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc.).

---

### B.Tech Computer Science Engineering – Frequently Asked Questions

**1. How can a student obtain an Honors degree?**  
- Pursuing an honors B.Tech gives a definite advantage over a general B.Tech.  
- Students may earn an **additional 18 credits** beyond the regular B.Tech curriculum.  
- Any student can earn honors credits on top of meeting the regular degree requirements, provided they meet the qualifying criteria through academic performance.  
- Selection of honors candidates is made solely by the Department, based on an assessment of the student’s strengths and capabilities.

**2. What are the eligibility and requirements for pursuing an Honors degree?**  
- The student must have a **CGPA of at least 7.5** and **no backlog courses**.

**3. What are the eligibility and requirements to pursue minors?**  
- Minor credits are earned through electives available across various departments.  
- Pre‑requisites vary by course and are determined by the department offering the course.

**4. How does a minor benefit in a student’s career?**  
- (Answer not provided in the source.)

**How does a minor benefit in a student’s career?**  
A minor is a combination of courses or electives taken during university that are outside the student’s primary department. These courses broaden a student’s understanding of the world by exposing them to a wide range of subjects and practical areas, which is essential for success in the future.

**Can an undergraduate student take part in research activities?**  
Bennett University emphasizes research, experiential, and applied learning. Undergraduate students are encouraged to collaborate with faculty on live research projects, giving them insight into the research process and deepening their knowledge.

**What are the options available to 8th‑semester students?**  
8th‑semester BTech students can either pursue an industry internship or launch a startup if they have an entrepreneurial idea. They also have the option to take courses with partner universities for a Master’s program. This flexibility helps students prepare for the future and chart a clear roadmap.

**Do you assist students in placements, summer internships, and industry tours?**  
Bennett University’s Career Services Centre works closely with industry partners to facilitate guest lectures, curricular input, joint program development, internships, and placements.

**To which companies have students gone for summer internships?**

**Summer Internship Destinations**

BTech students from various programs have secured summer internships with companies such as:

Microsoft, HP Enterprise, CommVault, Sourcefuse, Suzlon, Yamaha, BHEL, DMRC, CDAC, Google, Here Maps, Honda Motorcycle, Indigo, Reliance Jio, IBM, Saint Gobain, C&S Electric, Daikin, GAIL (India) Ltd., GMR, Gumption Labs, Hitachi, JK Paper Ltd., LG, Mobikon, Safexpress, iSMRITI (IIT Kanpur), JCB, Yamaha Motors, Xen.Ai, Hindustan Coca Cola Beverages, Irtish Consulting, Leadingindia.ai, Kennis, Tata Advanced System, Tata Power, Cloud Fabrica, and Tata Consultancy Services.

BBA

Why BBA Programme from Bennett University

BBA being a thorough professional programme, take students through an engaging journey of performing managerial, leadership roles.

Top BBA colleges in Delhi nurture versatility through their curriculum and course structure. There are several attributes including industry exposure, curating business strategy and much more.

Top BBA colleges in Delhi nurture versatility through their curriculum and course structure. There are several attributes including industry exposure, curating business strategy and much more.

Bennett University, being regarded as best colleges for BBA in India make students’ lives streamlined makes them learn sustainable efforts to run an organisation or business.

3rd Best BBA Program in India

Times BBA Ranking 2020

BBA & 7 Specialisations

Future Focussed Curriculum

Curriculum mapped to the top 10 job skills of 2025, as suggested by World Economic Forum in 2020

Internships & Industry Exposure

Corporate internship of two months between 2nd and 3rd year

Projects, Industry Visits & Expert Lectures to interact with the corporate leaders

Partnership with ACCA, UK

Collaborated with ACCA, UK, to start a new integrated BBA-ACCA specialization

9 Papers are exempted out of 13 Papers from the rigorous ACCA exams.

The student can complete ACCA as early as the sixth semester.

Foreign University Admissions

More than 30% BBA students take admission in reputed international universities

2021 BBA batch students got selected for Masters in top 100 Global Universities like University of Texas (USA), University of Liverpool (UK), Singapore Management University (Singapore), University of Greenwich (UK), to name a few.

ABOUT

Leap in Business with BBA

BBA program prepares students to develop the three most essential skills:

Critical Thinking

People Handling

Active Learning

Being the best university for BBA in India, the varsity has mapped the curriculum to the top 10 job skills of 2025, as suggested by the World Economic Forum in 2020.

The program involves summer internships, industry visits, community projects, Discovery walks, and various opportunities to gain experience through value-added courses and General Electives offered by other schools.

BBA includes Credit Based Choice System (CBCS) and is in line with UGC’s classification of courses such as Ability Enhancement and Skill Enhancement Courses

Duration 3 Yrs. / 4 Yrs.

Credits 120

Credits 140 for Honours

Specialisation 7

Start Date Jul/Aug 2025 (Tentative)

Curriculum

The program's structure will help BBA students delve deep into their coursework, enabling them to uncover the program's future potential. The comprehensive curriculum will provide a roadmap for navigating the program effectively.

BBA Curriculum 2022-25

BBA Curriculum 2021-24

BBA Curriculum 2020-23

BBA Curriculum 2019-22

BBA Curriculum 2018-21

Specialisation

The BBA Programme offers a wide scope of Specialisation. The Specialisation courses start from the fourth semester.

Marketing

Marketing Specialisation in BBA Programme acquaints student to gain complete knowledge of marketing management. The key courses canvassed in this Specialisation are Integrated Marketing Communication, Sales and Distribution, Services Marketing, Product and Brand Management, Consumer Behaviour, among others.

Finance

Finance

BBA in Finance imparts comprehensive understanding of money, accounting, and advanced concepts to settle complex monetary issues. The primary courses covered under Finance Specialisation are Financial Modeling and Analytics, Security Analysis & Portfolio Management, Financial Markets, Financial Services, and Insurance, to name a few.

Entrepreneurship

Entrepreneurship Specialisation focuses to encourage a sturdy entrepreneurial attitude among students. This Specialisation allows students to learn specific knowledge and abilities required to formulate an entrepreneurial venture. Further, it lends the firmness to transform their innovative thoughts into new organizations. Courses under this Specialisation are Venture Creation Strategy and Simulation, Family Businesses Essentials, Entrepreneurial Marketing & Finance, Global Entrepreneurship.

International Business

BBA degree with a Specialisation in International Business is the most ideal for a competitor who needs to seek after higher studies/profession abroad. The course prepares future leaders to know about the business administration in a global environment. The course list for this Specialisation incorporates Emerging Markets in International Business, International Business Environment, EXIM Procedures and Documentation, International Finance, Cross-Cultural Management, International Finance, among others.

Business Analytics - Integrated with Institute of Analytics, UK

Business Analytics evolves information and train students on prescriptive, descriptive, and predictive business modelling abilities. These are the most recent apparatuses/strategies and software are used to analyse and solve issues for the implementation of changes and business development. The course list for this Specialisation incorporates includes Statistics with R, Simulations and Heuristics, SaS and Tableau, Python Programming, SQL, Machine Learning & AI, Social Media Analytics, Neural Networks an Deep Learning.

Human Resource Management

BBA in Human Resource Management is a Specialisation which focuses on helping students understand the recruitment process, managing people, and providing direction and guidance to the workforce. The course list for this Specialisation includes HR Tools and Techniques, Industrial Relations and Labour Laws, Leadership and Change Management, Recruitment and Selection, and Organizational Development.

International Finance and Accounting – Integrated with ACCA, UK

This Specialisation is designed in collaboration with the Association of Certified Chartered Accountants (ACCA), UK. This is one-of-a-kind specializations offered with ACCA certification as it integrates the curriculum of ACCA with the BBA degree. A student thereby saves time spent on pursuing ACCA certification on their own. If a student pursues this Specialisation, they can directly get exemption of nine ACCA courses in the certification. The course list includes Advanced Management Accounting, International Law, Financial Reporting, International Financial Management, Performance Management, Audit and Assurance, among others.

Honours

Honours can be earned by completing additional 20 credit courses. This consist of a Dissertation and Managerial Effectiveness Workshop.

Internships

The industry reconciliation of the Bennett BBA program permits each student to complete of two months corporate internship between the second and third year. Some of the prominent recruiters for internship have been Outlook Group, Bajaj Capital, Jindal Poly Films, Tenhard India Pvt, India, Pragyata Foundation, Axis Bank, SMC Group, to name a few.

Experience of Inclusive Environment

Experience of Inclusive Environment

Every BBA student experiences an opportunity for Discovery Walkthrough in which students can observe skilled and talented parts of the society that help them to get out-of-box thinking and applied innovation. Moreover, students go through a community service project that makes them aware of their social and ethical responsibilities and instils community values.

Skills

Some of the advanced management skills students will acquire:

Understanding the relevance of context in business and develop social consciousness

Critical thinking and problem-solving skills

integrative thinking ability

interpersonal skills and networking

Understanding their Leadership Potentials

Empathetic - Understand other cultures, and bring in wider perspective

Agile – Work with difference, and adapt to new environments

Influential – Build trust, and solve complex challenges

Having an entrepreneurial approach to every idea

Business communication and networking

ELIGIBILITY

BBA Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process

Eligibility

Domestic

Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects.

NRI

Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR)

Selection

Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score)
