OUR ASK

BBA Frequently Asked Questions

Q. Why should I pursue BBA degree from Bennett University?

The BBA program at Bennett University provides an international perspective combined with industry exposure to its students.

Ranked 3rd Best BBA Program in India, Times BBA Ranking 2020

Curriculum in sync with Top 10 Future Job Skills (as per World Economic Forum 2020)

Strong Business and Industry Connect with Times Network

Semester Exchange & Pathway Opportunity with partner foreign Universities

Live projects’ exposure and Strong Business and Industry Connect with Times Network

Mandatory two credit-based internships, i.e., an internship after the first year with a non-profit organization or a firm involved in social activities and an industry internship after the second year

Foreign languages like German and French are taught in the program to introduce global languages and cultures to the students

To help students develop an entrepreneurial mindset, there are compulsory and optional credit-based courses on Entrepreneurial Learning for all BBA students

Collaborated with ACCA, UK, to start a new integrated BBA-ACCA specialization

Q. What is the difference between BBA and B. Com?

BBA, a three-year undergraduate program, focuses on developing vital business skills. Apart from classroom learnings, BBA is backed by practical experiences that include summer internships, industry visits, presentations, conferences, and case studies. Thus, it aims to prepare students for both possibilities, entering the job market after graduation or going for higher education in the respective domain.

B. Com is a highly theoretical program, and the curriculum provides an intense look into the fundamental subjects of commerce like Accounts, Administration, Finance, Economics, and Industrial Policy.

Q. Name the specializations which are offered in BBA?

There are eight specializations which are available in BBA:

Marketing

Finance

Human Resource Management

Entrepreneurship

International Business

International Finance and Accounting – ACCA Certification

Business Analytics

Advertising & PR

Q. How can a student earn a BBA Honours Degree?

Bennett University provides a platform for students to earn an Honours degree along with a general BBA degree in addition of 20 credits. For BBA Honours: 120 Credits. This includes a Dissertation and Managerial Effectiveness Workshop.

Q. Is internship mandatory part of the program and who will arrange the same?

Internships are a mandatory part of the program. Students have to compulsorily do a corporate internship of two months between 2nd and 3rd year.

Q. Does BU have a start-up culture?

Thriving start-up Culture with Bennett Hatchery. 21 Crore funding received by students

C-Safekey, by Zaid Naim,

Q. Does BU have a start-up culture?

Thriving start-up Culture with Bennett Hatchery. 21 Crore funding received by students

C-Safekey, by Zaid Naim,

FIX Coffee by Aditya Jain,

Skill Arena by Hardik Aggarwal,

Gym Trainers by Nikhil Panwar,

Chocolate Box by Krishnammagri Samhita,

Reeyal by Reeya Sharma, and

Meera Scrochet by Ayush Arora are the testimonies of entrepreneurial culture at the University.

Q. What are the key highlights of Partnership with ACCA, UK?

Bennett University has partnered with ACCA, UK, to start a new integrated BBA-ACCA program. This will give students a very lucrative opportunity to pursue ACCA certification along with their BBA studies. Bennett BBA students will get six papers exempted from the rigorous ACCA exams and have to clear only the remaining seven. Even for those seven papers, the syllabi are integrated with the curriculum and will be taught as complete courses.

Benefits of BU BBA-ACCA:

£650 waived off per student

Enhanced enrolment opportunity for university admissions overseas

Invite Speakers & Mentors from other countries and corporates

100% Placement Assistance

Q. What are your current placements statistics for BBA?

Average Packagae: 5.16 LPA

Highest Package: 8.5 LPA

Companies: Moody’s Analytics, To The New, Tres Vista Financial, Aditya Birla Fashion & Retail, Publicis, TCS, ITC, D E Shaw, Clairvolex, Byjus, Bata India, HFFC, Ebix, Grant Thornton.

Q. What are the job prospects after completing BBA from Bennett University?

A plethora of job opportunities is available to students after pursuing BBA. Depending upon the area of specialization, the student can choose to build their career in their respective fields. BBA from Bennett University will position the students to become leaders, managers, and entrepreneurs capable of developing and leading sustainable business organizations

Q. Have BBA students able to secure their position at prestigious foreign universities?

Q. Have BBA students able to secure their position at prestigious foreign universities?

More than 30% BBA students take admission in reputed international universities. 2021 BBA batch students got selected for Masters in top 100 Global Universities like University of Texas (USA), University of Liverpool (UK), Singapore Management University (Singapore), University of Greenwich (UK), to name a few.

**Bennett University Programs**

**Undergraduate Programs**

- B.A. Mass Communication
- BA Film, TV & Web Series
- B. Des (Hons.) Fashion Design
- B. Des (Hons.) Intelligent Textile Design
- B. Des (Hons.) Product Design with AI
- B. Des (Hons.) Game Design
- B. Des (Hons.) Advanced Animation & VFX in partnership with CII
- B. Des (Hons.) Communication Design
- Integ. BBA LL.B. (Hons.)
- Integ. B.A. LL.B. (Hons.)

**Postgraduate Programs**

- M.A. Mass Communication
- PG Diploma in TV & Digital Journalism
- LL.M.

**Global Programs**

- BA Global – Film, TV & Web Series/Mass Comm. (2+1)
- Bachelor of Design (B. Des) Global - Fashion Design (2+2)

**Doctoral Program**

- Ph.D.

**BBA Program**

- Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA)
  - Ace in managerial & entrepreneurial skills
  - Apply Now

**BBA Program Details**

- Why BBA Programme from Bennett University: 
  - BBA being a thorough professional programme, take students through an engaging journey of performing managerial, leadership roles.
- Top BBA colleges in Delhi nurture versatility through their curriculum and course structure. 
  - Attributes include industry exposure, curating business strategy and much more.

Top BBA colleges in Delhi nurture versatility through their curriculum and course structure. Several attributes include industry exposure, curating business strategy, and more.

Bennett University, regarded as one of the best colleges for BBA in India, makes students' lives streamlined by teaching sustainable efforts to run an organisation or business.

**Program Details**

- Ranked 3rd Best BBA Program in India
- Times BBA Ranking 2020

**Key Features**

- BBA & 7 Specialisations
- Future Focussed Curriculum, mapped to the top 10 job skills of 2025, as suggested by World Economic Forum in 2020
- Internships & Industry Exposure:
  - Corporate internship of two months between 2nd and 3rd year
  - Projects, Industry Visits, & Expert Lectures to interact with corporate leaders
- Partnership with ACCA, UK:
  - Collaborated with ACCA, UK, to start a new integrated BBA-ACCA specialization
  - 9 Papers exempted out of 13 Papers from the rigorous ACCA exams
  - Students can complete ACCA as early as the sixth semester

**Career Opportunities**

- More than 30% BBA students take admission in reputed international universities
- 2021 BBA batch students got selected for Masters in top 100 Global Universities like:
  - University of Texas (USA)
  - University of Liverpool (UK)
  - Singapore Management University (Singapore)
  - University of Greenwich (UK)

**Program Overview**

The BBA program prepares students to develop the three most essential skills: Critical Thinking, People Handling, and Active Learning. The program involves summer internships, industry visits, community projects, Discovery walks, and various opportunities to gain experience through value-added courses and General Electives offered by other schools.

**BBA Program Details**

* Program includes Credit Based Choice System (CBCS) and aligns with UGC's classification of courses such as Ability Enhancement and Skill Enhancement Courses
* Duration: 3 Yrs. / 4 Yrs.
* Credits: 120 (140 for Honours)
* Specialisation: 7
* Start Date: Jul/Aug 2025 (Tentative)

**Placement Statistics**

* 60+ Companies for placements
* Highest Package Offered: 21.50 LPA
* Highest Package: 7.25 LPA
* Average Package: 5.33 LPA

Curriculum
- The program's structure will help BBA students delve deep into their coursework, enabling them to uncover the program's future potential.
- The comprehensive curriculum will provide a roadmap for navigating the program effectively.

BBA Curriculum
- 2022-25
- 2021-24
- 2020-23
- 2019-22
- 2018-21

Specialisation
- The BBA Programme offers a wide scope of Specialisation.
- The Specialisation courses start from the fourth semester.

Marketing Specialisation in BBA Programme
- acquaints students to gain complete knowledge of marketing management.
- The key courses canvassed in this Specialisation are :
  - Integrated Marketing Communication
  - Sales and Distribution
  - Services Marketing
  - Product and Brand Management
  - Consumer Behaviour
  - among others.

Finance Specialisation is mentioned but no details of it are available.

BBA in Finance imparts a comprehensive understanding of money, accounting, and advanced concepts to settle complex monetary issues. The primary courses covered under Finance Specialisation are:

1. Financial Modeling and Analytics
2. Security Analysis & Portfolio Management
3. Financial Markets
4. Financial Services
5. Insurance

Entrepreneurship Specialisation focuses on encouraging a sturdy entrepreneurial attitude among students. This Specialisation allows students to learn specific knowledge and abilities required to formulate an entrepreneurial venture. The courses under this Specialisation are:

1. Venture Creation Strategy and Simulation
2. Family Businesses Essentials
3. Entrepreneurial Marketing & Finance
4. Global Entrepreneurship

BBA degree with a Specialisation in International Business is ideal for a competitor who needs to pursue higher studies or a profession abroad. The course prepares future leaders to know about business administration in a global environment. The course list for this Specialisation includes:

1. Emerging Markets in International Business
2. International Business Environment
3. EXIM Procedures and Documentation
4. International Finance
5. Cross-Cultural Management
6. International Finance

Business Analytics, integrated with the Institute of Analytics, UK, evolves information and trains students on prescriptive, descriptive, and predictive business modelling abilities. The course list for this Specialisation includes:

1. Statistics with R
2. Simulations and Heuristics
3. SaS and Tableau
4. Python Programming
5. SQL
6. Machine Learning & AI
7. Social Media Analytics
8. Neural Networks and Deep Learning.

BBA in Human Resource Management

This Specialisation focuses on helping students understand the recruitment process, managing people, and providing direction and guidance to the workforce. Key courses include:

- HR Tools and Techniques
- Industrial Relations and Labour Laws
- Leadership and Change Management
- Recruitment and Selection
- Organizational Development

International Finance and Accounting – Integrated with ACCA, UK

This Specialisation is designed in collaboration with the Association of Certified Chartered Accountants (ACCA), UK. It integrates the curriculum of ACCA with the BBA degree, offering a one-of-a-kind specialisation with ACCA certification. By pursuing this Specialisation, students can save time and get exemption of nine ACCA courses in the certification. Key courses include:

- Advanced Management Accounting
- International Law
- Financial Reporting
- International Financial Management
- Performance Management
- Audit and Assurance

Honours

Honours can be earned by completing an additional 20 credit courses. This consists of a Dissertation and a Managerial Effectiveness Workshop.

Internships

The industry reconciliation of the Bennett BBA program allows students to complete a two-month corporate internship between the second and third year. Prominent recruiters for internships include:

- Outlook Group
- Bajaj Capital
- Jindal Poly Films
- Tenhard India Pvt, India
- Pragyata Foundation
- Axis Bank
- SMC Group

Experience of Inclusive Environment

Every BBA student experiences an opportunity for Discovery Walkthrough in which students can observe skilled and talented parts of the society that help them to get out-of-box thinking and applied innovation. Moreover, students go through a community service project that makes them aware of their social and ethical responsibilities and instils community values.

**Key Skills Acquired:**

- Understanding the relevance of context in business and develop social consciousness
- Critical thinking and problem-solving skills
- Integrative thinking ability
- Interpersonal skills and networking
- Understanding their Leadership Potentials
- Empathetic - Understand other cultures, and bring in wider perspective
- Agile – Work with difference, and adapt to new environments
- Influential – Build trust, and solve complex challenges
- Having an entrepreneurial approach to every idea
- Business communication and networking

**Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process**

**Eligibility:** 
- Domestic: 
  Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects.
  
- NRI: 
  Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR)

**Selection:** 
Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score)

Student Testimonials

Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score)

BBA Program

Ranked 3rd Best BBA Program in India, Times BBA Ranking 2020

Curriculum in sync with Top 10 Future Job Skills (as per World Economic Forum 2020)

Strong Business and Industry Connect with Times Network

Key Features:

- Semester Exchange & Pathway Opportunity with partner foreign Universities
- Live projects’ exposure and Strong Business and Industry Connect with Times Network
- Mandatory two credit-based internships, i.e., an internship after the first year with a non-profit organization or a firm involved in social activities and an industry internship after the second year

**Program Features and Information**

Foreign languages like German and French are taught in the program to introduce global languages and cultures to the students.

To help students develop an entrepreneurial mindset, there are compulsory and optional credit-based courses on Entrepreneurial Learning for all BBA students.

**Collaboration and Specialization**

The university collaborated with ACCA, UK, to start a new integrated BBA-ACCA specialization.

**Program Comparison and Details**

Q. What is the difference between BBA and B. Com?

BBA, a three-year undergraduate program, focuses on developing vital business skills. It includes practical experiences such as summer internships, industry visits, presentations, conferences, and case studies. This prepares students for both entering the job market after graduation and going for higher education in the respective domain.

B. Com is a highly theoretical program, providing an intense look into the fundamental subjects of commerce like Accounts, Administration, Finance, Economics, and Industrial Policy.

**BBA Specializations**

Q. Name the specializations which are offered in BBA?

There are eight specializations available in BBA:

1. Marketing
2. Finance
3. Human Resource Management
4. Entrepreneurship
5. International Business
6. International Finance and Accounting – ACCA Certification
7. Business Analytics
8. Advertising & PR

**Honours Degree and Internship**

Q. How can a student earn a BBA Honours Degree?

Bennett University provides a platform for students to earn an Honours degree along with a general BBA degree, in addition to 20 credits. For BBA Honours: 120 Credits. This includes a Dissertation and Managerial Effectiveness Workshop.

Q. Is internship mandatory part of the program and who will arrange the same?

Internships are a mandatory part of the program. Students have to compulsorily do a corporate internship of two months between 2nd and 3rd year.

**Start-up Culture**

Q. Does BU have a start-up culture?

Yes, the university has a thriving start-up culture with Bennett Hatchery. Students have received 21 Crore funding, with examples such as C-Safekey, by Zaid Naim.

**Bennett University's Entrepreneurial Culture and Academic Programs**

**Start-up Culture:**
Bennett University has a thriving start-up culture with Bennett Hatchery, which has received 21 crore in funding from students. Notable examples of entrepreneurial ventures include:

- C-Safekey by Zaid Naim
- FIX Coffee by Aditya Jain
- Skill Arena by Hardik Aggarwal
- Gym Trainers by Nikhil Panwar
- Chocolate Box by Krishnammagri Samhita
- Reeyal by Reeya Sharma
- Meera Scrochet by Ayush Arora

**Partnership with ACCA, UK:**
Bennett University has partnered with ACCA, UK to offer a new integrated BBA-ACCA program. This program provides students with:

- Opportunity to pursue ACCA certification alongside BBA studies
- Exemption from six ACCA papers
- Integrated syllabi for the remaining seven papers
- Benefits including:
  - £650 waived off per student
  - Enhanced enrolment opportunity overseas
  - Invite speakers and mentors from other countries and corporates
  - 100% Placement Assistance

**BBA Placements Statistics:**
- Average Package: 5.16 LPA
- Highest Package: 8.5 LPA
- Companies: 
  - Moody’s Analytics
  - To The New
  - Tres Vista Financial
  - Aditya Birla Fashion & Retail
  - Publicis
  - TCS
  - ITC
  - D E Shaw
  - Clairvolex
  - Byjus
  - Bata India
  - HFFC
  - Ebix
  - Grant Thornton

**Job Prospects after Completing BBA:**
BBA from Bennett University positions students as leaders, managers, and entrepreneurs capable of developing and leading sustainable business organizations. Students can choose to build their careers in their respective fields, depending on their area of specialization.

BBA Student Admissions and Success Stories

Q. Have BBA students able to secure their position at prestigious foreign universities?

Yes, more than 30% of BBA students take admission in reputed international universities. In 2021, BBA batch students got selected for Masters in top 100 Global Universities like:

- University of Texas (USA)
- University of Liverpool (UK)
- Singapore Management University (Singapore)
- University of Greenwich (UK)

2nd Best BTech Program in India

Times Engineering Ranking 2020

**Curriculum Highlights**

Our curriculum is reviewed by the Board of Studies with Director & R&D Heads from:

- Biocon
- Mahajan imaging
- Jananom pvt. Ltd
- IIT Delhi
- Department of Biotechnology, Ministry of Science & Technology, Government of India

**Academic Collaborations**

We have collaborations with:

- Universite du Québec/Institute National de LA Recherche Scientifique
- York University
- POSTECH, South Korea
- University of Oklahoma, USA
- University of Nebraska, USA
- University de Lorraine, CNRS, LIEC, Nancy, France
- Florida International University, USA

These collaborations provide research opportunities and are continuously expanded to broaden our research collaboration opportunities.

**Project-Based Learning**

Our students are engaged in research-enabled project-based experiential learning from Semester-I.

**Industry Interactions**

Our students have interacted with scientists from:

- Emami Biotech
- Patanjali
- Biocon
- Bharat Biotech
- Serum Institute
- Ayurvet
- Dabur Research Foundation
- Panacea Biotech
- Talwar Research Foundation

We have also signed MoUs with industries for collaborative research and internship opportunities.

**Program Details**

- **Duration:** 4 years
- **Specialisation:** 5 specialisations
- **Core Electives:** 21 core electives including Phytopharmaceuticals: Engineering & Production, Drug Design, and Discovery, Microbiome in Health & Disease, Personalized Medicine and Therapeutics, Biorefinery and Bioeconomy, among others.

**Program Specialisation**

The B.Tech Biotechnology syllabus aims to broaden academic profile through minor courses and specialisations in:

- AI and Machine Learning in healthcare
- Biomedical engineering
- Nanotechnology
- Industry internships
- Exchange in foreign institutions
- Theme-based research projects in-house or at reputed R&D institutions in India or abroad.

Duration: 4 years

Specialisation: 5

Credits: 160

Start Date: Aug/Sep 2026 (Tentative)

Admission Counsellors:
- Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries.

Fee Structure 2025:
- Year 1: Tuition fee (₹) 3,60,000, Registration Fees (One Time) (₹) 45,000
- Year 2: Tuition fee (₹) 3,70,000
- Year 3: Tuition fee (₹) 3,80,000
- Year 4: Tuition fee (₹) 3,80,000
- Total: Tuition fee (₹) 14,90,000, Total Fees (₹) 15,35,000

Open Electives

Students can choose open electives from 2nd year onwards, i.e., from 4th semester, and will carry 15 credits. These are interdisciplinary courses that students can opt from different schools. The available open electives are:

- Linux Apache MySQL PHP (LAMP)
- Software Engineering
- Numerical Methods
- Introduction to Communication
- Launch Your Startup
- Personal Branding
- Project Management
- Constitutional Governance & RTI
- Theories of Mass Communication
- Inside China: An Indian Perspective

B.Tech. Biotech Specialisations

Biomedical Technologies

This specialization has provided invaluable tools for diagnosis and creating life-saving equipment. It comprises three courses:

- Biomedical Technologies in Diagnostics and Therapy: 
  3D Bioprinting, Artificial organs, electronic implants, biomaterials, biocompatibility, stem cells and tissue engineering, radiation oncology; biomedical devices: prefilled syringes, auto injectors, on body devices and pumps, skin patches, etc.

Bioimaging: 

- Magnetic resonance imaging
- Micro and nano tomography of biological tissues
- Image analysis
- Molecular imaging
- Data analytics towards diagnosis

Medical Innovations: 

- Ex-vivo immunotherapy
- Organs-on-chip
- Microbiome-based therapies
- Case studies

Drug Discovery & Protein Therapeutics:

This specialization focuses on understanding various drug discovery tools and methods used for finding, identifying, and designing a new drug and understanding the regulatory procedures for drug development.

Three courses include the following subjects:

1. **Drug Discovery Process and Molecular Basis:**
   - Natural products as pharmaceutical lead compounds
   - Modern medicinal chemistry techniques
   - Case studies on the development of a class of compounds (indicative areas: antivirals, oncology, and central nervous system diseases)
   - Introduction to screening hits
   - Optimization of hits to reduce potential drug side effects (increasing affinity and selectivity), efficacy or potency, metabolic stability (half-life), and oral bioavailability

2. **Rational Drug Design and Structure Determination:**
   - Protein structure determination (crystallography, NMR, and Cryo-EM)
   - Structure visualization
   - Homology modelling and docking
   - Virtual screening for lead discovery
   - Structure-based and ligand-based design methods
   - Use of computational techniques in library preparation and data handling

3. **Biotherapeutics:**
   - Modern therapeutic approaches
   - Overview of antibody-based therapeutics
   - Biologics for autoimmunity and inflammation
   - Genetically engineered vaccines
   - Present and future biologics
   - Principles of immunodiagnostic assay
   - Development and clinical implementation of live biotherapeutic products
   - Regulatory roadmaps

Genetic Counselling & Precision Medicine:
This specialization offers advanced expertise in applied genetics and genomics.

Students interested in acquiring advanced expertise in applied genetics and genomics should opt for this specialization.

Brief B.Tech Biotechnology syllabus for three courses include:

**Course 1: Human Genetics**

- Human genes and genomes
- Mendelian and Non-mendelian mode of inheritance
- Mitochondrial inheritance
- Genetics of complex and single gene disorders
- Genetic variations in health and disease
- Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium
- Haplotypes and linkage studies
- Diagnosis and Molecular basis of Cancer
- Cardiovascular, Cardiometabolic diseases
- Neurodegenerative disorders
- Mitochondrial and rare disorders
- Signal transduction and human diseases
- Animal models of human diseases

**Course 2: Genetic Counselling**

- Review of human genetics
- Pedigree analysis
- Causes and factors for seeking counselling
- Risks evaluation
- Basics of embryology
- Dysmorphology
- Risks associated with invasive procedures
- Foetal sampling procedure
- Analysing ultrasounds
- Therapeutic benefits of dietary management
- Various counselling approaches
- Preconceptional counselling
- Counselling recurrent pregnancy loss
- Pre-marital counselling
- Ethical and legal issues in genetic counselling

**Course 3: Genomics and Personalized Medicine**

- Human Genome Mapping
- Gene therapy with case studies
- Gene editing tools
- Genome wide association studies
- Omics technologies in precision medicine
- Nutrigenomics
- Metabolomics
- Microbiome fingerprinting
- Molecular Basis of Personalized Medicine
- Polymorphisms of Drug Transporters
- Drug Targets
- Drug metabolizing enzymes
- Ethical, Legal, and Social Issues related to Personalized Medicine
- Personalized management of miscellaneous disorders

**Industrial Biotechnology**

This specialization aims to provide advanced courses on green products ranging from enzymes and biocatalysts, biofuels, biobased platform chemicals, among others. The subjects covered under this specialization are:

Here's the refined text:

"Enzyme Technology:
- Enzyme production
- Purification
- Preservation
- Immobilization techniques
- Enzyme reactors and reaction kinetics
- Improvement of enzyme activity and stability
- Directed evolution of enzymes
- Applications:
  - Food industries
  - Pharmaceutical industries
  - Bio-pharmaceutical industries
  - Biochemical industries
  - Textile and detergent industries
  - R&D
- Case studies and current status of enzyme research
- Global market of enzymes and opportunities for biotech professionals
- Quality control of enzymes.

Green Chemicals & Sustainable Products:
- Concept of sustainability and green chemicals
- Carbon credit & carbon tax
- Lifecycle analysis (LCA) & cost benefit analysis
- Smart drop-in chemicals
- Fossil based chemicals vs. biobased chemicals
- Biobased bulk chemicals
- Bio-platform chemicals
- Specialty chemicals
- Biobased fragrance and aroma compounds
- Active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) and high value products
- Antibiotics production
- Biobased steroids & derivatives
- Bioplastics and polymers
- Biodegradable vs. non-biodegradable bioplastics
- Natural vs. synthetic biopolymers
- Final products manufactured using green chemicals
- Case studies of industrial production of green chemicals
- Current status of green chemicals research."

Biofuels & Biorefinery:
- Biomass & bioenergy
- Production and purification of bioethanol
- Biodiesel production
- Biobutanol
- Biogas & Bio-CNG
- Biohydrogen
- Microbial fuel cell
- Bio-aviation fuels
- Biofuel production under biorefinery framework
- Algal biorefinery for high value compounds
- Biorefinery for high value secondary metabolites
- Extraction and purification of high value phytochemicals under biorefinery framework
- Biofuel production and simultaneous waste management
- Metabolic engineering for biofuel production
- Biofuel production process simulation
- Scale-up of biofuel production processes
- Case studies of biofuel production
- Current status of biofuel research and job opportunities

Nanotechnology & Biosensors:
- This specialization is best suited for students who want to deep dive into subjects of nanotechnology, the uses of nanomaterial in biosensors, and wearable technology.
- Covered subjects:
  - Nanomedicine: 
    - Principles and concepts
    - Development of nanoparticles/ liposomes/ lipid nanoparticles/ micelles
    - Surface modification
    - Characterization
    - Encapsulation
    - Release kinetics
    - Cargo- Drug/ gene/ protein/ antibody etc.
    - Cellular and physiological barriers
    - Clinical Applications in different diseases
  - Introduction to Biosensors and Bioelectronics:
    - Thermal and electronic biosensors
    - Optical biosensors
    - Mechanical biosensors: microfluidics 
    - Fabrication, characterization and detection of bioanalyte
    - Live sensors
    - Applications- clinical and non-clinical (defense/ Agriculture/ Pollution)
    - Regulatory aspects
    - Commercialization examples

**Research and Projects**

Digital healthcare, Theranostics and Lab-on-a-chip: 
- Digital and mobile healthcare: wearable devices – commercial needs and contexts.
- Principles, Design, Preparation, Delivery mechanism, Imaging modality, Metallic nanoparticles, Photothermal Therapy/ Radiation Therapy/ MRI/ CT/ PET, Clinical Applications.
- Lab-on-a-chip sensing - device type, sample preparation, and readouts; Organ-on-a-chip – principles and applications. 
- Regulatory aspects. 
- Commercialization examples.

ELIGIBILITY

B.Tech Biotech Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process

Eligibility:

- Domestic: Passed XII standard from any recognized Education Board with Physics and Biology/Biotechnology/Botany/Zoology & any other third subject with minimum 55% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects.
- NRI: Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR)

Selection:
Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Biology/Biotechnology/Botany/Zoology as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score excluding Hindi).

Third subject should not be vocational - Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc.

PROCESS

B.Tech Biotechnology Admission Process for 2025

The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after a series of evaluative steps.

B.Tech Biotechnology Admission Process for 2025

The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after a series of evaluative steps:

1. Submission of the application form with required details by paying a non-refundable application fee of Rs. 1200.
2. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters.
3. Counseling by the Bennett University's admissions team.

Program Infra

The Department of Biotechnology at Bennett University has 11 laboratories. All labs are equipped with modern instruments and lab benches to implement research-enabled project-based learning. The labs' infrastructure is aligned to futuristic trends in industry and academia, making Bennett University one of the best B.Tech Biotechnology colleges in India.

The laboratories are equipped for the following:

1. Molecular Biology: Techniques such as isolation, purification, and handling of DNA, RNA, and proteins; RT-PCR, etc.
2. Cell & Tissue Culture Technologies: State-of-the-art cell culture facility covering mammalian cell culture techniques, stem cells, production of recombinant therapeutics in cell cultures.
3. rDNA Technology: Techniques of gene cloning, generating transgenic GMOs, CRISPR genome editing, etc.
4. Bioprocess Engineering: Bioprocess and fermentation techniques, including production of bioplastic and biofuel.
5. Microbiology & Immunology: Handling microorganisms such as bacteria, fungi, and bacteriophages, and different immunological assays such as Western blot and ELISA.
6. High-End Instrumentation laboratory (Biotechnology Central Instrumentation Facility-I): High-end equipment such as HPLC, GC, and fluorescent microscopes, trained students to operate this equipment to make them industry ready.

Chemistry & Bioanalytical Techniques:
- Students learn to analyze major biomolecules quantitatively and qualitatively, including carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids.

BSL2:
- The state-of-the-art BSL-2 Facility advances infectious disease research and addresses public health challenges posed by infectious agents.

Nanotechnology:
- At the Centre for Excellence in Nanosensors and Nanomedicine (CoENN), students learn about integrating biomolecules with advanced sensing technology for various biosensing applications, focusing on applying Nanotechnology-based characterization and detection techniques in MedTech/Biotech Industry.

Undergraduate Laboratories:
- Two undergraduate labs are used for conducting experiments for the first-year courses.

Faculty Profile:
- All faculty members are Ph.D., Post-doctorates, or have been affiliated with foreign universities and Indian institutes of high repute.
- Examples of affiliated universities include:
  - Université du Québec/INRS (Canada)
  - University of California (USA)
  - University of Central Florida (USA)
  - POSTECH (South Korea)
  - NIH (USA)
  - Cornell University (USA)
  - University of Milan (Italy)
  - University of Lorraine (France)
  - University of Galway (Ireland)
  - University of Connecticut (USA)
  - University of Duisburg Essen (Germany)
  - IITs (Delhi, Jodhpur, and Kharagpur)
  - AIIMS (Delhi)
  - THSTI (Faridabad)

Research Profile:
- B. Tech students pursue mini-projects with faculties and Ph.D. students, gaining hands-on exposure from these universities of high repute.
- Faculty research profile: 
  - Faculty members have affiliation with universities of high repute
  - They pursue mini-projects with B. Tech students and Ph.D. students.

**Research Profile of Faculty of Biotechnology Department**

The Faculty of Biotechnology Department is involved in diverse areas of research based on their expertise, including:

1. Infectious disease
2. Drug development
3. Immuno-diagnostics/therapeutics
4. Nanotechnology and nanoscience
5. Bioengineering
6. Environment biotechnology
7. Bioprocess engineering
8. Plant biotechnology
9. Genome engineering (CRISPR-Cas9)
10. Plant-based protein therapeutics
11. Metabolic engineering
12. Cancer biology
13. Neuro-oncology and neurological disorders
14. Computational Biophysics
15. Discovery genomics
16. Structure biology and drug discovery
17. Antimicrobial resistance
18. Vaccinology
19. Antibody discovery and engineering

The faculty has published 74 research articles in peer-reviewed journals and has 11 patents in the last 6 years, with extramural funding of 7 crores from DBT, DST, and other funding agencies.

**Post B.Tech Biotech Career Opportunities**

It is encouraged to do extensive project-based learning during the B.Tech program, which can lead to research project outcomes (such as patents or papers or conferences) that are helpful in getting into good institutions abroad for MS/Ph.D. with good scholarships.

Biotechnology is a major part of the economy in many western countries, with a high number of scholarships available, especially compared to computer science. Students are guided through the application process of biotech-specific scholarships.

**B.Tech Biotech Course**

The B.Tech Biotech course covers both technical and personal skills, preparing students for a career in biotechnology and related fields.

**Additional Information**

* Students can pursue MS or Ph.D. programs abroad after completing their B.Tech Biotech degree.
* Research project outcomes are highly valued in getting into good institutions abroad for MS/Ph.D. programs.
* Biotechnology is a lucrative field in many western countries, with a high number of scholarships available.

**Benefits of B.Tech Biotech Course**

Yes, a B.Tech Biotech course helps students learn both technical and personal skills. The curriculum includes courses to strengthen soft skills such as:

- Verbal and written communication
- Collaboration
- Teamwork

Additionally, students can choose to take minors from the humanities department or the School of Management, or opt for open electives or minor specialization. The Bennett Finishing School also assists students in developing soft skills that recruiters look for in potential employees.

**Technical Skills in B.Tech Biotech**

Technical skills are honed to international standards through:

- Core biotech courses
- Projects
- Research paper reading
- Class and project seminars
- Presentations

**Infrastructure Facilities at Bennett University**

The university has high-end instruments, including:

- RT-PCR machine
- Fluorescence microscope
- EVOS
- Mammalian and plant tissue culture
- HPLC
- GC
- Plate reader
- FT-IR
- Bio-reactor
- FPLC
- AKTA protein purification system

Minor instruments available in the labs include:

- Gel docs
- Thermal cyclers
- Refrigerated centrifuges
- 4 degrees freezer
- -20 degrees and -80 freezers
- Spectrophotometers
- Autoclaves
- Shaker incubators

Students also have access to instruments in other engineering departments.

**Research Opportunities for B.Tech Biotech Students**

B.Tech Biotech students can participate in research at Bennett University.

Research Opportunities for BTech Biotech Students at Bennett University

The B.Tech Biotech program at Bennett University emphasizes research for undergraduate students. The university has created a research ecosystem where faculty and Ph.D. students are actively participating in research. 

Key Features:

- Students are given examples of research problems.
- Every course has a hands-on research project component.
- Students must compulsorily take project credits from 6th semester onwards under the supervision of a faculty.
- This provides at least 18 months of extensive research exposure.

Computer-Based Learning Programs in Biotechnology

The B.Tech Biotech curriculum includes a core course on:

- Computational Thinking & Programming (1st year)
- Bioinformatics
- Minor in Artificial Intelligence with four courses from Computer Science & Engineering

Support for Entrepreneurship in Biotechnology

Bennett University has its incubation centre, Bennett Hatchery, which provides full support for entrepreneurship. The faculty also offers:

- Courses on entrepreneurship in the curriculum
- Support and mentorship to take advantage of BIRAC (Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council) set up by the Ministry of Science & Technology, Govt of India
- Minimal funds of Rs. 50 lakhs to students or faculty willing to set up a Biotech start-up

**B.Tech Engineering Physics Program**

* Explore dynamics of movement through innovation
* Research-oriented faculty
* Having experience of working in research laboratories in Germany, France, Netherlands, Sweden, Finland, Italy, USA, South Korea, Israel and Turkey.
* Live Projects
 + 4 externally funded (DST and DBT) projects running, worth more than ₹ 6 million.
* Emerging Technologies
 + Familiarity with advanced software and skills.
* Specialisations

B.Tech Engineering Physics

B.Tech. Engineering Physics (EP) students at Bennett University (BU) build strong foundations in Physics, Mathematics, and engineering with the help of the fundamental courses. The curriculum exposes the students to advanced courses in material science, photonics, and quantum physics, which find diverse applications in many technological sectors such as nanotechnology, optical fiber communication, quantum technology, to name a few.

The B. Tech EP program aims to offer a four-dimensional (4D) teaching and learning experience to the students. In a 4D approach, the students get the opportunity to develop knowledge, skill, character as well as metacognition mindset.

The curriculum is divided into three broad sections of Core, Elective and Project. The core curriculum is bifurcated in such a way that the basic science is in harmony with engineering science and social science courses.

Strong foundation in fundamental science, is the heart of the EP program. Apart from Physics, the curriculum has courses from other fundamental branches with an emphasis on Mathematics. The humanities and social science courses in the curriculum, cater for soft skill and character development. Additionally, it offers insights into the mental make-up of entrepreneurs.

The courses in engineering science have elements of computer science, mechanical engineering along with electronics and communication engineering. The curriculum allows a unique opportunity to students to choose between a computer science and electronic courses, apart from the mandatory foundation level subjects.

Program Details:
- Duration: 4 years
- Credits: 160-161
- Start Date: Aug/Sep 2026 (Tentative)

**Program Details**

- Duration: 4 years
- Credits: 160-161
- Start Date: Aug/Sep 2026 (Tentative)

B.Tech EP Curriculum Overview

The department offers six Specialisation on the following topics:

1. **Nanoscience and Nanotechnology**
2. **Quantum Science and Technology**

Specialisation Requirements

To get specialization in the degree, a student must complete at least 4 electives from the corresponding specialization basket and do Major Project II in the same field.

Electives

The department offers the following elective courses:

1. **Renewable Energy Engineering**
2. **Advanced Statistical Mechanics with Biophysical Applications**
3. **Quantum Electronics**
4. **Elements of Quantum Computation and Quantum Information**
5. **Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos**
6. **Nuclear and Particle Physics**
7. **Computational Modeling of Materials**
8. **Superconductivity and Condensate**
9. **Particle Physics**

Skills Acquired

During the four years of the programme, students learn and acquire:

1. **Analytical ability**
2. **Experience of coding physical problems**
3. **Knowledge of different computing languages**: Python, C++, Fortran
4. **Ability to handle advanced computation and lab automation tools**: Matlab, Mathematica, LabView
5. **Experience of handling state-of-the-art experimental instruments**: Sputtering unit, XRD, Electrochemical workstation, Fourier Transform Infrared Spectroscopy, surface profilometer etc. along with experimental automation
6. **Acquaintance with advanced software widely used in Material Science and Molecular Dynamics research**

Acquaintance with advanced softwares widely used in Material Science and Molecular Dynamics research.

**Projects**

- 12 credit Industrial R&D Project in the 8th semester
- Two mandatory projects in the 6th and 7th semester, each for 2 and 3 credits, respectively
- Past projects have focused on:
  - Nanotechnology
  - Photovoltaics
  - Soft condensed matter physics
  - Biophysics
  - Computational condensed matter physics
  - Ultracold atoms
  - Quantum optics
  - Quantum information science
- Students have also participated in international startups in the industrial project, earning remuneration based on their performances
- They have also been selected for fully funded final semester R&D project in eminent universities in Europe and top tier research labs in India

**Live Projects**

- Photonic Quantum Walks in Waveguide Lattice
- Study of quantum correlations and their quantification
- Quantum Simulations
- Solid state dye sensitized solar cells
- Resistive Switching of Vanadium Oxides
- Development of simulation software based on Tight binding method for computing physical properties of real materials
- Learning Molecular Dynamics simulation and its applications
- Study of Supersolid Phase

**Internships**

- All B.Tech Engineering Physics students must take up a summer internship at the end of the 6th semester
- Past internship locations include:
  - IISER-Kolkata (summer school)
  - IIT Guwahati (winter internship)
  - CDAC Pune (winter internship)
  - European Universities (summer internships with competitive salaries)
- Students have also qualified for jobs with competitive salaries from university placement drive

B.Tech Curriculum

Theory and experiment are blended in the B.Tech curriculum.

Laboratories

The department has a total of nine laboratories.

1. Two 1st-year laboratories in:
   - Electromagnetics
   - Mechanics

2. Advanced Physics Lab-I:
   - Dedicated darkroom facility with advanced optics experiments, including:
     - Kerr Effect
     - Magnetostriction Effect

3. Computational Physics laboratory:
   - High-Performance Computing (HPC) facility
   - State-of-art computational and graphical software:
     - Mathematica
     - Matlab
     - Labview
     - Origin

4. Advanced Physics Lab-II:
   - Condensed matter laboratory with advanced experiments, including:
     - Quantum Analog
     - UV-Vis-NIR Spectrophotometer
     - Dual Channel Source Measurement Unit
     - Solar Cell

5. Nanotechnology lab:
   - DC/RF Magnetron Sputtering Unit for the fabrication of nanostructured materials
   - Electrochemical workstation

Facilities

- FTIR
- Plasma
- Quantum Efficiency
- Source Meter Unit
- Spin Coater
- Sputtering
- Surface Profilometer
- UV-Vis-NIR Spectrophotometer
- Advanced Optics and Magnetic Resonance Lab

- Solar Simulator

**International Tie-Ups**

Bennett University brings international exposure to students through its strategic collaborations, providing a glimpse of global education practices.

International Tie-Ups

Bennett University brings international exposure to students. Our strategic collaborations ensure that our students get a glimpse of global education practices.

**Collaborations:**

1. **Florida International University, USA**
   - Non-degree seeking opportunities for BU students to be enrolled in FIU UG/Graduate courses (including master's and Ph.D. courses)
   - Student exchange programs

2. **University of Nebraska, Omaha**
   - Student Mobility programs
   - Customized educational/training programs for students and faculty
   - Cooperative research and development projects

3. **The University of British Columbia, Canada**
   - Participation of Bennett students in UBC Summer Academic Program

4. **University of Missouri-Kansas City (UMKC), USA**
   - BU’s B.Tech graduates get the opportunity to pursue an MS. degree
   - Students will complete 3.5 years (7 semesters) at BU for B.Tech and 1.5 years at (8th semester of B.Tech + 2 semesters of MS) at UMKC’s School of Computing and Engineering

**ELIGIBILITY**

**B.Tech Engineering Physics Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process**

* **Eligibility:**
 + Domestic: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third* subject with minimum 55% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects.
 + NRI: Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR).

* **Selection:** Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score excluding Hindi). Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc.

B.Tech Engineering Physics Admission Process for 2025

The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after a series of evaluative steps. The process includes:

1. Submission of the application form with required details by paying a non-refundable application fee of Rs. 1500.
2. Merit-based assessment of candidates and the release of provisional offer letters.
3. Selected students must pay an advance fee within the given deadline.
4. Submission of documents and payment of pending fees to complete registration as per the shared timelines.

B.Tech. Engineering Physics Program Overview

The B.Tech. Engineering Physics program at Bennett University guarantees coursework quality alignment with academic and industry developments. The program aims to inculcate a habit of independent research, enabling students to take on research projects at an early stage.

Frequently Asked Questions

1. How is Engineering Physics different from other branches?

Engineering physics blends courses from engineering, physics, and math to build an understanding of how these areas interact and support each other. It is expected to boost one’s knowledge of the physical environment while discovering how physics is applied to problem-solving in our rapidly changing high-tech world. Therefore, the horizon of EP graduates expands many folds compared to other branches.

2. What makes Engineering Physics at Bennett University unique?

(No specific information provided in the original text)

What makes Engineering Physics at Bennett University unique?

- The Engineering Physics course is only offered by a handful of IITs across India, besides a few Universities (Private as well as Public).
- Bennett University is among the few places where the course is available.
- The university atmosphere allows a unique blending of science, arts, and culture, which is essential for holistic development.
- The freedom to choose courses from Law, Liberal Arts, Management, or Media in the form of open elective and Minor gives an extra edge.

How can I check if B.Tech. in EP is the right fit for me?

- If you have an inherent passion for physics and mathematics and wish to know how modern technologies work, then you will enjoy the B. Tech EP course.

As a B.Tech engineering physics student, you'll study:
- The fundamentals and intricacies of both engineering and physics, such as:
  - Electromagnetism
  - Thermodynamics
  - Quantum mechanics
  - Signals and systems
- Some programs include research in:
  - Superconductivity
  - Optical communication
  - Nano-science and technology
- You'll also take courses in:
  - Calculus
  - Differential equations
- Laboratory work will teach you how to use experimental techniques and give you hands-on experience with high-tech equipment.

What are the major career profiles after completing B.Tech. in EP?

Career Profiles after Completing B.Tech in EP

Major career profiles after completing B.Tech in Engineering Physics (EP) include:

* Financial companies: America Express, Goldman Sachs, HSBC
* IT jobs in well-known IT companies
* R&D jobs in places like ISRO, DRDO, BARC
* Industries engaged in the design and manufacture of semiconductors, microelectronics or photonics components and systems
* Scientific software development industry: Mathworks (Matlab) and Wolfram (Mathematica)

Internship Opportunities for EP Students

EP students may have summer internships in:

* Institutions of national importance: ISRO, GMRT, DRDO
* Institutions of international repute: TIFR, IISc, IISERs, IITs
* Industry, arranged by the university placement cell

Existing EP Student Projects and Internships

EP students are engaged in academic as well as industry projects related to:

* Quantum technology
* Material science
* Stochastic processes and their application in finance
* Decentralised finance

Companies/Institutes where B.Tech EP Students have been Placed

* Aalto University, Finland
* Gutenberg University, Germany
* Instadapp, USA
* IIT Tirupati, India
* University of Nebraska, Omaha

  - Higher studies: University of Heidelberg, University of Bologna, University of Cologne, University of Leipzig, University of Tubingen, University of Freiburg, University of Nurnberg
  - Jobs: Byju's

- Celebrated International Day of Light with colloquiums from Prof. Ajoy Ghatak and Dr Amitava Sengupta.

Career Opportunities After BTech Engineering Physics:

1. **Opting for PhD in India:** 
   - Currently, few IITs and some leading research institutes take Ph.D students right after B. Tech.
   - The recent National Educational Policy has opened avenues for direct entry to Ph.D for 4-yr degree programmes.
   - There are several scholarship exams in India for Ph.D, such as CSIR-NET, GATE, JEST, which provide financial assistance.
   - Various IITs and IISERs also take entrance exams for the intake of Ph.D students.
   - The Govt of India has put a strong emphasis on indigenous research, increasing scholarship amounts and numbers every year.

2. **Advantages of Doing a PhD:**
   - Students put themselves on a higher-level career path towards a leadership role.
   - They can get academic faculty positions in various institutes in India or abroad.

3. **Going Abroad After B.Tech Engineering Physics:**
   - Students can pursue a Master's (MS) or PhD program abroad.

**Options for Going Abroad after B.Tech Engineering Physics**

It is strongly encouraged in the Department of Physics that B.Tech students take research projects during their studies at Bennett University. This plays a crucial role in getting admission to Universities abroad. Research project outcomes (such as patents or papers) are often more helpful than even CGPA, for getting into good institutions for MS/Ph.D.

**Admission to PhD Programs Abroad**

Yes, some universities have direct entry from B.Tech to Ph.D programs for bright students. Others may take students at an MS program, and if their training and aptitude are found suitable for a Ph.D, they are usually transitioned into a PhD after 1 year. It is observed that 50% of the Engineering Physics students tend to go abroad for Ph.D programs.

**Foreign Exchange/Semester Exchange Programs**

The final semester is left to the student’s choice of whether he wishes to pursue an industry internship or a semester abroad. Students are encouraged to take up the semester abroad option and then pursue a Master’s (MS) there, with the possibility of scholarship support. This is in addition to faculty-level research collaborations.

**About Mechanical Engineering**

Mechanical Engineers are engaged in creating the future in Aerospace Engineering, Automotive Engineering, Biomechanics Technologies, Energy Technologies, Mechatronics & Robotics Technologies and Industrial Engineering. They are the driving force behind many technologies and industrial processes in our modern world. Mechanical Engineering at the University trains the student in manufacturing, design, automation, and thermal sciences.

Students have adequate flexibility to specialise in areas of their choice through electives and projects. The course emphasises on subjects such as Flexible Manufacturing Systems, Computer Integrated Manufacturing, Robotics, Tribology, Composites and Laser Materials, Energy Engineering, and Finite Element Methods so as the students can eventually take up positions in manufacturing, design, energy generation and conservation and R&D.

**Program Details**

- Duration: 4 years
- Specialisation: 8
- Credits: 160
- Start Date: Aug/Sep 2026 (Tentative)

**Fee Structure 2025**

- Tuition fee (₹):
  - Year 1: 3,60,000
  - Year 2: 3,70,000
  - Year 3: 3,80,000
  - Year 4: 3,80,000
  - Total: 14,90,000
- Registration Fees: One Time (₹) 45,000
- Total Fees (₹): 15,35,000

Scholarship 2025

Scholarship Range: 
- XII (Best of 3)*: 50% for >=95, 30% for 85-94.99, 20% for 80-84.99, 10% for 75-79.99
- JEE Mains Score: >=1401 for 50%, 1351-1400 for 30%, 1301-1350 for 20%, 1201-1300 for 10%
- IB Score: >=80 for 50%, 75-79.99 for 30%, 65-74.99 for 20%, 60-64.99 for 10%
- SAT Score: >=40 for 50%, 34-39 for 30%, 32-33 for 20%, 30-31 for 10%

Curriculum Details

The B.Tech. course curriculum in the ME Department is prepared under the guidance of globally renowned professors, scientists from established research laboratories, and industry experts. The goal is to prepare global players and professionals of the future. Each Core course is designed with 60% hands-on experience in well-equipped laboratories and 40% classroom teaching. Additionally, students can select electives based on their individual interests and specialisations.

The University curriculum covers subjects from the basics of the mechanical course to industry-oriented courses. Students can also take courses from other departments, which opens the scope of working on interdisciplinary projects. The ME Department offers a design project course, which runs in all semesters, helping students develop skills in technical writing, oral presentation, data analysis, code writing, and experimental research. This course ultimately helps in their final year dissertation and higher studies.

Key Highlights

Minors

- Students must earn 16 credits in a field other than ME through crediting specific Minor courses offered by other Departments.
- Minors offered by different engineering streams are open to B.Tech. ME students.
- A Minor in 'Robotics and Mechatronics' (with ECE Department at BU) is open to all engineering students from all disciplines from AY 2024-25 onwards.

Minor Programs

- The Robotics and Mechatronics minor (with the ECE Department at BU) is open to all Engineering students from all disciplines from AY 2024-25 onwards.
- Students are required to earn 16 credits in a field other than Mechanical Engineering through crediting specific Minor courses offered by other Departments.
- Minors offered by different engineering streams are open to B.Tech ME students.

Honours

- Honours in Mechanical Engineering is obtained by earning 18 more credits than the regular degree by studying six advanced courses from the Mechanical domain.

Elective Courses

- The Mechanical Engineering Department offers a rich list of elective courses from various domains to choose from.
- B.Tech (ME) students have to complete 5 elective courses in their curriculum to fulfill the requirements of the B.Tech (ME) Degree.

Specialisation Streams

- Aerospace Engineering
- E-Mobility
- Energy Technologies
- Industrial Engineering
- Mechatronics
- Robotics
- Product Design
- AI & ML

Program Specific Outcomes (PSO)

- PSO1: Effectively build and communicate solutions using learnings from basic mechanical engineering, basic sciences, mathematics, computational skills, and tools.
- PSO2: Ability to conduct experiments and simulate real-life problems in engineering using computational techniques and instrumentation; and work independently in research or industrial environments.
- PSO3: Capability to compete with available employment opportunities and solve complex engineering problems related to production, design, thermal, and allied industries using systematic tools, while giving due consideration to environment protection and sustainability.

Recent Projects

- Free vibration and stability analysis of functionally graded rectangular plate.
- Studies on tribological behaviour of solid lubricating coating.

Research Projects:

1. Free vibration and stability analysis of functionally graded rectangular plate.
2. Studies on tribological behaviour of solid lubricating coating.
3. Numerical simulation and analysis of cavitating flows around hydrofoils and in injector nozzles.
4. Vibro-acoustic and wear phenomena in rail-wheel contact.
5. Experimental and modelling studies on the high strain rate characterization of shear thickening fluids for dynamic loading applications.
6. Analysis of rotary cement kiln for failure diagnosis and prediction of failure life.
7. Design and Analysis of Gas Turbine Inlet and Exhaust Structures, AAF International, UK, 2019
8. Design and Development of Test Rig for High Strain Rate Characterization of Materials for Dynamic Applications.
9. Design & development of Foot categorization device.
10. Kinematic & kinetic analyses of human movement & spine postures.
11. Design & Development of low-cost hybrid ATV.
12. A Biomechanical Analysis of Movements of Body Segments and Quantification of Performance Enhancement factors while Playing Badminton.
13. Design and Analysis of an active prosthetic knee for trans-femoral amputees.
14. Measuring and optimizing biomechanical risk factors of lifting load tasks in Industry through Wearable System.
15. Biomechanical Loads in Running-Based Sports: Estimating Ground Reaction Forces from Segmental Accelerations using wearable sensors

**Eligibility Criteria for Btech ME Program**

**Domestic Eligibility Criteria:**

- Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third subject with minimum 55% marks.
- OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects.

**NRI Eligibility Criteria:**

- Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR).

**Selection Criteria:**

- Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score excluding Hindi).
- The third subject should not be vocational, i.e., Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc.

**Admission Process for 2025:**

The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after the following evaluative steps:

1. Submission of the application form with required details by paying non-refundable application fees of Rs. 1500.
2. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters.
3. Selected Students to pay advance fee within the deadline given.
4. Submit documents and pending fee payment to complete registration as per timelines shared.

**Frequently Asked Questions:**

- What is the current scope of Mechanical Engineers?
Mechanical Engineers are focusing on new arenas of research such as nanotechnology, development of composite materials, biomedical applications, environmental conservation, and IT Setups etc.

**Bennett University BTech Mechanical Engineering Program**

**Specialisations Offered**

Bennett University offers six specialisations under BTech Mechanical Engineering:

1. Aerospace Engineering
2. E-Mobility
3. Energy Technologies
4. Industrial Engineering
5. Mechatronics
6. Robotics
7. Product Design
8. AI & ML

**Career Opportunities**

Students can opt for various job opportunities after completing the B. Tech degree in Mechanical Engineering, including:

1. Materials Engineer
2. Maintenance Engineer
3. Automotive Engineer
4. Control and Instrumentation Engineer
5. Government jobs
6. Teaching or professor roles in government schools, colleges, and universities
7. Opportunities through government exams such as UPSC, Railways, Banks, etc.

**Industrial Interaction Program**

Throughout the year, students will get opportunities to:

1. Work on live projects under industry experts
2. Participate in industry visits for hands-on experience
3. Apply for internship opportunities with leading industries
4. Participate in global immersion programs to expand their career dimension

**About BTech ECE**

B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering focuses on developing a suitable academic environment to promote research attitude, employability readiness and entrepreneurial spirit among students.

It is an intensely focused undergraduate program tailored to a student’s interests and real-world opportunities.

**Program Details**

- Duration: 4 years
- Specialisation: 4
- Credits: 162
- Start Date: Aug/Sep 2026 (Tentative)

**Program Highlights**

- Exposure to state-of-the-art software/hardware tools like MATLAB, Vivado, OptiSim, Milling machine, FPGA Virtex/Kintex boards, MSO, VNA, etc.
- Hands-on practical exposure
- Experience working on live projects funded by the Government of India and leading industries
- Exposure to work on Future Technologies (5G+AI, smart sensors, Cognitive IoT, End-to-end Systems, Quantum Communication, Signal Processing, 5G Antenna Design)
- 100 Cr+ funding for start-up projects
- Work under the supervision of Industry and Research Experts
- Collaboration with industry and research institutes

**Career Opportunities**

Electronics and Communication Engineers work in the areas of design, development, research, and manufacturing of Internet of Things, 5G & Beyond and Information Processing & Analytics.

**Curriculum**

The program includes courses in Basic Sciences, Mathematics, Basic Engineering, along with core subjects of Electronics & Communication Engineering (ECE). Electronic Communication Engineering is supported by a relevant, up-to-date curriculum, unique teaching pedagogy, fully equipped labs, and experienced faculty.

Curriculum Details

The programme structure will aid ECE pursuers to understand the coursework in-depth to identify the future potential of the programme. The detailed curriculum will shed light on the navigation of programme throughout the duration of four years.

**B.Tech ECE Curriculum**

- 2024-28
- 2023-27

Key Highlights

**Open Electives**

These courses are University-wide courses from different schools. A student in ECE can choose a course from an open list of electives provided by ECE department.

**Specialisations**

- Embedded Systems and IoT

Embedded Systems and IoT

In this specialisation, hardware design and software programming are used for innovative product development. The various embedded systems are interconnected using IoT based network. IoT is crucial for achieving high productivity, efficiency, safe working environment, and low carbon emissions. IoT solutions will play a significant role in home automation, security, and various industrial problems with 5G communications.

Course Topics:
- Hardware-Software Co-design
- Real Time Operating System
- Data Management and Cloud Computing
- IoT Networks and Protocols

Career Opportunities:
Students completing the course can opt for jobs in various companies such as Synopsys, Intel, NXP, Qualcomm, Analog Devices, Samsung, Microsoft, Siemens. Job profiles include:
- IoT lead Engineer
- IP Design Lead Engineer
- Hardware Architect
- ASIC Design FPGA Engineer
- Application Engineer

Data and Signal Analytics

This specialisation focuses on design, development, and research areas such as:
- Data Analytics
- Machine and Deep Learning
- Information Processing & Analytics
- Computer Vision

The specialisation is designed to attain technical aspects that enable students to grow into competent Data Analytics professionals.

Signal and Data Analytics

Signal and Data analytics is about making conclusions from the information they contain. Data analytics with machine learning technologies are widely used in commercial industries and research communities. The specialisation focuses on building foundations in data analytics using machine learning, a deep understanding of the area of specialisation, and innovative ability to solve new problems.

Key skills include:

- Building foundations in data analytics using machine learning
- Deep understanding of the area of specialisation
- Innovative ability to solve new problems
- Interdisciplinary skill sets and knowledge to handle and analyse large amounts of data

The specialisation offers courses in:

- Machine Learning
- Exploratory Data Analytics
- Big Data
- Natural Language Processing
- Artificial Intelligence
- Basic courses in programming languages

Meatronics

Mechatronics is an interdisciplinary field combining mechanical engineering, electronics, computer science, and control engineering to design and create intelligent systems and products. The goal is to integrate these diverse engineering disciplines to develop more efficient and intelligent systems with enhanced functionalities.

Key applications of Mechatronics include:

- Design, development, and maintenance of systems and products that involve a combination of mechanical and electronic components
- Incorporating sensors, actuators, and control systems

Key industries that rely on Mechatronics include:

- Manufacturing
- Automotive
- Aerospace
- Robotics
- Healthcare

Key demand for Mechatronics professionals is in India and globally due to the increasing complexity of modern engineering systems. The manufacturing sector in India has a strong demand for Mechatronics engineers, particularly in the automotive industry for the development of smart vehicles with advanced features.

Job opportunities for mechatronics engineers include roles such as mechatronics engineer, automation engineer, robotics engineer, control systems engineer, and product development engineer. The skill set required for mechatronics professionals spans mechanical design, electronics, programming, and system integration.

The various companies which offer job opportunities to Mechatronics engineers include Toyota, General Motors, Tesla, Siemens, ABB, Boston Dynamics, iRobot, NVIDIA, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Medtronic, Johnson & Johnson, Samsung, Sony, LG Electronics, Apple, Intel, IBM, Google, NASA, ISRO, Accenture, Deloitte, etc.

As industries continue to adopt advanced technologies, the demand for mechatronics professionals is expected to rise, providing ample job opportunities in India and globally for individuals with expertise in this interdisciplinary field.

**VLSI Design**
VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) Design is a field of electronics engineering that involves the design and development of integrated circuits (ICs) with a large number of transistors on a single chip. VLSI technology enables the integration of thousands to billions of transistors, allowing for the creation of complex and highly functional electronic systems on a small silicon chip.

In India and around the world, VLSI design plays a crucial role in the semiconductor industry, contributing to the development of advanced electronic devices such as microprocessors, memory chips, and application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs). VLSI engineers are involved in various stages of the IC design process, including architecture design, logic design, physical design, and verification.

The demand for VLSI engineers is significant, driven by the growth of the electronics and semiconductor industry. Companies like Intel, Qualcomm, Broadcom, and Samsung have a strong presence and offer job opportunities in VLSI design. Additionally, numerous Indian companies and startups focus on VLSI design services and contribute to the global semiconductor supply chain.

Globally, VLSI engineers find employment in leading semiconductor companies across the United States, Europe, and Asia. Silicon Valley in the U.S. is a hub for VLSI design, with companies like NVIDIA, AMD, and Apple actively involved in chip design.

Job opportunities in VLSI design include roles such as:

- VLSI design engineer
- ASIC design engineer
- Physical design engineer
- Verification engineer

The demand for VLSI professionals is expected to grow, as new technologies such as 5G, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things (IoT) rely heavily on advanced semiconductor solutions.

Specialisations:

- Data and Signal Analytics:
  - Data Analytics

- Embedded Systems and IoT:

Minors:

- Minors offered by different engineering streams are open to B.Tech. ECE students.

**Honours**

The Honours in Electronics and Communication Engineering is obtained by earning 18 more credits than the regular degree, which requires studying six advanced courses.

**Projects**

- 12 credit Major Project in the 8th semester
- 5 credits in the 7th semester
- 1 credit in the 6th semester
Students have the opportunity to start the project in the 6th semester, continue to evolve during the summer after 6th and 7th semesters, and finally finish it in the final semester.

**Eligibility Criteria and Selection Process for BTech ECE**

**Eligibility**

- **Domestic**:
  - Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third* subject with minimum 55% marks
  - IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects.

- **NRI**:
  - Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR).

**Selection**

- Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score excluding Hindi).

Admissions Criteria:

Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score excluding Hindi).

The third subject should not be vocational, such as Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc.

**B.Tech. ECE Program**

**Career Opportunities**

B.Tech. Electronics and Communication Engineering is offered to students who are interested in public sector companies like Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL), ISRO, DRDO, IES and private sector companies as well.

**Specializations**

The program offers three specializations:

1. **Data and Signal Analytics**
2. **Embedded Systems and IoT**
3. **VLSI Design**

**Career in IoT (Internet of Things)**

A student pursuing B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering can become an expert in IoT, by learning courses related to Embedded Systems and IoT and improving skills by doing projects in this area. The different courses in this area are:

1. **Embedded System Hardware**
2. **Embedded System Hardware-Software Co-Design**
3. **IoT Networks and Protocols**
4. **Embedded Systems Design and Programming**
5. **IoT Systems and Software**

Specializations Offered by the Programme:

The programme offers three specializations:

1. Data and Signal Analytics
2. Embedded Systems and IoT
3. VLSI Design

Future Focus for ECE Aspirants:

The electronics industry is expected to be a game-changer after the government's announcement in the Union Budget 2022-2023. With initiatives to make the electronics industry robust, electronics chip manufacturing has gained momentum. It is estimated that there will be around 1.3 Lacs jobs per year for ECE students in India by 2025 (Source: about ECE in Electronics for You). This is the right time to take admission in ECE, as All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) has issued a letter to various educational institutes/universities in India to start Chip design and manufacturing courses in Electronics.

B.A. (Hons.) Business Studies

The Business Studies major at Bennett University’s School of Liberal Arts offers a dynamic and interdisciplinary approach to understanding the complex realities of the Indian business world. Combining core business principles with insights from the humanities and social sciences, this program equips students with critical thinking, leadership, and problem-solving skills. With a strong emphasis on problem identification, ethics, innovation, and global perspectives, students gain hands-on experience through case studies, internships, industry interactions, and entrepreneurial initiatives. Graduates are turned future-ready, prepared for diverse career paths in management, entrepreneurship, and consulting, making them adaptable professionals ready to navigate the evolving business landscape with confidence and creativity.

The Business Studies program is designed to:

- Introduce foundational business concepts and interdisciplinary approaches in the first two semesters.
- Explore areas such as management, entrepreneurship, and global markets in the following semesters.
- Offer specialized courses in business ethics, digital transformation, and strategic leadership in the later years.

Curriculum Overview:

The curriculum is designed to offer a strong academic foundation through Major-Core courses, complemented by Major-Electives for added specialization.

Major-Core:
- Delivers essential and advanced coursework.
- Students required to complete a minimum of 45 credits, up to a maximum of 60 credits.

Major-Core Courses:
- Accounting Principles
- Corporate Finance

Teaching Methodology:

- Theory courses: 3-4 lectures a week, each lasting 55 minutes, depending on course credits.

**Course Offerings**

Accounting Principles

Corporate Finance

Statistics for Business

Operation and Information Management

Legal Studies and Business Ethics

Intro to Org Behavior

Design Thinking and Innovation

Intro to Business Research and Methods

Introduction to Financial Management

Major-Elective
- Credit options: 27-36 credits

Product and Brand Management

Digital Marketing

Merger and Acquisitions

Financial Service and Insurance

Entrepreneurial Strategy

International Marketing

International Supply Chain and Logistics

Cross Cultural Management

Venture Creation Strategy and Simulation

**Fee Structure**

Fee Structure 2025:

- Major: Psychology | Political Science & IR | Economics | English | Business Studies | Philosophy | Sociology
- Tuition fee:
  - Year 1: ₹4,70,000
  - Year 2: ₹4,70,000
  - Year 3: ₹4,70,000
  - TOTAL: ₹14,10,000
- Registration Fees: One Time (₹) 45,000
- Total Fees: ₹14,55,000

**Study Abroad Programs**

- International Immersion Program: Spend one semester to a full year studying at prestigious international institutions, gaining exposure to diverse academic frameworks and cultural environments. Current partner institutions include:
  - National Taiwan University
  - Augustana University
  - HSE, Russia
- Semester Abroad Program: Students have the option to spend one semester to a full year studying at prestigious international institutions.
- Summer School Programs: Provide specialized learning opportunities for shorter yet impactful global experiences:
  - University of Nebraska, Omaha: A 6-week program designed to offer a deep dive into liberal arts disciplines.
  - Babson College, USA: A 1–2-week Babson Build Entrepreneurship Program exclusively for UG2 and UG3 students, focusing on entrepreneurial skills and innovation.
  - University of British Columbia, Canada: A 4-week intensive program providing exposure to international teaching standards and diverse academic content.

University of British Columbia, Canada: A 4-week intensive program providing exposure to international teaching standards and diverse academic content.

Short-Term Study Abroad Programs
Students can also participate in short-term study programs, such as the 2-week immersive program at UCJC, Spain, which combines academic learning with cultural exploration.
